summary: Investigation on 503 spike for a live event
description:
*Issue Description*

The customer / IAT team reported a spike in 5xx errors occurring between 15:15 and 15:17 Dec. 19th UTC.

!503spike.jpeg|width=571,height=189!

*Impact*
 * End users received a significant number of 5xx responses during a short time window.
 * Despite fail action being configured, approximately 40,000 HTTP 503 responses were served.
 * Approximately 3,000 HTTP 504 responses were also recorded.

*Analysis / Findings*
 * The primary error observed was HTTP 503, delivered to end users.
 * Error spike duration was brief (~1 minute) but high in volume.
 * HTTP 504 errors were also present, though at a lower volume (~3K).
 * Tracing logs there are errors with ERR_READ_ERROR|fwdStateFree and it seems MPE amplifed the issue (1)
 * There are also SSL connect timeout errors between Akamai midgress regions (2)

(1) For errors related to ERR_READ_ERROR|fwdStateFree symptom is similar to [https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/DPEREQ-355] 

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10725166_tracelog] has end to end logs. Can you please verify if this is the same case?

(2) For SSL connect timeout, it shows strange request flow. Sample log shows child server went to mch and chapacf. They all went to the same CW server. And the CW server went to ch210 which is the same mch region 48062.

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10725166_tracelog2] has details for that. From Akatec analysis the region 48062 experienced a sudden overload of requests for www.tiktok.com, which led to a sharp increase in hits and flits. This transient spike appears to have caused resource pressure on machines in the region, following which multiple nodes entered self-suspension as a protective measure.

IAT wants to clairfy some questions on the issue (2).
 # When the suspended flags were set, did we still serve the traffic? If so can we please understand the reason for this?
 # Can we please understand why it took time for the underline mapper to shift the traffic to a better region? 
 # What are we doing to ensure that this issue do not re surface?

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10725166] has more information on the impact.
resolution_description: Downstream impact while significant, was very brief lasting under 40 seconds. Given the current RA feedback cycle times, traffic cannot be shifted sooner by mapper, but will need to rely on product/config workarounds to use alternate path.
root_cause: Overload of a Cloud Interconnect region due to attack on TikTok resulted in self-selfsuspension of multiple machines in the region. Lack of failover path at GCI resulted in 5xx downstream due to negative cache.
creator: Dimitris Kim
mitigation_plan: n/a
longterm_mitigation: Config optimizations to workaround issues at CI tier

h - - - mid=8002;cca=bbr;mtse=g|4294967295;aid=234605;pin=http|dsa;ssl=3001302:::1d;netp=i;aidv=8;tff=8007;mle=19206;vcd=3412 - - - 248:22025:0:0 NS:NS:s:NS:core_rsa2k_NS_client_50678: - mas=647:::::::21:8:;masc=::213:;cpu=//thd@phtm27/1643;f=0|1|DPF:1|DPF1:0|DPF_BP:D|DPF_ECMV:1|DPF_G2OP1:s|DPF_ORSESSL:s|DPF_QCP:1|DPF_REP:D;rct=n;h_ssl=TLSv1.3;csw=19875:1;swapsz=ok;fips=1;rawhs=1490 - - 1f89ac4a - - 22769 972 - - - - - -
 {code}
Error:  104 - ECONNRESET: Connection reset by peer

 

Good number of requests latched to forward requests and avoiding forward connections but there were too many fwd requests in one second. 

Checking the cpu and flit load of the machine 23.55.163.155, it looks normal during the issue time. 

We are trying to understand how the situation could be avoided for the customer for future instances if customer triggeres a similar scenario. 

About the specific behavior where huge number of requests hit the edge regions at same time. Customer said :
"Typically, one of the (short) teasers is activated at 18:55, shortly before the news. At that time, a large audience is expected to be tuned in. Viewers are invited to press the green button on their remote to switch to ZDF’s internet platform and watch a dedicated movie or show. I do not know how many TVs are tuned in at that time, which makes it difficult for me to estimate the number of more or less simultaneous requests."

We are planning to suggest a few things to prevent similar situation in the future.  
 # Pre-warm cache with expected cacheable objects so that the number of forward requests can be reduced. 
 # Introduce another layer of cache to [TD1] catch requests before going to origin. 

In case of MCH, our understanding is that url hash is created to route traffic to specific MCH machine. Is there no default retry mechanism to another MCH machine/region before going to origin? [Configuration here has normal TD enabled and no Siteshield/OIPACL]

Could you confirm the Connection resets here is because of large number of new connections hitting the same server 23.55.163.155 ? 

Is there any other recommendations to handle the client request behavior in this case? 

Log analysis [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10707321_16] 

Customer had similar situation on Dec 12th [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10707321] 

Cache.log lcoation: 


lsg 13

mmalik

-rw-r--r-- 1 mmalik install 1009K Dec 22 10:42 cache-GIP2.19.98.142-202512161753-23.33.25.197-509-1766399738.gz

-rw-r--r-- 1 mmalik install  1.4M Dec 22 11:21 cache-GIP23.55.162.155-202512161730-23.33.25.197-808-1766401735.gz
root_cause:
1: Suboptimal latching of the requests so the fwd requests to MCH are high in number from the edge for flash crowds.
2: Lack of fail-action during failures from the above.
creator: Sandeep Bhat
mitigation_plan: NA
longterm_mitigation: MPE with FailAction

summary: F-CS-10721058 - ChinaCDN Users Mostly Getting Mapped Outside of China
description:
Customer: AIG Global Operations, Inc.
Configuration: www.aig.com.cn v24
Hostname: www.aig.com.cn

 

Resolving Hostname from an edge server in China frequently returns an edge IP located in US. From the CNAME, we can see that we are resolving to the correct ChinaCDN map (e2832.dscca.s.tl88.net). 
{noformat}
 122.224.10.4 /usr/local/akamai> dig www.aig.com.cn

; <<>> DiG 9.18.39-0ubuntu0.22.04.1aka22.0.6-Ubuntu <<>> www.aig.com.cn
;; global options: +cmd
;; Got answer:
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: NOERROR, id: 6814
;; flags: qr rd ra; QUERY: 1, ANSWER: 4, AUTHORITY: 0, ADDITIONAL: 1

;; OPT PSEUDOSECTION:
; EDNS: version: 0, flags:; udp: 512
;; QUESTION SECTION:
;www.aig.com.cn.			IN	A

;; ANSWER SECTION:
www.aig.com.cn.		1	IN	CNAME	www.aig.com.cn.edgekey.net.
www.aig.com.cn.edgekey.net. 1	IN	CNAME	www.aig.com.cn.edgekey.net.globalredir.akadns.net.
www.aig.com.cn.edgekey.net.globalredir.akadns.net. 1 IN	CNAME e2832.dscca.s.tl88.net.
e2832.dscca.s.tl88.net.	1	IN	A	23.36.136.81 {noformat}
 

Checking mapping for this IP, we can see that sometimes it is mapped to an edge server in China, but more often it is not.
[https://mapper.akamai.com/ip/#from%3D1766421600632%26to%3D1766508000646%26treeType%3DA%26mrule%3D4858%26viperSlot%3D2832%26cidr%3D122.224.10.4%26resolver%3Dip%26useGMTTime%3Dtrue%26timeRange%3D%7B%22min%22%3A1766421600632%2C%22max%22%3A1766508000646%7D%26]

 

This seems like it's possibly a ChinaCDN capacity issue. Their map currently has 14 regions in China, and 2 are suspended. Do we have any options to increase the percentage of requests that are routed in country in CN?
resolution_description: product expectations
root_cause: product EOL - capacity is no longer expanding , there are some increases in flit heavy traffic at  ca2 level which further causes ca traffic to be more ooc.
creator: Ted Frost

cache-GIP92.123.9.6-202512231555-23.33.25.197-590-1766524075.gz -  tdeary@lsg-gss13

The Customer reported an influx of VPFs globally, initially observed in the SA-east-1 but also other parts of LATAM and NA between 15:55 - 16:10 UTC. 

Checking tools internally we saw there was a spike in *ERR_SSL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT|SSLTimeout* errors on cache-h forwards:
!Screenshot 2025-12-23 at 12.23.00 PM.jpg|width=655,height=479!

Tracing some of the samples shows that fail action was successful in the vast majority of cases however, the customer is still reporting VPFs and playback failures from their end.

      9 [r53061:SDN-GRU31i]
      9 [r45136:SDN-BUEk]
      8 [r53060:SDN-GRU31j]  {code}
Checking the load graph for these regions we can see that they all had a sudden spike in flits on API MR 15220 which caused ghost rolls and suspend flags for the majority of these regions above. 

[Region 52067|[https://region360-aps.akamai.com/?inputValues=52067&startDateTime=2025-12-23%2015:30:00&endDateTime=2025-12-23%2016:30:00&startDateTimeForCacheLogs=2025-12-23%2015:30:00&endDateTimeForCacheLogs=2025-12-23%2016:30:00]]

High load reported on Serial 2047 for region 52067
[https://mapper.akamai.com/dnsp/#from%3D1766442540000%26to%3D1766528940000%26dataFrom%3D1766505705000%26useGMTTime%3Dtrue%26timeRange%3D%7B%22min%22%3A1766502687310.4019%2C%22max%22%3A1766508477188.7712%7D%26regionId%3D52067%26service%3DW%26]

!Screenshot 2025-12-23 at 4.24.46 PM.jpg!
*Load graphs for some of the impacted SA and US regions:*
[r52067:FABRIC-GRU12q|https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=start1766501520end1766510580&region=52067&network=A&maprule=&display=maprule&ymax=&utcoffset=-5]
[r45135:SDN-BUEj|https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=start1766501520end1766510580&region=45135&network=A&maprule=&display=maprule&ymax=&utcoffset=-5]
[r47296:SDN-GRU2w|https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=start1766501520end1766510580&region=47296&network=A&maprule=&display=maprule&ymax=&utcoffset=-5]
[r47182:FABRIC-GRU1e|https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=start1766501520end1766510580&region=47182&network=A&maprule=&display=maprule&ymax=&utcoffset=-5]
[r40970:FABRIC-MIAy|https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=start1766501520end1766510580&region=40970&network=A&maprule=&display=maprule&ymax=&utcoffset=-5]
[r44485:SDN-SCLk|https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=start1766501520end1766510580&region=44485&network=A&maprule=&display=maprule&ymax=&utcoffset=-5]

 

Ask for ERE: 
Seeking further details WRT what caused the spike in flits on API MR 15220 and thus impact to the customer.
resolution_description: improvement to timeouts for customer can be addressed , but also under SI-00023383, looking into improvements including CRP to help against this.
root_cause: series of attacks - both to tiktok and vavle over holiday weeks has caused performance issues at large. Hitting this customer D+
creator: Tyler Deary
mitigation_plan: improve configs resiliency
longterm_mitigation: SI-00023383 - details there on long term items

summary: Increase timeout request for network:http2.connection-write-timeout
description:
There was an ERE Jira opened(ERE-2776) for the same issue and that was closed as customer mentioned they were not going to pursue increasing connection-write-timeout. Recently customer reported CONNECTION_CLOSED error is still occurring and there is an increase in VOC from end users.

IAT opened ETAM Jira(ETAM-526) to get an apporval for the tag and they requested to open an ERE Jira ticket.

*Steps to Reproduce*

1. Play the URL '[https://vid-transcoded-vp.daumcdn.net/43a997718d60651e76f80656418056961763714678.mp4]' in the browser with developer tools.
2. If you play this video, some seconds later, console shows 'GET https://vid-transcoded-vp.daumcdn.net/43a997718d60651e76f80656418056961763714678.mp4 net::ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED 206 (Partial Content)'.
3. If it doesn't happen at the first play, please try to play again. Then it will be reproduced.

Sample log shows H2 connection timeout after 10s.
{code:java}
[jseo@lsg-gss7 ~/kakao]$ zcat CP1264104-202512040630-23.33.25.197-436-1764895689.gz | grep "ac69e792"

*Impact* 

Chrome browser just shows the error in the browser console but customer is using their own app and when the issue happens they got an error and connection closed.

Please review this and let us know if there needs any other information.

summary: Slow performance for ICP requests
resolution_description:
Read-timeouts primarily on ICP requests is likely due to a ghost-bug, where the sibling seems healthy but it isn't clear why it is marked as Dead and Revived in the very same millisecond. 
https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOST-6267
root_cause: Increased timeouts seen only on ICP requests, likely due to a ghost bug.
creator: Dimitris Kim
mitigation_plan: N/A
longterm_mitigation: N/A

summary: High count of ERR_SSL_HANDSHAKE leading to 503s for Sony Interactive
description:
Account Name : Sony Interactive Entertainment LLC - Platform
CPCODES: 1307226, 945856

Summary : On two separate time-stamps, Sony Interactive Entertainment CPCODE : 1307226 encountered spikes in HTTP 503 responses. Upon analyzing the error instances, it was noticed that this being 2-Tree config, we noticed high counts of ERR_SSL_HANDSHAKE errors from the CPCODE : 945856 (Leg 2) of the configuration.

Analysis of 25th Dec : Etherpad : [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10745206]
Analysis of 26th Dec : Etherpad : [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10747623]



Timeline of ERR_SSL_HANDSHAKE for Dec 25th

 
{noformat}
[psalunke@lsg-gss13 /home/vvenkate]$ zcat CP945856-202512252010-23.33.25.197-926-1766696332.gz | awk ' $2=="f" && $30~/ERR_FWD_SSL_HANDSHAKE/ {print $3}' | sort | time_convert --format="%H:%M" | uniq -c 
      1 20:12
2660920 20:17
1330471 20:18{noformat}
 

 
{noformat}

1997637 23.61.11.152
1993754 23.61.11.143
      1 127.0.0.1 {noformat}
 

 
{noformat}

IP 23.48.249.152 (HERO=23.61.11.152) (IG 8) is in region 38243 (FABRIC-IAD21g) ESSL (DNS:RX)
IP 23.48.249.143 (HERO=23.61.11.143) (IG 7) is in region 38243 (FABRIC-IAD21g) ESSL (DNS:RX)

RHM : https://perf.akamai.com/cgi-bin/mahollan/rhm.cgi?region_input=38243&start_date=12%2F25%2F25+18%3A30&stop_date=12%2F25%2F25+21%3A30 

Loadgraph : https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=start1766686560end1766697060&region=38243&network=A&maprule=&display=total&ymax=&utcoffset=5 
{noformat}

We could see this anamoly of high count of 5xx from 2 regions in particular : r38243 and r34840



We raised this with NOCC on 2 different cases as mentioned above under:  B-V-67DVXYR and B-V-67A0517 and for both the instances NOCC confirmed that these 2 spikes have the same root cause and below is the response / analysis provided by them

The region 38243's ips in log analysis seemed to have been under load pressure from Sony's PS traffic [1] for about 2 mins approx.Spike was seen on serial 40436 and major cpcode seems to 945856 [2] (you can see how the traffic pattern was 2 mins ago and at the time of the issue) .As per vice, there seems to have been an event for Sony and ACC too seems to show an uptick in this cpcode's traffic . 



Vice: [https://vice.akamai.com/#!/vice/calendar]

ACC: [https://control.akamai.com/apps/reports/#/predefined/traffic-by-hostname-2?cpcodes=945856&dateRange=last7Days&timezone=Greenwich]

Mapper's DNSP state : [https://mapper.akamai.com/dnsp/#from%3D1766729073298%26to%3D1766815473298%26dataFrom%3D1766772841000%26useGMTTime%3Dtrue%26timeRange%3D%7B%22min%22%3A1766772314500.899%2C%22max%22%3A1766773239187.7441%7D%26regionId%3D38243%26service%3DS%26]

Mapper's Demand estimate : seems to be coming in from EU , which is odd . May be due to high demand 
[https://mapper.akamai.com/ip/#mrule%3D1039%26mpg%3D1634%26resolver%3Dmpg%26treeType%3DA%26from%3D1766772314500%26to%3D1766773239187%26useGMTTime%3Dtrue%26]

 
{noformat}

resolution_description:
Requested for Pre-spreading at SS layer.
FOM is the immediate fix that can be done in case of seeing continuous issues.
root_cause: Concentration of traffic and aggressive retries causes overloads to the region at SS layer.
creator: Pratik Salunke
mitigation_plan:
Requested for Pre-spreading at SS layer.
FOM is the immediate fix that can be done in case of seeing continuous issues.
longterm_mitigation: Customer config to fix the retry logic by reducing the attempts.

summary: China mapping to HK poor performance
description:
Hello GPO,

Problem report from customer:

~~~

Customer reported slowness in response for traffic going via Akamai. Requests sent directly to the origin server responded quickly.

Hostname - [www.dior.cn|https://www.dior.cn/]

Few reported paths -

/zh_cn/fashion/store/rest/applet_cdc/V1/mp/applet-menu/config.
/zh_cn/fashion/store/rest/V2/mp/catalog/categoryTreeV2
/zh_cn/fashion/store/campaign/OpenSearch/reports

Customer stated that latency is noticed specifically by China Mobile ISP users.

Issue Start Time - Dec 18, 2025
Issue End Time - Ongoing

When we tried checking mapping aspect, we checked the HATS tool and we could see a shift in traffic from China regions to HK regions after Dec 18. This timeline aligns with what customer reported as well.

CN regions which were serving traffic earlier can be seen still but a higher concentration of requests seems to be going to HK compared to before Dec 18 and we suspect this is causing increased latency.

HATS: [https://www.nocc.akamai.com/miniurl/?id=292b2]

[https://mapper.akamai.com/ip/#from%3D1766047300010%26to%3D1767170535705%26treeType%3DA%26mrule%3D489%26viperSlot%3D40087%26cidr%3D218.203.69.238%26resolver%3Dip%26useGMTTime%3Dtrue%26]

Considering there is a significant shift in traffic and it is impacting customer traffic, can we tweak the mapping to prefer some China regions? The top DNS going to HK regions seem to be mostly from China (see attached).

Logs: https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10747078

~~~

Thanks
executive_summary: China mobile users mapping to HK routinely
root_cause: Increase flit consumption from Samsung CPCode -7960 since mid December is causing the china regions to run full and forcing other customer traffic OOC impacting perf.
creator: Rahul Sawarkar
mitigation_plan: Working with Samsung IAT to work on problematic config to alleviate the current situation.
longterm_mitigation: Customer to follow guidelines as china-cdn is EOL.

summary: Brotli compressed incomplete object on forward side
description:
Issue : Akamai received an HTTP 200 response from the origin but timed out after approximately 122 seconds with the error message {{{}ERR_READ_TIMEOUT | before_resp_body{}}}. Instead of logging an origin 0xx error after the timeout, Akamai retained the 200 response and cached an incomplete object.

*Timeline and content length served for successful (HTTP 200) responses without errors:*

We reviewed the logs for the object main.f85d3288.js over a ~20-hour window, starting from Dec 31, 09:00 GMT through Jan 1, 05:00 GMT, to analyze the content bytes received from Origin

During Dec 31, the content bytes received for this object were consistently 2,080,313 bytes, as observed across multiple successful origin fetches. Below are a few representative timestamps from that period:

awk '$2=="f" && $19~/o/ && $15=="200" && $30=="-" \{print $9, $3}' | time_convert

23.200.145.6 r 1767173091.206 0 1 66 121954 108361 108829 109500 23.35.216.25 GET /L/165350/1686776/0s/cwl.hkjc.com/static/js/main.f85d3288.js 200 - gZPeJW application/javascript cwl.hkjc.com - [https://cwl.hkjc.com/priority-registration?lang=zh-HK&source=AOSBS&fontSize=M&gdpr=1&isPCardMember=false&adobe_mc=TS%253D1767173090%257CMCMID%253D03730665417238940285361493179990520975%257CMCORGID%253D06AB2C1653DB07AD0A490D4B%2540AdobeOrg] Mozilla/5.0%20(Linux;%20Android%2012;%20SM-A5260%20Build/SP1A.210812.016;%20wv)%20AppleWebKit/537.36%20(KHTML,%20like%20Gecko)%20Version/4.0%20Chrome/143.0.7499.34%20Mobile%20Safari/537.36 - TLSv1.3 3 - 1649 - - 265a69b3 0 0 2e1eab4d.85765f3f 165350.s2695 - 2080313 - - - - - - 4408 2262 20585 9fe7ad176e1d0a61 - - - o 0 r7f548e978eb0.268657194.p.g 10092 s8 - 5fde0880 - - - - 182.239.119.177 1163 - - - %20Cg%20_{_}slot194554{_}__{_}networkESSL{_}__{_}levelL3{_}__{_}certCUS{_}_ - - - - - cca=bbr;br=ba;mtse=g|268657194;aid=1089583;pin=http|ion;ssl=3001302:::1d;netp=i;sip=1;aidv=1;cpu=//ctx@waf/113/593\//prod@bm/2\//prod@bmi/25\//prod@sec/80;tff=8007;mle=42133;fsd=1;vcd=6744 - - - 29658:286488:7387:0 L3:L3:s::core_rsa2k_L3_client_47848: - mas=738::::118:56:114:78:59:;masc=::68:;cpu=//thd@phtm0/6633;f=0|1|DPF:4|DPF1:0|DPF_G2OP1:i|DPF_ORSESSL:i|DPF_QCP:1|DPF_REP:I|DPF_RM:1|DPF_ROBR:A|DPF_RUA:1;rct=n;rbuf=b;brl=0|0|0;csw=2753:3;swapsz=ok;fips=1;rawhs=1649 - - 1e2341cb - - 83743 891 - - - 23.200.145.6|1|1|1|165350|0|848|20|0 ua=%22Android%20WebView%22%3bv%3d%22143%22,%20%22Chromium%22%3bv%3d%22143%22,%20%22Not%20A(Brand%22%3bv%3d%2224%22;ua-mobile=%3f1;ua-platform=%22Android%22 -
{quote}
Sample request at *09:24 UTC* encountered {{{}ERR_READ_TIMEOUT | before_resp_body{}}}.

The origin returned {*}HTTP 200{*}, but only *425,687 bytes* were sent, compared to the expected {*}2,080,313 bytes{*}, which was consistent across multiple previous requests.

*cURL response headers* were not captured during the incident. To check for EOF, cache logs were reviewed; however, these logs have now been rolled out.

Client-facing (r-line) and origin-facing (f-line) logs confirm this is a single correlated request.

 

*Network timeout configuration:*

{{network:http.read120s}} applies {*}after origin connection and headers are received{*}.

Akamai successfully received headers, but body stalled for ~120 seconds, triggering {{{}ERR_READ_TIMEOUT{}}}.

Headers were already committed → HTTP status remained 200 (expected protocol behavior).

References:
[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10764239]
[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10764239_6Jan]

_Based on discussion with customers about their origin Microsoft IIS server log had 995 win32 errors at the time which lead to the smaller than expected content size._

_However from their IIS log they said this request lasted only 6 seconds from the IIS server's perspective._

_From Akamai origin f line we saw 122 seconds_

 

_*Similarities to Prior Incidents: [https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/ERE-2262*_]
_Reference: *F-CS-8650754 – ERR_READ_TIMEOUT before_resp_body*_
 * _Origin returned 200 OK_

 * _Origin read timed out → {{ERR_READ_TIMEOUT | before_resp_body}} in f-line_

 * _f-line object status {{L}} (object deletion due to content-length mismatch)_

_This incident aligns closely with_ _*ERE-2262*_ _behavior, *except this time the object was ~425 KB (not 0 bytes).*_
executive_summary: Akamai received an HTTP 200 response from the origin but timed out after approximately 122 seconds with the error message ERR_READ_TIMEOUT | before_resp_body. Instead of logging an origin 0xx error after the timeout, Akamai retained the 200 response and cached an incomplete object.
creator: Rashida Pathan

summary: Region overloaded issues during Super Bowl for NBC
description:
The we observed an issue during a Peacock Exclusive event on Dec. 27th @ 21:23 ET and 21:59 ET. We raised a NOCC Ticket for the second instance where they had confirmed 51473 was over loaded during the timeframe due to high volume of requests for hostnames tiktokcdn.com & fts.rbxcdn.com. 
        *•*        During this timeframe, a few client aborts were observed, with slowness occurring across multiple mid-tier clusters.
        *•*        The issue was traced to CW regions and confirmed not to be related to the origin.
        *•*        Several SSL timeouts (ERR_SSL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT) originated from a single parent region, resulting in 200 ERR_SSL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT delivery errors.

51473
+Drop In Flits+

+*Ask to ERE*+
This overloading issue happened on the same Config that will be used during the Super Bowl for NBC. 
- The IAT has requested that we open a Jira with ERE seeking recommendations on how to avoid Similar load related spikes on neighboring Customer Maps causing issues for NBC during the Super Bowl.
root_cause: Ask for review of config in lieu of superbowl prep to overcome noisy neighbor issue .The analysis done by BOCC showed Tiktok (2 of which already spread) and Roblox domains to be highest hit contributors . Due to lack of available logs flit analysis couldn't be done to take appropriate action if needed.

summary: F-CS-10780325 - High Metadata Application Time Seen From One Region
description:
Account: Citigroup Global Markets Inc
Hostname: tts.apib2b.citi.com
Configuration: tts.apib2b.citi.com_pm
CP Code: 1834080

 

Client times out after 500ms, and started seeing timeouts around 06:00 GMT on 2026-01-06. The issue stopped when region 40045 was suspended at 19:22 GMT on 2026-01-06.

 

Logs show this happens primarily from two edge servers: 
{noformat}
 [efrost@lsg-gss7 /home/sanaik/brod-c]$ zcat CP1834080-202601061400-23.33.25.197-125-1767715675.gz | cc_log_convert | awk '$2=="r" && !($16~/[fGKPX]/ || $53~/m/) && !($11~/^(10\.|127\.0\.0\.1)/) && $5>500 && $13~"tts/api/v1/oauth2/token" && $48~"secure5ssl.arcot.com" {print $1}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | head -10
    349 23.223.35.156
    168 23.223.35.172
      1 23.223.35.183  
{noformat}
  

 

Checking examples from 23.223.35.156, the issue is very intermittent. Looking at one specific time period, it does seem to correlate with spiky traffic:

summary: {F-CS-10779687} SR routing the traffic towards the faulty regions
description:
Issue: Midgress showing a high Turnaround time while contacting the SS parent. Impacted regions (r47448 r47393)

Account: Shein Group Limited
Hostname: api-service.shein.com	
Configuration: api-service.shein.com v381
SR: 29831.Shein-Group-Limited-e13545.akasrg.akamai.com
SS: 11178	e;s1678.akamaiedge.net

Description:
The issue started around 10:00 am UTC on 6th Jan 2026. From the logs, we could see high Turnaround time between child and parent. The issue was twowards region (r47448 r47393), the region was suspended around 18:00 manualy which fixed the issue. 

From the logs, we could see the races were run towards two regions despite having other regions. 
Available SS parent regions in US: https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=1w&region=39725,41734,42173,42402,47393,47448,48556,51358&network=A&maprule=11178&display=region&ymax=

summary: Large number of machine for 22.4.0 ESSL phase one timed out on mdt-agent update
description:
During phase 1 checklisting, we saw large number of ESSL machine reported suspendflag 1024.  It happened on FF network too. (report 2048 instead). Investigation shows:
 * The issue was *not* seen when Ghost was suspended but mdtagent *was* running. The issue *was* seen when Ghost was serving traffic but mdtagent was {*}stopped{*}. The mdtagent is likely not involved in this errant behaviour.
 * Israth is trying to setup a QA machine to reproduce the issue, she is installing machines now and analyzing the traffic seen on the machine exhibiting the increased mdt processing times.  She has seen that there is TikTok POC traffic with client aborts, which may lead to requests still downloading data after the client closed – are the downloads/requests not being freed?
 * Further discussion: is this X8-specific issue: not clear as most machines on both FF and ESSL are X8s (FF = 78K/127K total, ESSL = 50K/78K total). John reported that a couple of X7s are also reporting this behaviour. Instead of hardware-related, perhaps resource-related (running out of resources due to the client aborts??) Still to be definitively determined.
 * MC for baseline has been filed
creator: John Pan

*Investigation:*

*Observations:*

#1 Based on NOCC observations under [B-V-691DXYZ|https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi?issue_id=B-V-691DXYZ] DNSP Leader got Memory, CPU bound due to overload, with suspect culprit to be: HTTP2 traffic of rukminim2.flixcart.com MR: 1327.

 

#2 Further observation wouldn't be that straight forward as there was a traffic pattern change (Offload drop with increase) as well for ESSL (DD - Download Delivery) of JIO since 7th continuing, CP:  586523.

Property:

Feature-Phone
Digital property: fota.slv.kai.jiophone.net
Group: FOTA SLV
Account: JIO PLATFORMS LIMITED (F-AC-2341982)

 

Map:

2978        e;e28.akamaiedge.net        SNI-only map for EIPB (Anycast) ESSL solution

!image-2026-01-09-15-32-17-884.png|width=280,height=89!

 

#3 Reviewing eight Culver SiteShield (mr: 11857) regions available in India, all are having increased Flits majorly at CPU since 7th.

[https://bessel.akamai.com/queries/maprule.regions.php?maprule=11857&slot=&country=India&continent=]®match=&gbsize=&tier=0

 

[https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=1w]®ion=12005%2C26813%2C28265%2C34474%2C35145%2C35329%2C20691%2C30531&network=A&maprule=1327%2C2978&display=region&ymax=&utcoffset=1&diskcpu=on

!image-2026-01-09-15-36-54-319.png|width=561,height=512!

 

*Ask:*
 * Confirm SiteShield flit capacity stability status in India for Culver.
 * Why mapper hasn't remapped is there any DNSP/gmon/ghost/other issue or just capacity constraint?
 ** [https://mapper.akamai.com/ip/#from%3D1767874202092%26to%3D1767960602092%26treeType%3DA%26mrule%3D11857%26cidr%3D23.217.110.101%26resolver%3Dip%26useGMTTime%3Dtrue%26]
 * should we take any preventative actions like FOM reduce potentially leading towards undesired side effects or treat it as one-off for now?
 * should we reach out the Flipkart Internet Pvt Ltd (AANA-6WET7X) IAT to review the CPU traffic pattern or is it just side effect of JIO Bits starting to shift the traffic around?
root_cause: DNSP Leader in SiteShield region r35329 (TATA-DELp, India) became CPU and memory bound due to overload from two concurrent traffic pattern changes: (1) demand spike from Flipkart rukminim2.flixcart.com (HTTP/2, maprule 1327) on India Culver SiteShield map (mr:11857); (2) Jio FOTA ESSL traffic offload shift (CP 586523) since Jan 7. All 8 India Culver SiteShield regions showed increased CPU flits causing ERR_FWD_SSL_HANDSHAKE 503 failures for SonyLIV.
creator: Maciej Maciaszek
mitigation_plan: FOM (Fault Override Map) adjustment applied to TATA-DEL region (r35329) while root cause investigation was ongoing. SS-MapOps engaged to review SiteShield map (mr:11857) for capacity. Flipkart IAT engaged to review increased CPU traffic pattern from rukminim2.flixcart.com. Customer (SonyLIV) asked to acknowledge the SiteShield changes.

summary: F-CS-10787620 - Mobile Content Cached Under Desktop CID
description:
Account: IE ONLINE MEDIA SERVICES PRIVATE LIMITED
Hostname: indianexpress.com
Configuration: indianexpress.com v501

 

The configuration has logic to extract the is_mobile attribute from the EDC output. This is_mobile value is used to modify the cache key and also set the X-Mobile-Class response header to smart if the is_mobile attribute is true. We are seeing examples where the X-Mobile-Class response header is set to smart, but this is not reflected in the cache key.

 

This is hard to see in logs, but tracing an example where the customer saw mobile for desktop we found the following in cache manager. This is an example from the child server. The CM logs for parent had already been overwritten:

summary: fail-action2 forward request ignores lma.origin-edge off flag
description:
During a live event in December, this account experienced an issue on the origin server and conducted an investigation. In the process, they observed a strange thing that multiple Akamai edge servers were sending requests to the origin with the Accept-Encoding: gzip header.

However, the configuration (AMD-livecloud.akamaized.net) indicates that “Supports gzip compression” is set to “No.” The customer has requested clarification on how these requests could occur despite this setting.


Checking this further on an internal configuration Akatec noticed that the issue happnes when fail action triggered. 
 
- Account: Akamai Korea
- PM: www.sukimm.com.edgesuite.net v379 (Staging)
- Test URL: curl -v -k  http://www.sukimm.com.edgesuite.net/test.html -H "Accept-Encoding: gzip" --connect-to ::a1.g.akamai-staging.net
- Content size: 23553 (unzip), 127 (gzip)

This request will trigger fail over and Edge request & Origin response headers show Accept-Encoding: gzip.
{code:java}

There are several KB articles like [https://akamai.my.salesforce.com/kA30f000000g5JQ]

creator: Dimitris Kim

summary: RET+XFER Increase after enabling APISEC on ajdacent domain
description:
Hello GPO

Please refer to attached screen shot about traffic peak.

It seems that RET and XFER time are causing  high flit purely because of the change made by applying API security.

Customer ‘Coupang Co.,Ltd.’ reported that the request end time and transfer time of [cmapi.coupang.com|https://cmapi.coupang.com/] (CPcode 1589472) have increased since Jan 7, 20:00 UTC.
At 20:00 UTC Jan 7, API security was deployed from API security and security configuration API SC v310. 

We are monitoring [cmapi.coupang.com|https://cmapi.coupang.com/] using traffic peak, it shows request end time and transfer time have increased. (Traffic peak’s time zone is KST.) Mapping is local within KR.

CSE checked CPcode 1589472’s flit dashboard, and it showed flit, F2B, F2H have increased.
[https://grafana-dmz.akam.ai/org477/dashboard/db/country-high-flit-usage-cpcodes?from=1767393243395&to=1767915330278&var-country=KR&var-network=ff&var-account=Coupang%20Co.,Ltd.&var-cpcode=1589472&var-threshold=5]

The other domain [mercury.coupang.com|https://mercury.coupang.com/] (CPcode 1723882) with security configuration Oncall SC 173v for this domain had API Security enabled at the similar time stamp,
but API security configuration was not  applied to this domain. CPcode 1723882’s flit dashboard, it also showed flit, F2B, F2H have been increased after API security enabled.

[https://grafana-dmz.akam.ai/org477/dashboard/db/country-high-flit-usage-cpcodes?from=1767393243395&to=1767915330278&var-country=KR&var-network=ff&var-account=Coupang%20Co.,Ltd.&var-cpcode=1723882&var-threshold=5]

Checking load graph, flit has been increased much after API security enabled.
[https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=3d&region=19101%2C22141%2C24437%2C26727%2C31010%2C32355%2C33693%2C38402%2C42926%2C46031%2C47270%2C48883%2C48921%2C48922%2C49630%2C50064%2C50458%2C51456&network=W&maprule=15211%2C15212%2C15213%2C15214%2C15215%2C15216%2C15217%2C15218%2C15219%2C15220&display=region&ymax=&utcoffset=9&utc=on]

([mercury.coupang.com|https://mercury.coupang.com/] is auto sharded, so CSE checked load graph with maprule 15211~15220. [cmapi.coupang.com|https://cmapi.coupang.com/] is using 15211 map only.)

Logs before and after API security enabled, and it shows avg flit has increased.
Request end time and transfer time also have been increased. 

Logs: etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10789847

Could you please confirm that
1. The increase in flit is caused just by enabling API security?
2. If so, whether this behavior is expected or if we can tweak anything about load distribution to bring it back to normal. The bump in latency is only 10-20msec however customer noticed it in trafficpeak and wants the same old performance.
executive_summary: RET and XFER time are causing high flit purely because of the change made by applying API security.
root_cause: Increase in response time due to security feature's (flit increase) being added to the customer config. KR regions are running flit bound and this could degraded the current state, also the inspection rules could add the delay. There were no logs provided to ascertain the same.
creator: Rahul Sawarkar
mitigation_plan: NA
longterm_mitigation: Customer to use all api shards to spread load better .

summary: Jio WPL 10th Jan perf degradation
description:
*Background:*

Customer reported performance degradation during WPL match on 10th Jan.

Date time: 14:03-14:07 GMT 10th Jan

 

*NOCC ticket: [B-V-69AY0WZ|https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi?issue_id=B-V-69AY0WZ]*

*Etherpads:*

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10795154]

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10795154-1]

 

*Investigation:*

2 machines from r40697 got flit overloaded by Jio traffic `live12p-pristine-akt.cdn.hotstar.com`

!618ebd67-3712-4a26-9c39-8f922f6af71b.PNG|width=400,height=212!

 

for the two machines, format:

datetime, cpcode, flytes, hits

2026-01-10_14:03 1498654 132103244  49912
2026-01-10_14:03 1761130 61116470  40560
2026-01-10_14:03 1778729 157221999  36677
2026-01-10_14:03 1644840 37453901  33876
2026-01-10_14:03 1217945 34355588  21611
2026-01-10_14:03 1811853 24328934  16102
2026-01-10_14:03 1681589 16847989  15422
2026-01-10_14:03 1692348 21223356  10126
2026-01-10_14:03 1217948 11651532  9233

*2026-01-10_14:04 1644840 658421240  330866*
2026-01-10_14:04 1498654 146076513  45638
2026-01-10_14:04 1761130 78375961  41457
2026-01-10_14:04 1778729 170946049  31662
2026-01-10_14:04 1811853 33553785  17545
2026-01-10_14:04 1217945 32675861  15933
2026-01-10_14:04 1681589 15380208  9893

 

the overloading traffic originated from two AWS clients:
{code:java}
[mmaciasz@lsg-gss12 ~]$ zstdgrep 1644840 GIP23.209.103.229-202601101403-23.33.25.197-337-1768309859.gz | log_filter -a a 'rt,host_header,serial_and_map,c_ip' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | head
  50245 S live12p-pristine-akt.cdn.hotstar.com 1707.dscapi2 13.235.97.68
  20395 S live12p-pristine-akt.cdn.hotstar.com 1707.dscapi2 3.110.248.59
   6180 S live12p-pristine-akt.cdn.hotstar.com 1707.dscapi2 15.206.111.246
    502 r live12p-pristine-akt.cdn.hotstar.com 1707.dscapi2 13.235.97.68{code}
 

*Ask:*

Please review the feasibility, approve the use of pre-spread serial for this hosts or confirm other solutions to prevent single machines overloading.

I would opt for putting GTM in front as they have custom targets ready pointing to respective API shards, yet currently being used following way:
 * all ENG traffic shardX
 * all Hindi traffic shardY
executive_summary:
22nd Jan 2026
- For few matches of WPL, where we saw the region overload issue, customer has confirmed that there was a issue/bug at their end.  Ads, segments are not expected, only child manifest calls are expected.

Next Action:
Have a discussion pending with IAT.
Review pre-spread, as the pristine rps is expected to increase due to multiple concurrent events.
root_cause: Customer (MediaTailor/AWS) had a client-side bug causing ad segments and video segments to be routed to the pristine live streaming workflow. Only child manifest calls were expected. The unexpected high-bandwidth segment traffic caused flit overload on 2 machines in r40697 during the Jio WPL match on January 10, 2026.
creator: Maciej Maciaszek
mitigation_plan: IAT consulted. Customer confirmed and fixed their client-side bug (MediaTailor routing segments to pristine workflow). Recommendations provided: review feasibility of pre-spread serial for the pristine hostname, and consider GTM-based load distribution across API shards to prevent future traffic concentration on specific machines.

summary: {F-CS-10599375} Linkedin: modify ACAO rule not working after XML opt is enabled
description:
Product: Object Delivery
Config (staging v177) : [https://control.akamai.com/apps/property-manager/#/property-version/8030742/175/edit?gid=37333]

Issue:

 In v175 and v177, we have a similar rule which matches the client-request metadata stage and changes the outgoing response header ACAO to '*'

With version 177 the ACAO rule stopped working after XML optimization was enabled

 
Steps to Reproduce:
 
 - With cracker go to  URL [https://static.licdn.com/aero-v1/sc/h/8siq6sr06g1mc6ovp753jnckh]
 - Sent the request to Staging to get v177
 - you see ACAO value is "Access-Control-Allow-Origin: [https://www.linkedin.com|https://www.linkedin.com/] "   
 
 
I have collected logs, including ouiji cache.logs from Staging v177:
[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10599375]
 
line #1025
 
 
Akatec was able to replicate the same behavior in a test configuration after enabling XML Optimization in PM for the config.  Hostname [www.adivai.xyz|https://www.adivai.xyz/]

          Test URL   [https://www.adivai.xyz/test/headers]

 

 

Investigation of the issue was done already by ERE and it was requested to create a new Jira for ERE-Delivery-Portal. 

 

All the details about the issue and ERE investigation are here https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/ERE-3065
executive_summary:
- modify ACAO rule not working after XML opt is enabled
https://docs.google.com/document/d/14jxj-8sHTNz3wSn2eVQMc6mWHUcT_tbg/edit#heading=h.nbhahd8xpd10
resolution_description: Updated the configuration in v178 by removing the match:metadata-stage = client-request condition, allowing the header modification to occur at the correct stage. This restored the expected ACAO header behavior.
root_cause: The issue occurred due to incorrect configuration where response headers (ACAO) were being modified at the client-request metadata stage, where response headers are not available. This behavior previously appeared to work due to legacy template behavior, but enabling XML Optimization enforced stricter stage execution and exposed the misconfiguration.
creator: Cesar Roque Quiros
mitigation_plan: Validated configuration changes and confirmed correct behavior post-fix. Communicated the correct usage of metadata stages to avoid similar misconfigurations.
longterm_mitigation: Introduce validation checks, warnings, or improved documentation in Property Manager to prevent configuring response header modifications at unsupported metadata stages. This will help avoid similar issues when stricter processing features like XML Optimization are enabled.

summary: Citi seeing poor performance from London when using Brave region in Germany to reach parent and origin via London
description:
Citi's customer 'Equals' has reported performance problems when requesting from AWS EC2 London which occur when the requests go through the Brave region in Germany from our research. When requests go through London to London the performance is as expected. 

They are not reusing pconn or ssl session so the round trips add up and they are trying to stay under half a second. 

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/EIPB-Citi-Equals] has the most details filtered down, but more information in [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10725859] and CSE had opened [https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi?issue_id=B-V-69IYKOZ] but I am not sure it was looked into as a brave issue initially

Domain: tts.sandbox.apib2b.citi.com

AWS London IPS:
18.169.48.138 
3.11.180.171 
35.178.84.186

Brave IPS
Within: 23.55.37.0/24. and 2.18.28.0/24
23.55.37.197
23.55.37.203
23.55.37.200
2.18.28.11
2.18.28.8
2.18.28.5
Parent Ghost Regions:
2.18.26.17  (49150 - FABRIC-LON3h)
2.16.144.27 (46213 - FABRIC-LON21f)

Can we adjust the routing to keep EC2 within London as when so the performance is the expected level. We recognize it is like 25ms added, but  that adds up with the excess roundtrips from the client setup.
creator: Billy Tiemann

summary: F-CS-10816794 - FirstByteTimeout For PUT Is Being Retried
description:
Account: Carl Zeiss AG
Hostname: q-live-med.ecommerce.zeiss.com
Configuration: q-live-iqs-ecommerce.zeiss.com v42
CP Code: 1671891

 

We are seeing PUT requests with ERR_FIRST_BYTE_TIMEOUT when going forward to the origin getting retried. I don't see anything in the configuration that would cause PUT requests to get retried, and as far as I know and all the documentation I have checked, PUT should not be retried on ERR_FIRST_BYTE_TIMEOUT.

summary: {F-CS-10732926} Flits overload by horse racing event traffic affects multiple customer’s connectivity in mr1327
description:
+*Environment:*+

Product: Dynamic Site Accelerator
Property Hostname:  gw.aeonpayment.com
Edge Hostname:  gw.aeonpayment.com.edgekey.net
Slot: 26017, Enhanced TLS (SNI)
Maprule ID: 1327
CPcode: 826437

Client IP: 52.197.137.56, 52.197.158.90, 52.198.9.30, 52.198.79.125 (All are located in AS16509 of Amazon Data Services)

+*Issue:*+

The customer’s payment transaction in POST from the clients above which is an API server aggregating the end users application request intermittently fails in C2G connection level. 

*The timeframe is:*
2025-12-21 06:45:43 - 06:46:41 UTC
2026-01-11 06:49:04 - 06:49:27 UTC
2026-01-12 06:49:10 - 06:49:23 UTC

+*Analysis:*+

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/ER-258899]
 * ghost.ddc.log for those client's requests:   The assigned region during the time of incident was either r48512 or r36436.
 * Flotslaod spike caused Ghost’s self-suspention, which is the direct cause of disruption.
 ** 2025-12-21 06:45:43 - 06:46:41 UTC in r48512
[https://mapper.akamai.com/dnsp/#from%3D1768111200000%26to%3D1768114800000%26dataFrom%3D1768114791000%26useGMTTime%3Dtrue%26timeRange%3D%7B%22min%22%3A1768111200000%2C%22max%22%3A1768114800000%7D%26regionId%3D36436%26service%3DS%26]
 ** 2026-01-12 06:49:10 - 06:49:23 UTC in r48512

 * 
 ** 
 *** [https://mapper.akamai.com/dnsp/#from%3D1768197600000%26to%3D1768201200000%26dataFrom%3D1768201199000%26useGMTTime%3Dtrue%26timeRange%3D%7B%22min%22%3A1768197600000%2C%22max%22%3A1768201200000%7D%26regionId%3D48512%26service%3DS%26]
 *** !image-2026-01-16-15-58-52-878.png|width=447,height=210!
 *** !image-2026-01-16-16-07-04-188.png|width=460,height=335!
 *** [https://map.akamai.com/cgi-bin/FreeFlow/Analysis/HDB_Server.pl?startmonth=1&startday=12&startyear=2026&starthour=6&startmin=0&endmonth=1&endday=12&endyear=2026&endhour=8&endmin=0&param1=48512&dbname=production&param2=&param3=&param4=&display=graph&normalize=none&ptdisp=normal&yaxis=y-axis&size=large&scope=all&query_name=REGION_FLYTELOAD&customSql=] !image-2026-01-16-16-00-26-133.png|width=489,height=234!  
 * Deeper analysis for the case in 2026-01-12 06:49:10 - 06:49:23 UTC
 ** r74:  the top flits consumers in r48512 in 06:48 - 06:50 UTC are www.jra.go.jp, sp.jra.jp, app.jra.jp (Account: Japan Racing Association) firstly and race.api.netkeiba.com, race.sp.netkeiba.com (Account: Net Dreamers Co., Ltd.) secondary.
 *** Note that those hosts are all configured with maprule 1327.
 ** r60:  metadata application cost is quite higher (off by an order of magnitude) in the timeframe even in the same request path and on the same edge, compared with no-spiky timeframe.
 *** For instance, sp.jra.jp/JRADB/accessP.html the cost spikes sharply around 06:48:50 UTC
 ** r70:cpu:  flits cost per product indicates ‘waf’ is the majority and it’s getting higher gradually during the spike.  
 * Overview of traffic for Japan Racing Association
 ** 2026-01-12 06:49:10 - 06:49:23 UTC
 *** [https://control.akamai.com/apps/reports/#/predefined/traffic-by-hostname-2?cpcodes=1456894,1860159,1860208,1860171,1860131,1859391,1860161,1859400,1860168,1858931,1860120,1864369,1860137,1864375,492843,1860170,1864381,1864371,1860149,1859422,1860118,1859409,1864379,459616,1864378,1864363,459591&start=2026-01-12T00:00:00Z&end=2026-01-12T23:59:59Z&timezone=Greenwich]
 *** !image-2026-01-16-16-03-38-442.png|width=453,height=150!
 ** Japan Racing Association and Net Dreamers Co., Ltd. are the service company for horse racing events that hosts scheduled graded races at the fixed times on every weekend. The traffic spike is the one anticipated. 

+*Ask:*+
AEON Financial Service Co.,Ltd is a critical service provider for shop payment transactions, and very sensitive to this type of error. IAT is looking for any mitigations. I would like to ask you to review the analysis above and suggest the right direction to mitigate the issue. Is there any feature to make it on the platform side (like ongoing work for CRP)? Any region capacity tuning (like FOM)? Any idea to suppress flits consumption in Japan Racing Association’s spiky traffic?
root_cause: Japan Racing and Net Dreamers have flit intensive API like traffic but use DSA product and map. During the event betting days the spike in their traffic causes the JP region to get overwhelmed impacting other customers traffic , AEON here.
creator: Yasuhiro Oyabu
mitigation_plan: Prespread to minghost=32
longterm_mitigation: Japan Racing/Net Dreamers used API product/map

We need to update Samsung about why it failed as the impact was long-lasting and the stale content was served to multiple users because many Ghosts went forward to the .38 IP.

Thanks

summary: {F-CS-10731241} - 256 segfaults causing financial transactions to fail

* Acc: Fidelity Information Services, LLC - AANA-WQFJ3
 * Domain: eftwebpay.au.fisglobal.com

 

The customer started reporting intermittent errors on financial transactions around mid December. Client side they see a 504 but there are no matching ddc lines, meaning the connection fails on the edge.

 
 * Upon further checking all of the errors they've  seen are on machines from r 51352.
 * Cross checking their timestamps with ours, without a fail there's always a segfault  256 rollcode, always caused by traffic on  ws.airbnb.cn.

summary: Fail action latching to initial negatively cached request
description:
Configuration || a-prope54d1acg-1c6a782 v74
CPcode || 1924322 
Etherpad ||  [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/0116_cbs_load]

Region logs for CPcode 1924322 Jan 15th 21:20 -21:35
Location:
lsg-gss13 /home/tdeary CP1924322-202601152120-23.33.25.197-974-1768588496.gz

Scope
We encountered this issue while load testing with CBS for their upcoming UFC Event, however similar configuration snippets will also be used in the AFC and Super Bowl events.

Problem Statment
When we tested 100% failover from the primary Fastly origin to their backup Varnish origin we are seeing 504s served to ~25% of client requests. All of the 504 FA requests are negatively cached and when we trace them we can see the MTSE thread is the same for each tier of failover and latching to the initial Negatively cached request.

executive_summary:
I do see that the original request is served from negative cache and a fail-action is triggered, but the fail-action requests are “latched” to the original negatively cached response and I see the the fail-action metadata includes no-store.
Recommended adjust no-store in fail-action metadata to allow the edge to forward and process fail-action requests. Also review negative cache settings to ensure fail-action logic is not bypassed.
root_cause:
If a request is marked with no-store in fail action metadata, the edge will not cache the error response, so subsequent requests will not hit negative cache. However, if negative cache is already present for a URL, fail action requests may be latched to the original negative cached object, and the fail action may not execute as expected.
For fail-action to work, the edge must be allowed to forward the request and process/store the response.
creator: Tyler Deary
mitigation_plan: Adjust fail-action metadata by removing/relaxing no-store so the edge can forward requests and properly execute failover instead of latching to negative cache.
longterm_mitigation: Refine configuration to align fail-action and negative caching behavior, ensuring failover logic isn’t bypassed due to cached error responses.

summary: Site shield parent rolled due to excessive test traffic
description:
Account: NIKE, Inc.
Config: retail-payment.services.nike.com v4 (since Jul 26, 2024)
DP: retail-payment.services.nike.com / e31097.x.akamaiedge.net / SNI-cert
Logs: https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10824541

Issue:
The customer has reported sporadic spikes in 30.xx reference errors (Since 17 Jan 2026) across multiple Nike hostnames, specifically confirming issues with retail-payment.services.nike.com (CPcode: 1485411). This hostname is directly related to in-store payment affecting their sales.

What has been done:
Since 17 Jan, MR was suspended on MR: 5741 [siteshield map s441.akamaiedge.net] for four parent regions (39411, 41744, 39183, 43333).
- Traffic was subsequently rerouted to a new parent region (38361) which is now experiencing similar issues.
- Regions recovered after the suspension of Maprule 5741.
- During this period, the only spike in maprule activity for this region was from 5741; after its suspension, the region recovered.
- Overall capacity has been reduced by 30% due to the suspension of multiple parent regions.

Common SSL error:
{noformat}
      SSL_get_error	5 - SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL
      Linux errno	104 - ECONNRESET: Connection reset by peer
{noformat}

NOCC tix:
https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi?issue_id=B-V-6AGNE9N

We suspect the certs were not loading properly on these servers.


For the region 41744:
 !Screenshot 2026-01-18 at 11.22.04 AM.jpg|thumbnail! 
 !Screenshot 2026-01-18 at 11.23.01 AM.jpg|thumbnail! 

We can see Flits and Bits drop fir the timeframe it was majorly serving the MR 5741 traffic. Once the region has MR suspension in place then we noticed the region Flits and Bits improved.

https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=2d&region=41744&network=A&maprule=&display=total&ymax=&utcoffset=5

https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=2d&region=41744&network=A&maprule=5741&display=total&ymax=&utcoffset=5.5


Other suspending & un-suspending maprules, what else can we do?


CC: [~bupadhya]
root_cause: Excessive and unexpected test traffic caused an overflow on the site shield region
creator: Alan Boon

summary: Increase in latency for Phonepe across all domains
description:
[B-V-6AK75PD|https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi?issue_id=B-V-6AK75PD]
Summary: Starting Jan 6, 2026 at 14:00 UTC, Phonepe has seen an increase in latency across all metrics and across 95th,99th percentile, across all domains.

 

Customer is seeing latency at their API end points which is impacting their transactions.

This change in latency is in line with mapping change for VIP optimization that was done on Jan 6, 2026 and we see improvement since 13th Jan but is still not back to levels prior to the change (in Dec).

 

There is a Risk involved as customer might move their traffic to Cloudfare leading to a financial loss of 2.5 Million USD.
executive_summary:
[29-Jan]
Issue: Increased latency to end user.
Resolution Summary: Phase 2 incident activities
1. PMR related.
2. Increase prespread for Phonepe slot (current at 16)
Blocker: None
root_cause:
Score adj changes as part of VIP Optimization project caused the traffic to shift to public regions which were overwhelmed with traffic shifts already. Customer saw increase of  ~50-100ms  latency at 95th /99th percentile.

https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GSPMAPSTRA-5090
https://collaborate.akamai.com/confluence/spaces/EPA/pages/1278384124/VipOpt
https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GSPMAPSTRA-4447
creator: Trisha Thomas
mitigation_plan: Rollback of the score adj of 10ms .
longterm_mitigation: Better observability on such changes.

summary: CBS - Origin Failover Requests Not Returning 200s to Edge
description:
CP Code: 1702457
Timeframe: 22:36 UTC
Etherpad: [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/ms_01_17_26]
Impact: Mild customer impact but customer removed traffic. Definitely appears to show a much larger vulnerability

This issue is possibly related to ERE-3150. During CBS/Paramount's coverage of the Bills v Broncos NFL playoff game the customer reported a low baseline of 5xx edge errors they were monitoring. At 22:36 UTC 1/17/26 there was a moderate spike in 5xx edge errors that prompted the customer to remove Akamai from the CDN mix. The spike lasted around 45 seconds. From a 1 minute cross section of logs we can see that it made up about 0.5% of edge requests. 
!image-2026-01-18-17-37-22-143.png|width=597,height=261!
{code:java}
[mshea@lsg-gss13 /home/tdeary]$ zcat CP1702457-202601172236-23.33.25.197-694-1768690033.gz | awk '$2=="r" && !($16~/[ftGKPX]/) && !($11~/^(10\.|127\.0\.0\.1)/) {print $14, $15}; $2=="S" && !($18~/[ftGKPX]/) && !($11~/^(10\.|127\.0\.0\.1)/) {print $16, $32}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | awk '{array[$2" "$3]=$1; sum+=$1} END { for (i in array) printf "%s %d %3.3f%%\n", i, array[i], array[i]/sum*100}' | sort -rn -k3,3 | column -t -s' '

 {code}

Investigation quickly showed the 504s were negatively cached r lines impacting mainly edge servers and clients in the US west coast. 
{code:java}
[mshea@lsg-gss13 /home/tdeary]$  zcat CP1702457-202601172236-23.33.25.197-694-1768690033.gz | awk '$2=="r" && !($16~/[ftGKPX]/) && !($11~/^(10\.|127\.0\.0\.1)/)  && $14==504 {print $2, $16};  $2=="S" && !($18~/[ftGKPX]/) && !($11~/^(10\.|127\.0\.0\.1)/) && 416==504 {print $2, $18}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn
  31838 r njHV
    608 r iHV
     83 r nHV
     47 r gHV
      7 r moHV
      5 r mgHV
      1 r oHV {code}

This configuration ([a-propefe9ga3a-9bhh7dd|https://control.akamai.com/apps/property-manager/#/property/11389582?gid=192652]) has 2 origins:
*Primary* - *propefe9ga3a-akamai.airspace-origin.cbsivideo.com* aka "{*}Fastly{*}" as it points to the Fastly origin shield
*Backup* - *p-ause1-propeller-syncbak.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com aka* "{*}Syncbak{*}" as it points to Syncbak via AWS

The configuration should have failover to backup at each tier. 

We can explain the error spike in that one of the Fastly origin IPs (146.75.93.188) suddenly began returning 0xx FIRST_BYTE_TIMEOUT errors (the other Fastly origin servers appeared healthy). However, edge request analysis showed that requests were not consistently executing failover, which after speaking with the EA we learned is a byproduct of enough requests upstream erroring, and the failover requests bypassing the td tier were going forward to the same problematic origin server. 

Looking further upstream to the td tier there were 2 things preventing edge from fetching a healthy segment request from the td0 tier:

1. 17% of the requests to the backup (Syncbak) origin were returning a 403 response from the origin. This is something we're pursuing with the customer, and not the focus of this jira.
2. In instances where td went forward to the Syncbak origin and successfully returned a 200, ghost still did not return a 200 successful response to the edge


On point 2 here is a sample instance to illustrate. I'm grepping on a segment name, and looking at all log lines requesting or serving that segment from a metrocache td0. There are a number of instances here where the Syncbak origin returns a 200 and receives the full content body, however there is no successful response returned to the edge.

*Ask for ERE:*

executive_summary:
CBS removed Akamai from the CDN rotation during their coverage of the NFL playoffs on Saturday 1/17/26 after a moderate spike in edge 504 errors. After review it looks as though failover to the customer's configured backup origin is working with relative success but 200 responses are not being returned to the edge causing negatively cached 504 responses to be served at the edge in the presence of an origin error spike.

I do see that the original request is served from negative cache and a fail-action is triggered, but the fail-action requests are “latched” to the original negatively cached response and I see the the fail-action metadata includes no-store.
Recommended adjust no-store in fail-action metadata to allow the edge to forward and process fail-action requests. Also review negative cache settings to ensure fail-action logic is not bypassed.
root_cause:
If a request is marked with no-store in fail action metadata, the edge will not cache the error response, so subsequent requests will not hit negative cache. However, if negative cache is already present for a URL, fail action requests may be latched to the original negative cached object, and the fail action may not execute as expected.
For fail-action to work, the edge must be allowed to forward the request and process/store the response.
creator: Mike Shea
mitigation_plan: Fix the configuration by removing the invalid metadata-stage constraint and ensuring response headers are modified only in supported stages
longterm_mitigation: Introduce validation, warnings, and improved documentation in Property Manager/Optimize XML to prevent unsupported configurations and enforce correct stage usage.

summary: [B-V-6AQ2J8P] [F-CS-10827625] Ghost rolls on r52910
description:
NOCC had received an escalation from Akatec on r52910 ghost rolls, which led to customer impact. I'm looking for confirmation if ghost rolls which happened on 19/01/2026 ~00:51 UTC for most machines in the region are related to KMI associated rolls as described in ERE-2897, GREL-3643, GREL-4939 - with expected fix in Ghost-22.4.1 release.

summary: {F-CS-10735111} Issues when multiple failover behaviours match
description:
Hello ERE team,

 

We're having a problem with some tags in failover metadata, when multiple failover behaviours are matched and last match win is expected.

1. *Query String:*

When the failover behaviour sets "Include Query String" to ON, then that generates the tag <preserve-query-string>on</preserve-query-string> .
But there is no way to revert this setting: when "Include Query String" is set to OFF, I'd expect it generates a <preserve-query-string>off</preserve-query-string> . But in reality, no preserve-query-string tag is generated -> the earlier setting persists unchanged. The setting promises to "do not *include* query string", but the behaviour is actually "do not change the last applied setting for qs" - that can be misleading. So if at any point (an earlier match on another failover behaviour) preserve-query-string got set to ON, the request gets stuck with this setting.

 


2. *Failover path:*

It is similar but a little more subtle with Modify Request Path: when set to YES it generates the <path> tag, when set to NO it doesn't generate any tag. I guess there's two ways the custoemr could interpret "Modify Request Path: NO":
A do not modify the failover path at all-> that is, preserve the original pre-failover request path
B do not modify the failover path here -> that is, whatever failover path has been set (in previous match) that value remains. 

The real behaviour is B, although the behaviour tooltip suggests A.
Anyway, if the customer uses a logic where several failover behaviours match, first with modified path then with preserved, then they need workarounds to actually preserve the pre-failover path.
This  can get further complicated further if QS settings also vary between these failovers.

Technically, this aligns with last match win rule https://docs.akamai.com/esp/user/edgesuite/guides/Metadata_html/index.html if you apply it on tag level, rather than on behaviour level. But customers don't have access to xml, so this can get confusing.


*Is it intentional for these behaviour settings to generate metadata like that? Can this be improved?*


Here is a test setup with a few scenarios: pincer-dev-clone-aberdin-test.com v101 (stg).
(account: Akamai Professional Services)

 

+Behaviour vs metadata:+
 * modify path and preserve qs - both tags are generated -> OK

!image-2026-01-20-17-04-33-215.png|width=639,height=339!
{code:java}
                    <comment:note value="start rule: 4156103f-6674-4e18-b153-aaa35464bd33"/>
                    <comment:note value="Start Feature failAction"/>
                    <forward:availability.fail-action2>
                        <status>on</status>
                        <type>serve-alternate</type>
                        <request-host>%(AK_HOST)</request-host>
                        <path>/aberdin/failover/sorry_500.html</path>
                        <preserve-query-string>on</preserve-query-string>
                    </forward:availability.fail-action2>
                    <comment:note value="End Feature failAction"/>
                    <comment:note value="end rule: 4156103f-6674-4e18-b153-aaa35464bd33"/> {code}
 

 
 * don't modify path -> tooltip suggests config will actively preserve the original path -> in reality, path is just not changed - no tag generated. If there was a earlier match on failover that changed failover path, that earlier setting will persist, not the original requested path

!image-2026-01-20-17-06-44-703.png|width=498,height=254!
{code:java}
                        <comment:note value="Start Feature failAction"/>
                        <forward:availability.fail-action2>
                            <status>on</status>
                            <type>serve-alternate</type>
                            <request-host>%(AK_HOST)</request-host>
                        </forward:availability.fail-action2>
                        <comment:note value="End Feature failAction"/> {code}
 

 
 * Include Query String set to NO -> in reality, qs setting is not changed (no tag generated)

!image-2026-01-20-17-08-16-509.png|width=619,height=367!
{code:java}
                        <comment:note value="Start Feature failAction"/>
                        <forward:availability.fail-action2>
                            <status>on</status>
                            <type>serve-alternate</type>
                            <request-host>%(AK_HOST)</request-host>
                            <path>%(AK_ORIGINAL_URL)</path>
                        </forward:availability.fail-action2>
                        <comment:note value="End Feature failAction"/> {code}
executive_summary:
We're having a problem with some tags in failover metadata when multiple failover behaviors are matched and last match win is expected.
Issue is that the pm behavior doesn't generate path tag when option modify incoming path = no and it doesn't generate preserve-query-string tag when option include incoming qs = no
Thus, when a request activated the behavior with one of this option = yes, the tag it generates remains active even when a subsequent behavior is activated with same option = no
It seems like the solution involves fixing the metadata generated by PM, 
Though we tested the tag <preserve-query-string>off</preserve-query-string>  separately and it seems working.

I have raised a https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/DPEXREQ-1128, We can follow-up there.
root_cause:
The ticket was raised to understand when you set "Include Query String" to Yes in UI, it will generate tag <preserve-query-string>on</preserve-query-string>. If you set to No, it does not generate any tag.
what is happening, A request first matches on failover with Include QS Yes, the tag gets applied, but then it matches on another failover, with Include QS No. This is where it breaks. Once you set this behaviour to Yes, there is no way to override it further down in the config.

Tested that tag preserve-query-string>off</preserve-query-string separately, and raised DPEXREQ ticket to understand if this is something intentionally by design or if it requires changes.
creator: Marta Siemczonek
mitigation_plan: Follow-up on https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/DPEXREQ-1128
longterm_mitigation: Follow-up on https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/DPEXREQ-1128

summary: cloud wrapper migration question
description:
Hi team,

I have a cloud wrapper migration accross accounts question.

Source Account:TENCENT Inc.

Destination Account:Tencent Cloud(account is not ready now,will migrate CW when it is ready)

For source contract C-11LRMVX,there are 2 cloud wrapper configurations under it.Can I migrate only one of the configration “Nikke” to destination contract?

For another contract +3-1BLBGHT+,there are 6 cloud wrapper conifigrations,can I migrate only “gcloud-contra“ to destination contract?

Can you advise?Then I can follow your advice and decide how we do the migartion.

Thanks in advance!

summary: Intermittent connection timeouts to some Brazil regions
description:
Customer reported intermittent throughput drop on Dec 20th and 21st during different time periods. All these timeframes correlate to an increase in load for r47504:FABRIC-GRU1u. Reviewing the loadgraph for maprules, we noticed some spikes for unassigned traffic. The customer also uses load balancing to control their traffic to different CDNs, so the CNAME wasn't pointing to an edgekey hostname, as seen below. That LBCDN CNAME used to return Akamai IP addresses. 

{code:java}
$ dig +short mb.api.pagseguro.uol
lbcdn-mb.api.pagseguro.uol.
104.18.2.30
104.18.3.30
{code}

Given the unassigned traffic, we suggested adding reporting:maprule-host with the edgekey hostname, which was activated on Jan 8th. 

Even after that change, they reported throughput drops and also had multiple 5-second connect timeouts on their "health" requests. 
https://mb.api.pagseguro.uol/actuator/health

We didn't get more details on the throughput drop; however received the following for connection timeout samples, along with the server IP. 
* 01/10 15:06:05 - IP 2.20.132.66 (HERO=2.18.20.130) (IG 4) is in region 49247 (SDN-GRU21j) ESSL (DNS:RX)
* 01/11 19:09:08 - IP 23.63.250.165 (HERO=23.62.50.37) (IG 1) is in region 52099 (FABRIC-GRU12w) ESSL (DNS:RX)
* 01/12 01:03:07 - IP 23.63.250.20 (HERO=23.193.118.148) (IG 10) is in region 52100 (FABRIC-GRU12x) ESSL (DNS:RX)

We reviewed load graphs for these regions, and we were able to see a spike in traffic, mainly for MR 1327, around that time. DNSP showed self-suspension for the first 2 timeframes, but the 3rd one didn't have any self-suspensions. 
Links can be found from line 290 here: https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10780845

Reviewing cache.log, we see an increase in AVG manager cycle for the 1st example (going as high as 42k), while the 2nd and 3rd didn't have the spikes. 

{code:java}
[seselvan@lsg-gss3 /home/msoares/uol_2026/cache_logs/jan_10_15PM]$ zcat cache-GIP2.20.132.66-202601101500-23.33.25.197-745-1768262825.gz | grep "Robust - stats: Accepts" | cut -d ' ' -f 2-300 | grep -oE "^\w+.\w+|Accepts: http/https \s*(\S+)|flit \s*(\S+)|CPU: all \s*(\S+)|AVG manager cycle \s*(\S+)"|paste - - - - - | time_convert | grep "0+0000" | less -S
2026/01/10-15:04:30+0000        Accepts: http/https 47/2561     flit 76%,       CPU: all 83%,   AVG manager cycle 1300us
2026/01/10-15:04:40+0000        Accepts: http/https 42/2757     flit 81%,       CPU: all 85%,   AVG manager cycle 1626us
2026/01/10-15:04:50+0000        Accepts: http/https 52/2852     flit 90%,       CPU: all 95%,   AVG manager cycle 24655us
2026/01/10-15:05:00+0000        Accepts: http/https 80/2475     flit 93%,       CPU: all 95%,   AVG manager cycle 42931us
2026/01/10-15:05:10+0000        Accepts: http/https 58/2825     flit 96%,       CPU: all 96%,   AVG manager cycle 9453us
2026/01/10-15:05:20+0000        Accepts: http/https 74/3028     flit 95%,       CPU: all 95%,   AVG manager cycle 33224us
2026/01/10-15:05:30+0000        Accepts: http/https 54/2958     flit 96%,       CPU: all 96%,   AVG manager cycle 33073us
2026/01/10-15:05:40+0000        Accepts: http/https 22/248      flit 69%,       CPU: all 51%,   AVG manager cycle 746us
2026/01/10-15:05:50+0000        Accepts: http/https 34/2287     flit 86%,       CPU: all 90%,   AVG manager cycle 48833us
2026/01/10-15:06:00+0000        Accepts: http/https 21/1125     flit 101%,      CPU: all 85%,   AVG manager cycle 2621us
2026/01/10-15:06:10+0000        Accepts: http/https 15/2965     flit 94%,       CPU: all 96%,   AVG manager cycle 35482us
2026/01/10-15:06:20+0000        Accepts: http/https 17/3817     flit 92%,       CPU: all 97%,   AVG manager cycle 25616us
2026/01/10-15:06:30+0000        Accepts: http/https 15/2082     flit 62%,       CPU: all 72%,   AVG manager cycle 893us
2026/01/10-15:06:40+0000        Accepts: http/https 19/2304     flit 101%,      CPU: all 97%,   AVG manager cycle 37230us
2026/01/10-15:06:50+0000        Accepts: http/https 19/1218     flit 57%,       CPU: all 53%,   AVG manager cycle 619us
2026/01/10-15:07:00+0000        Accepts: http/https 13/111      flit 34%,       CPU: all 33%,   AVG manager cycle 395us
{code}

We did some flit analysis on the ghost.ddc.log, and api-service.shein.com seems to report high flit around that time 
For the 1st timeframe, we see it increasing from 3 to 9% of the flit for that machine, while for the 3rd timeframe, it was not there in the top 10 hostnames, and then takes up 9% of flit usage within 10 seconds. Analysis can be found line 522 - 626: https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10780845

{code:java}
[seselvan@lsg-gss7 ~/uol]$ zcat GIP2.20.132.66-202601101503-23.33.25.197-990-1768318555.gz | grep "r 17" | awk -v ts=1768057490 '($3>ts-5 && $3<ts){a=$18;split($75,b,":");x[a]+=b[1];y+=b[1];}END{for (k in x)print k, x[k]/5, x[k]*100/y;}' | sort -k 2,2nr | head | column -t
api-service.shein.com             1498680  9.98179
mb.api.pagseguro.uol              811906   5.40761
a0.muscache.com                   737150   4.90971
ap-gateway-web.bancopan.com.br    442926   2.95006
static.bradesco.com.br            417412   2.78013
api-bancodigital.bancopan.com.br  377413   2.51372

ps4.np.playstation.net            303227   2.01961
api16-log-sg2.pangle.io           256251   1.70673
{code}

We also used Edge Log Miner for these 2 timeframes
10th Jan: https://deadpool.akamai.com/loganalysisapp/data-view/1e9173f8-ecb9-4053-81cd-dbbb9022efe9/
12th Jan: https://deadpool.akamai.com/loganalysisapp/data-view/5a18d70a-b347-43ea-a9e1-7fc267e1f58a/

Reviewing Edge Log Miner, I noticed an increase in slow handshake, especially because the customer is not able to establish a connection for 5-seconds. Around 15:06 on 10th Jan, we see slot 74346 appears to have some slow handshakes, which is for www.itau.com.br
 !Screenshot 2026-01-21 at 5.43.19 PM.jpg|width=100%! 

The customer reported additional connect timeouts on Jan 12th 13:16 - 13:19 across 3 different IPs. We briefly reviewed cache.log for those IPs and didn't see an increase in flit/ AVG manager cycle, except for one machine. 

{code:java}
[seselvan@lsg-gss3 /home/msoares/uol_2026/dia12/cachelog_12jan]$ zcat cache-GIP23.63.250.32-202601121310-23.33.25.197-968-1768421770.gz | awk '$1=="23.63.250.32"'  | grep "Robust - stats: Accepts" | cut -d ' ' -f 2-300 | grep -oE "^\w+.\w+|Accepts: http/https \s*(\S+)|flit \s*(\S+)|CPU: all \s*(\S+)|AVG manager cycle \s*(\S+)"|paste - - - - - | time_convert | grep "0+0000" | less -S

More data on this can be found on a different etherpad: https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10780845-2

Custoemer moved the traffic to Cloudflare, so we didn't have any new samples, however they used a different property to setup the same health requests and we continue to see connect timeouts intermittently. 
Below are the timeouts on Jan 21st 
* 13:22:40 - 13:24:00 UTC Target Edge IP: 95.101.31.71
* 14:56:10 - 14:58:10 UTC Target Edge IP: 95.100.156.180

api16-core-c-alisg.tiktokv.com           363036   2.57776
www.livelo.com.br                        313197   2.22387
app.contaazul.com                        268158   1.90407
{code}

Reviewing cache.log analysis, we see a spike in slot 28622 (*.tiktokv.com) for slot handshakes, although it always seems to have some level of slow handshakes, but the increase perhaps confirms the issue with the machine. 
 !Screenshot 2026-01-21 at 6.00.04 PM.jpg|width=100%! 
 !Screenshot 2026-01-21 at 6.00.37 PM.jpg|width=100%! 

Log analysis for the latest timeouts can be found here: https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10780845-4

Ask: Please help us review the analysis and confirm the root cause for these connect timeouts. Customer has moved their traffic to Cloudflare, so it's important that we identify the root cause and eliminate these connect timeouts so that they can confidently bring some of the traffic back. Let us know if you need more information.
executive_summary: Customer reported a throughput drop intermittently, which correlates with a 5-second connect timeout to their health requests. At this point, we don't have their traffic, and we continue to get reports of a connect timeout. Log analysis shows flit spikes along with spikes in slow handshake around these times. The customer has moved traffic away from Akamai due to these instances.
root_cause: DNS override by customer
creator: Senthuran Selvanayagam
mitigation_plan: NA
longterm_mitigation: Customer should use Akamai DNS to reach us.

summary: EIPB Reachability issues reported likely due to network loss
description:
Reachability issues reported from multiple locations in England (London, Manchester, Cambridge). 79% of errors attributed to Network Loss detected via ThousandEyes and confirmed by end-users for total1.worldpay.com and total2.worldpay.com (EIPB - eipb-total-waf.worldpay.com) during January 21st 11:43 to 13:08. Issue mitigated post 13:08 UTC Jan 21. 

Investigation shows that BraveIP is not logged from customer's traceroute, but upon checking the logs collected from all brave regions for the customer's prefix, we could see 
r51078:FABRIC-LON31s brave region was handling the traffic.

arcd.log shows the following
.....

embryonic_rst
184.25.122.197 1768995491.191 E (8649,8709) KernelNotificationThread.cc:60: [netlink] Netlink: removed connection in bad state   64.29.140.111 55193  104.94.221.140   443      D  95.100.104.194             new edgecl:   embryonic_rst
184.25.122.197 1768995491.192 E (8649,8709) KernelNotificationThread.cc:60: [netlink] Netlink: removed connection in bad state   64.29.140.111 41027  104.94.221.140   443      D  95.100.104.182             new edgecl:   embryonic_rst
184.25.122.197 1768995499.338 I (8649,8772) AckQueue.cc:136: [nfq] Nfq-ACK-158 #2157785 (64.29.140.111+45377 -> 104.94.221.140+443) Unknown Ack: resetting connection 

It looks like ACK packet did not match any known or valid connection state, so the system proactively closed the connection to maintain integrity.

https://grafana.akam.ai/org172/dashboard/db/brave-kpi-monitoring?from=now-24h&to=now-10m. - total default destinations per second sees a sharp increase in grafana. 

 !image-2026-01-22-14-34-58-864.png|thumbnail! 

But logs do not not show new target added  - something like this
*1591837644.028 W (3567,3831) KernelNotificationThread.cc:57: [netlink] Netlink: added connection [79.159.217.90+60206 -> 23.1.106.236+443] -> 92.122.241.227 (Original target was: 185.43.182.82)*

but rather, resetting the connection as follows
