summary: Dior Config review for - SI-00023426
description:
Ask was to look to see if there was anything in the config that could be tuned to help in the scenario  of oocm mapping - request back is to give any specific examples that are not oocm related / cap related  - SPECIFICALLY in relation to china.

 

PSG suggestion was already put in chat - 

1.The srto is missing from origin, that needs to be fixed

2.they are not using akadns88/edgekey88  which is for compliance in china

3.SS map includes eu servers but does not request anything but china regions

4.lots of data is put into the flex cache key but we are not forcing eccu purge so we likely are not getting optimal caching

5.this could be confirmed but i think image manager is not in china so all those requests have to leave the country to get filled

6.Netstorage origin dior-common is in london and New Jersey

7.does cloudflare have china service? We use [stores.dior.cn.pagescdn.com|https://stores.dior.cn.pagescdn.com/] as an origin which is served by cloudflare

8.ohd and lowering retries can help us and them
executive_summary: Look to see if there is any obvious perf improvements that could help general performance
resolution_description: beyond some other optimizations PSG suggested, adding a few more APJ SS regions could help reduce latency to China Origin if we are OOC, gives more options loccally.
root_cause: SI action item to review DIOR config to see if there are any general improvements that could help in times of ooc.
creator: Kyle Griffin
mitigation_plan: consultation
longterm_mitigation: stop gap while chinacdn product is EOL.

summary: {F-CS-10786478} MSFT: OIPACL OOC mapping
description:
Configuration: storeedgefd.dsx.mp.microsoft.com_hc

Product: DD + GTM

 

Around Jan 7 , 09:40 GMT, customer reported spike in traffic going to US datacenter.  We could see at the same time spike in 0xx, 5xx and slow requests. 

Checking the logs we found end users in HK going to DC in US because the OIPACL region mapping changed from region located in HK to a region located in US.

The same behavior was found for regions in Amsterdam going to the US datacenter.

 

For example, we found the parent region 45795 geo located in US Santa Clara had suddenly started picking high traffic:  [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10786478]  line 178 and 191

 

You can see it in hats as well

[https://grafana-hats1-epremo.akamai.com/d/e20b1f9b-85f2-4323-8590-ab10e9778885/hats-v3?orgId=1&var-load=yes&var-breakdown=srv_region&var-maprule=api-e6827&var-destination=-&var-cpcode=1654086&var-cache_hit=-&var-service=S&var-region_country=-&var-region_continent=-&var-region=-&var-client_country=HK&var-client_continent=-&var-child=-&var-client_asnum=-&var-client_asname=-&var-client_asaccount=-&var-client_geo_id=-&var-proto=-&var-connection_type=-&var-ipv=-&var-tput=5000&var-rtt=50&var-size=500&var-topn=1-10&var-resolution=10min&var-hours=-&var-title=breakdown%3Dsrv_region%20%2F%20service%3DS%20%2F%20cpcode%3D1654086%20%2F%20maprule%3Dapi-e6827%20%2F%20client_country%3DHK&var-title_no_breakdown=service%3DS%20%2F%20cpcode%3D1654086%20%2F%20maprule%3Dapi-e6827%20%2F%20client_country%3DHK&from=1767772779375&to=1767783607718]

 

!image-2026-01-22-12-04-49-844.png|width=1230,height=147!

 

I dont see any problem in the OIPACL region located in  in HK (34656) that was taking traffic before the US region 

[https://map.akamai.com/cgi-bin/FreeFlow/Analysis/HDB_Server.pl?startmonth=1&startday=7&startyear=2026&starthour=0&startmin=0&endmonth=1&endday=7&endyear=2026&endhour=19&endmin=0]¶m1=34656&dbname=production¶m2=¶m3=¶m4=&display=graph&normalize=none&ptdisp=normal&yaxis=y-axis&size=large&scope=all&query_name=DASHBOARD2&customSql=

 

[https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=start1767741780end1767838980]®ion=34656&network=A&maprule=&display=region&ymax=&utcoffset=2

!image-2026-01-22-12-38-00-848.png|width=652,height=402!

 

We need your help to find out why the OPIACL region in US started taking traffic from Europe and Asia MCH and how to avoid this scenario in the future

 

Details of investigation and logs are here https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10786478
executive_summary: - OOC mapping in OIPACL regions
root_cause: Origin look up changed origins IPs received by machine hence the mapids changed . Logic for origin IP handout to be reviewed by IAT.
creator: Cesar Roque Quiros
mitigation_plan: na
longterm_mitigation: na

{noformat}
 

This machine does not have Cache or DDC logs around this time period. The machine still seems to be in a bad state. I am trying to figure out what caused the issue with this machine and how to fix it.

 

There was a NOCC case open - [https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi?issue_id=B-V-6BJKONX]

They referenced some cache logs indicating that there might be connectivity issues, but that was over an our before the machine experienced issues.
creator: Ted Frost

summary: {F-CS-10846698} 503 with ERR_NON_SUPERNET_IP was served to client when config has Cloud Interconnects Product (GCP) enabled
description:
Hi Team, 

Domain: [www.mobile01.com|https://www.mobile01.com/] 
property : [prop-www.mobile01.com|https://prop-www.mobile01.com/] v53
Recent Incident window:  
1) 1 hr ( 17:40 - 18:40 UTC 22nd Jan)  
2) 3 hr (15-18 UTC 19th Jan) 

Etherpad with logs and customer impact analysis:
[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10846698]  

config has Cloud Interconnects for Google Cloud (GCP) enabled with cloud location as Asia along with TD (Global (ch2)). 

We see ERR_DNS_IN_REGION failure as child and parent in same region followed by child going forward to origin directly where we see ERR_NON_SUPERNET_IP error.

Cache.log for same: 
{code:java}
210.61.248.29 /a/logs/cache.log.old> grep "1bff4c16" cache.log.o8fa4-41a2z-rl2d7-y84pa-26798.171.1769103208.1769106812.gz
1769105509.029|7f1591262880|    7f145dd66d10|1bff4c16|119| 1|C| 5908| (skipped 9 instances of following message...)
1769105509.029|7f1591262880|    7f145dd66d10|1bff4c16|119| 1|C| 5908| FWD-FAIL(1bff4c16): connection to fwdServer 35.201.120.164 was closed before sending response, therefore retrying to connect{code}

As we know Cloud Interconnects for Google Cloud (GCP) which incorporate SR and OIPACL origin  and we could see that the child IP was non-OIPACL and hence the error. 

As it is part of Web product Quick retry is not an option, also checking the 
https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/DELIVERENG-22835 we don't see any progress there on BMP. 

From the confluence page of OIPACL could see below metadata to be added as part of mitigation
[https://collaborate.akamai.com/confluence/spaces/EPRE/pages/1147089350/OIPACL+Product+Profile+Page+and+Troubleshooting+Document#OIPACLProductProfilePageandTroubleshootingDocument-Troubleshooting] 


{code:java}
<assign:variable>
  <name>SIACL_SKIP_IF_IN_MAPRULE_STATE</name>
    <value>off</value>
</assign:variable>{code}

will the above code help mitigate the issue in this scenario where ERR_NON_SUPERNET_IP issues caused by Cloud Interconnects Products ? 

Also could see https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/DPEREQ-312 where it has been mentioned to be out of scope where ERR_NON_SUPERNET_IP issues caused by Cloud Interconnects Products . 

Along with it although above metadata suggested for OIPACL could see under GHOST-5828 salesforce still faced the issue

https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOST-5828?focusedId=12454181&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-12454181


Hence would like to understand below
1) will the metadata suggested in confluence page for mitigation help in this scenario ?
[https://collaborate.akamai.com/confluence/spaces/EPRE/pages/1147089350/OIPACL+Product+Profile+Page+and+Troubleshooting+Document] 

2) if not would it be possible to help with the temporary mitigation for the customer ? 

3) Is https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOST-5828 also considering the ERR_NON_SUPERNET_IP issues caused by Cloud Interconnects Products ?
executive_summary: If adding CI to the configuration caused an increase in that error, then it is likely metadata related to CI that may have caused it. We are not familiar with the product and cannot provide advisory on metadata changes.
root_cause: NA, we are not familiar with the CI product and cannot provide advisory on metadata changes.
creator: Suman Mishra
mitigation_plan: Nothing from ERE, we are not familiar with the product. Requested to check with CI team
longterm_mitigation: Nothing from ERE

summary: Defect on property manager.
description:
Continuation of https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/PRTLDEVOPS-84647



IAT/PS team have identified a possible defect where Property Manager Includes fail to honor failover origin configuration when the include is invoked within a main PM config.

This has been identified when working on configuration for customer: Jet2

They've asked me to forward the details on for a JIRA to be raised to the portal team.

Main Configuration: vani_emea – v126
Include: testincversion – v8
Hostname: [www.vanitest.com|https://www.vanitest.com/] (tested on Staging network / Cracker; not CNAMEd to production)

Observed Behavior:
 * The main PM configuration calls the include when requests match paths /test and /test1.
 * Each path has a different failover origin defined within the include.
 * When the failover origin is a NetStorage hostname, the failover fails with 400 Bad Request.
 * When the failover origin is another Akamai hostname, it works as expected.

[www.vanitest.com/test|https://www.vanitest.com/test] -> NS Fails
{noformat}
HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request
Server: AkamaiGHost
Mime-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/html
Content-Length: 278
Expires: Wed, 29 Oct 2025 12:40:08 GMT
Date: Wed, 29 Oct 2025 12:40:08 GMT
Connection: close
X-Akamai-Staging: ESSL{noformat}
Reference #227.25333217.1761742000.293853

[www.vanitest.com/test1|https://www.vanitest.com/test1] -> [www1.vanitest.com|https://www1.vanitest.com/] (vani_emea_clone config) -> Works
{noformat}
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: application/json
Server: gunicorn/19.9.0
Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true
Vary: Accept-Encoding
Content-Encoding: gzip
Date: Wed, 29 Oct 2025 12:45:42 GMT
Content-Length: 494
X-Cache: TCP_REFRESH_MISS from a23-50-51-31.deploy.akamaitechnologies.com (AkamaiGHost/22.3.1-cc61a4af8cfe783fae677e7a432c89ef) (S)
X-Cache-Key: /L/16382/1737360/1m/www1.vanitest.com/get
X-Cache-Key-Extended-Internal-Use-Only: /L/16382/1737360/1m/www1.vanitest.com/get vcd=4122
X-True-Cache-Key: /L/www1.vanitest.com/get vcd=4122
X-Serial: 16382
Connection: keep-alive
X-Akamai-Staging: ESSL
X-Check-Cacheable: YES
X-Akamai-Request-ID: 230151
X-Akamai-Pragma-Client-IP: 23.79.238.33, 23.79.238.33{noformat}
From testing, this appears to be linked to how the /AK_PM_VPATH1 variable is being set or inherited when the include executes.
The same logic and failover configuration function correctly when defined directly in the main property, but not when inherited through an include.

This behavior is reproducible on staging, suggesting an issue in the include-level failover handling or variable expansion logic in PM.

Please raise a JIRA with the Property Manager / Includes team to investigate:
Failover handling when defined inside an Include file.
Potential misassignment or non-expansion of /AK_PM_VPATH1 within includes during failover routing.

JET2
[https://tools.gss.akamai.com/art/#227.e4393217.1761203253.2a831c8]
[https://tools.gss.akamai.com/art/#227.04383217.1761123437.1490070]
[https://tools.gss.akamai.com/art/#227.04383217.1761663926.29263c]

Vani's Test:
Reference #227.25333217.1761742000.293853
h3.
creator: Konrad Domanski

summary: {F-CS-10210072} Mobiile Rider: Incorrect forward hostname
description:
Continuation of https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/ERE-2900

 

Mobile Rider is reporting an issue on the account LiveBarn, Inc.

On [cdn-akamai-livebarn.akamaized.net|https://cdn-akamai-livebarn.akamaized.net/] v18, they changed the cache key hostname to incoming host header.

After activating v18, they experienced a spike in traffic to origin and SSL handshake errors.

Checking the logs, before v18 was activated, we observed [origin-i-07f3e478b1395fc3a.livebarn.com|https://origin-i-07f3e478b1395fc3a.livebarn.com/] in both ARL and forward ARL. (line 144 for a request)

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10210072]

While during the issue, we observed [cdn-akamai-livebarn.akamaized.net|https://cdn-akamai-livebarn.akamaized.net/] (line 62 for a request) there which caused the errors as it's not in the Match CN list on the property.

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10210072]

The forward hostname was changed from [origin-i-07f3e478b1395fc3a.livebarn.com|https://origin-i-07f3e478b1395fc3a.livebarn.com/] to [cdn-akamai-livebarn.akamaized.net|https://cdn-akamai-livebarn.akamaized.net/] after activating v18.

Customer rollback to previous version to fix the issue.  

 

However, they recreated the configuration in a test configuration and were able to replicate this behavior. 

From the test configuration we can see in Edge logs that the forward hostname is the same as the client request Host header. 

The Edge is going forward to MCH but also to Origin based on object status field.

h1. Bottom line:

Those 3 conditions have to be true for this to happen:
 # Origin server is populated by a variable
 # That variable is SENSITIVE
 # Setting Cache-key as Incoming host header

<forward:origin-server.host>no default</forward:origin-server.host>

By default, and if used alone, serves as:
 # Origin server
 # Forward host header, and
 # Cache key values

When this value is the Origin server, everything works.

<forward:origin-server.dns-name.value> overrides the origin-server.host as the new Origin server to go forward to.
This tag does not affect the forward Host header value or the cache key.

When you set the cache-key as the incoming host header, you set <forward:origin-server.host>%(AK_HOSTHEADER)</forward:origin-server.host>, which SHOULD be overwritten by <forward:origin-server.dns-name.value>%(PMUSER_ORIP)</forward:origin-server.dns-name.value> but does not.
Ghost is unable to evaluate the sensitive variable and populate it as the <origin-server.dns-name.value>. This could be due to a stage conflict(?) or the fact that the variable gets encrypted.

 
{noformat}
<forward:origin-server>
<host>%(AK_HOSTHEADER)</host>
<dns-name>
<status>on</status>
<value>%(PMUSER_ORIP)</value>
</dns-name>
</forward:origin-server>
<match:arl.type value="cname">
<forward:modify-host-header>
<status>on</status>
<value>%(PMUSER_ORIP)</value>
</forward:modify-host-header>
</match:arl.type>
{noformat}
Solution: Sensitive should not be used. Switching the variable to Hidden or Visible resolves the issue
creator: Cesar Roque Quiros

summary: [AKAM-CASE #F-CS-10865166] Unable to Allocate Full 150TB Web Optimized Capacity for CW Configuration
description:
Config: CW-Web-India

 

The customer reported that they are not able to allocate full 150TB Web Optimized Capacity for India in CW Configuration.


When they try to change the capacity to even 100 TB the following error is displayed:

_Web Optimized capacity for India exceeded max config capacity of 50 TB._

_!Pasted_Image_1_27_26__4_40 PM.png|width=581,height=325!_

 

Why are we getting that capacity error when the UI shows that this location has 150 TB approved capacity, out of which 100 TB capacity is available?
executive_summary:
It is confirmed that while 50 TB is the current system-enforced limit per Web Optimized CW configuration, higher capacity (up to 150 TB in a single configuration) can be considered only as an exception, subject to validation of a strictly web-only use case. The limit exists to prevent misuse of Web CW capacity for media traffic.
From the account side, the intent is to use one CW configuration with 150 TB exclusively for web traffic, but there is no immediate implementation requirement—this was a feasibility check only.
As the information request is fulfilled, the ticket is being archived. A new ERE request will be raised if the customer decides to proceed.
resolution_description: Confirmed that while 50 TB is the default system-enforced limit, an increase up to 150 TB can be supported as an exception for strictly web-only use cases after proper validation. No changes were implemented as there was no immediate requirement.
root_cause: No actual issue observed. The request was a feasibility check regarding increasing the Web Optimized CW configuration limit beyond the standard 50 TB.
creator: Rebeca Gonzalez Porras
mitigation_plan: Clarified system limits and exception criteria to avoid confusion and unnecessary escalations. Advised that a new request should be raised if implementation is required.
longterm_mitigation: Establish clear documentation and guidelines around CW capacity limits and exception handling to reduce repeated feasibility queries and ensure proper usage aligned with intended traffic types.

SSAI setup - planning to use ESSL for Edge and Protocol downgrade to FF for TD.

https://tools.gss.akamai.com/art/#0.f1343217.1769587237.e86aa6 

https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/ER-258818

HAR is attached for the failure

We tried comparing it with the working instance, bypassing Akamai and requesting the origin directly, and we see it working. (HAR attached bypassing Akamai).
HAR files: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1QFXIewS-Zt8D4OkkNmra73Cxisvv68ee?usp=sharing

In working instance via origin, we are seeing 206 response with "Content-Range bytes 0-1/18676578", however via Akamai we see 200 OK without response for Range request. 

You can replicate by spoofing the request to the staging IP 23.50.52.246 for host "backend.leonardo.essilorluxottica.com"
Make sure to change the query parameter every time a request is made, as once the object gets cached on Akamai (maybe when a request is made via Chrome), the second request from Safari works. 

This needs a credential 
Username = elearntestakamai1
Password = T+umYyTU9=c6

Origin IP (51.105.130.75), when we spoof a request to the origin, it does not need credentials. 

At present, the customer has disabled the caching on Akamai production to have it working on Safari. 

Need assistance to understand why the video does not play on Safari and how we can have it fixed.
executive_summary:
The video is not playing on Safari, it works on Chrome and Firefox. The staging version has TTL of 365 days enabled. 

The response from origin contains both Transfer-Encoding header field and a Content-Length header field and as per the rfc2616 doc, https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2616#section-4.4, If a message is received with both a Transfer-Encoding header field and a Content-Length header field, the latter MUST be ignored. Secondly, I created the test property to understand the behavior of cache = ON and POC = OFF.
This below curl request is made for an object where the cache is set for 7d and I don't see the range request is being honored. Hence it is expected behaviour
root_cause: The response from origin contains both Transfer-Encoding header field and a Content-Length header field and as per the rfc2616 doc, https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2616#section-4.4, If a message is received with both a Transfer-Encoding header field and a Content-Length header field, the latter MUST be ignored.
creator: Rohit Sharma
mitigation_plan: NA, As the origin contains both Transfer-Encoding header field and a Content-Length header field, content length will be ignored.
longterm_mitigation: NA

summary: Out-of-country mapping on W.i6w40, W.i6w41, and W.i6w42 for the Reliance JIO provider is causing an increase in 5xx errors at the delivery
description:
* We are seeing out-of-country mapping on W.i6w40, W.i6w41, and W.i6w42 for the Reliance JIO provider. During a review of the allowed region list for the map rule country combination, it appears that most of the top regions are experiencing high FLIT and BIT load.
 * This is contributing to an increase in 5xx errors at the delivery for on going event of [JIOSTAR INDIA PRIVATE LIMITED|https://akamai.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/001A0000004utyhIAA/view]
 * Affected Hostanme : [live11p-ssai-akt-mum.cdn.hotstar.com|https://live11p-ssai-akt-mum.cdn.hotstar.com/]

 * [https://grafana-dmz.akam.ai/org1506/dashboard/db/maprule-qos-v-4-2?from=now-12h&to=now&var-business_unit_owner=Media&var-service=W&var-map_type=edge&var-ip_version=v6Only&var-map_group=All&var-map=W.i6w40&var-map=W.i6w41&var-map=W.i6w42&var-continent=AS&var-geo_name=India&var-provider_name=RELIANCE_INFOCOMM_LIMITED&var-provider_name=RELIANCE_JIO_INFOCOMM_LIMITED&var-case=All]
 * Distance distibution is around 8000 miles and there is a dip in on net mapping and the score distribution spikes above the threshold
 * !image-2026-01-28-23-06-25-946.png|width=625,height=316!
 * [https://mapinfo-ab.akamai.com/mapinfo.php?map=4776&cont=&country=IN]®_set=all

resolution_description: consultation.
root_cause: major increase in traffic combined with install causing deviations of oocm, first leg of request sometimes had issues but retry logic helped reducing error rate to be fairly low
creator: Shwetha Sudhakarshetty
mitigation_plan: before major event, use blackout dates to reduce risk of capacity issues
longterm_mitigation: cap limitations.

summary: {F-CS-10785532} Cloud Interconnect slow DNS for Charles Schwab
description:
Domain: alto-spa-sit01.dev.schwab.tech 
Test URL: : [https://alto-spa-sit01.dev.schwab.tech/platform/mockpath/assets/akamai/testobject.html]

Description: While testing Cloud Interconnect they are noticing very high DNS times are abnormally high (200-600ms) going forward to the CI 
map [Cumulus-e6786.akasrg.akamai.com|https://cumulus-e6786.akasrg.akamai.com/]

From the logs, we get the letter code: l - We didn't have the LL Map for at least one of the target regions. For SR3, this is printed even if just one of the required LL Maps are missing.

Testing by bypassing cloud interconnects yields desirable results.
X-DEBUG-CI-ENABLE: false
Pragma: akamai-x-ci-trace

Issue is very similar to: AKAWEBGH-486

Ask: Would like to know the root cause for the missing maps and how to fix this for the customer
resolution_description: The only way to ensure an LL map entry for a region/serial remains available is by making regular requests to it. So, tt is required to test with frequent requests to eliminate the overall elevated DNS times.
root_cause: The first request is expected to behave this way since the LL map entry will not initially be available in the ghost cache. LL map cache has a defined limit in each ghost, and older entries are evicted when the cache is full to make room for new ones. If requests are made with delayed frequency, the entry will not persist in cache, resulting in higher DNS times being reported.
creator: Billy Tiemann
mitigation_plan: The only way to ensure an LL map entry for a region/serial remains available is by making regular requests to it. So, tt is required to test with frequent requests to eliminate the overall elevated DNS times.
longterm_mitigation:
SRMM team have already increased the cache limit to 1000 from 200 to work around these kind of issues. 
It looks like we would be requiring high cache limit with growing customer base.

summary: Midgress SSL handshake failures due to spike
description:
Hello GPO

2 Parent IPs caused a lot of midgress errors 
ERR_FWD_SSL_HANDSHAKE due to 104 - ECONNRESET: Connection reset by peer

Etherpad line#218: [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10828562]
203.66.199.177
203.66.199.219
 
While cpu/flits was low in cache.log NOCC indicated the machines were self-suspending because of which the ECONNRESETs happened.

NOCC T3  investigated and concluded the following:

 
{panel:title=NOCC#B-V-6B7RUKF}
The parent region ghosts  -203.66.199.177 & 203.66.199.219 noticed surge of traffic at 14:05 UTC Jan 17th for account - Fubon Multimedia Technology Co., Ltd , all related hostnames-  [app.momoshop.com.tw|https://app.momoshop.com.tw/] , m.momoshop.com.tw, [www.momoshop.com.tw|http://www.momoshop.com.tw/] 
 
This surge caused machines to autosuspend due to high flit traffic caused by serial 1855 - Fubon * 
[https://mapper.akamai.com/short/UzwdSbERuB]  - serail 1855 load ( MR 19959)  * high load on Ghost - 203.66.199.219 and 203.66.199.177
 * Both Ghosts was suspended and not usable  during 14:05:39 - 14:06:03 

 Correlates exactly with the failed log noticed on edge machine  ^[2]^
Traffic spike for account Fubon was noticed on multiple regions at the same time -   [http://grafana-hats2-epremo.akamai.com/goto/CryWRnNvR?orgId=1] 
 
 At 14:18 UTC the traffic spike was associated with LY corp requests which affected ghost 203.66.199.219 only, was suspended and unusable - 14:19:03 - 14:21:03  * 
serail 555 - LY corp ^[3]^ traffic spike - [http://grafana-hats2-epremo.akamai.com/goto/DS3lg7NDR?orgId=1]

 
 The 104 - ECONNRESET noticed on parent ghost ip were due to high load, caused from spiky customer traffic, high flit usage and auto suspension, which resulted in SSL Handshake failure at the edge ghosts. 

Request to work with ERE and PSG to determine the best way forward for spiky customer traffic (Futon). 
We had advised similarly in the last case Jan 5 ([https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi?issue_id=B-V-68Q0BTV]) for the same spiky traffic of customer.
{panel}
Account = Fubon Multimedia Technology Co., Ltd-W-P15AEB

CPcodes and hostname
1622158 — [apisearch.momoshop.com.tw|https://apisearch.momoshop.com.tw/]
1618371 — [app.momoshop.com.tw|https://app.momoshop.com.tw/]
1622160 — [cart.momoshop.com.tw|https://cart.momoshop.com.tw/]
1622154 — [m.momoshop.com.tw|https://m.momoshop.com.tw/]
1622150 — [www.momoshop.com.tw|https://www.momoshop.com.tw/]

URLs:
[https://cart.momoshop.com.tw/api/shoppingcart/modify/addGoods]

[https://cart.momoshop.com.tw/api/shoppingcart/modify/addGoods]

[https://apisearch.momoshop.com.tw/momoSearchCloud/moec/textSearch]

This is the second occurence of the issue - Jan 17th. Initially reported on Jan 5th.

Q: Is there anything we can do spread the spiky traffic to prevent this issue from recurring again.  

Thanks
executive_summary: Spiky traffic load caused self-suspension on parents and midgress SSL errors
resolution_description: in discussions with customer on what is possible
root_cause: LY Corp flit increase due to security enablement of AAP
creator: Rahul Sawarkar
mitigation_plan: Discussing with IAT on what can be done to lower flits to cause less issues.
longterm_mitigation: thread opened on discussing what should be done generally on this kind of enablement.

summary: {F-CS-10871108} - Expedia consuming majority of ChinaCDN ESSL flits
description:
Expedia has the highest flit traffic on any account in ESSL China.
 * Property:  ARL_api.epsapi.cn.775159.xml
 * Edge hostname (provided): api.epsapi.cn-v1.edgekey.net
 * Edge hostname in China (target): api.epsapi.cn-v1.edgekey88.net
 *  

Traffic Data on largest endpoint by bandwidth - [api.epsapi.cn|https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://api.epsapi.cn/__;!!GjvTz_vk!TICb50hBYP9RQASeEG4FkhsRCsXttChfvG6q0AfqQrGazP4VYYpvOLhQYpHQwh9zrexXBMcyT2_mNqjqgw$]: 
 * *≈ 4.96 PB* of traffic in the Past 30 days
 * *≈ 421 Billion* hits for the same period
 * *≈ 5%* AVG Offload ({*}≈ 419 Billion{*} Hits Reaching Origin)
 * *≈ 99.87 %* HTTP 200 OK (High Availability)
 * Peak of *≈190K RPS* ({*}≈ 20.72 GB/s{*} @ midgress)
 * Currently *≈50%* of ESSL China's Flit Capacity

See More: [https://control.akamai.com/apps/reports/#/predefined/traffic-by-hostname-2?cpcodes=1291836&hostnames=api.epsapi.cn&dateRange=last30Days&timezone=Greenwich]

Of all the traffic [api.epsapi.cn|https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://api.epsapi.cn/__;!!GjvTz_vk!TICb50hBYP9RQASeEG4FkhsRCsXttChfvG6q0AfqQrGazP4VYYpvOLhQYpHQwh9zrexXBMcyT2_mNqjqgw$] delivers today, *≈97%* it is all requests to * \{*}[https://api.epsapi.cn/v3/properties/availability] ({*}≈ 4.04 PB *of EDGE Bytes, or* ≈ 406 Billion Edge Hits\{*}). This is an API call for property availability and is used by Expedia’s partnered sites in the region. 

 

===

The account team wants assistance from a platform perspective to see if there's any way to help alleviate this flit utilization without compromising performance first.  IAT is already taking action reducing security mechanisms to reduce flits, and potentially separating the config from features that are resource-heavy.

We could move some some of their traffic to a lower priority map or split some of it out of China, but that would definitely impact perf for them. Let us know if you can think of anything else that might help while they get ready to migrate  off of ChinaCDN to Tencent Cloud. Thanks

This was prompted by SI-00023426 since ChinaCDN capacity is pretty thin across the board right now.

root_cause: The flit cost analysis have to be run by PS and ERE can be contacted if needed any help on evaluating specific feature settings.
creator: Javier Padilla
mitigation_plan: NA
longterm_mitigation: NA

summary: {F-CS-10841325} Roblox: Sudden spike in traffic in region 48891
description:
Customer reported connection issues during ~15min on Jan 17th.

 

From logs we can see most of the issues are related to region 48891.  The region got flash traffic when the issue happened.
[https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=start1768651200end1768693740&region=48891&network=A&maprule=&display=maprule&ymax=&utcoffset=5]

 Roblox itself was the top traffic carrier on this region. Traffic peaked suddenly from 40 GBPS to 150 GBPS.

[https://www.nocc.akamai.com/miniurl/?id=294ad] :

 Roblox traffic kept climbing when it peaked around 20:00 UTC.

[https://www.nocc.akamai.com/miniurl/?id=294b0]

 

Region 48891 shows several machines with high cpu

[https://mapper.akamai.com/dnsp/#from%3D1768673940000%26to%3D1768760340000%26dataFrom%3D1768679818000%26useGMTTime%3Dtrue%26timeRange%3D%7B%22min%22%3A1768676397867.9514%2C%22max%22%3A1768681798231.4387%7D%26regionId%3D48891%26service%3DW%26]

!image-2026-01-29-16-58-13-461.png|width=955,height=353!

 

Reaching capacity

https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=start1768677120end1768680720®ion=48891&network=A&maprule=&display=region&ymax=&utcoffset=5

!image-2026-01-29-17-09-44-276.png|width=327,height=447!

 

Now, if we have had suspended the machine or the maprule during the issue we probably just move the issue to a new region.  Should be worth to pre-spread this config?  or cap this region?

 
{code:java}
[croquequ@lsg-gss3 /home/msilesva/roblox]$ zcat CP1780529-202601171952-23.33.25.197-141-1769201253.gz | awk '$2=="S" {print $58}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head
3351240 915.dscd
   4165 4329.dscd
    278 5931.dscd
    273 5399.mch-ff-lo.akasrg.akamai.com
    267 4298.dscd
    265 5939.dscd
    263 5576.dscd
    261 4275.dscd
    260 4152.dscd
    258 6055.dscd {code}
 

 

 

Logs are located here

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10841325]

 

According with the customer 

_one of Roblox's top developers has been having weekly events around noon PT Saturdays._

I have asked Akatec/IAT more details about this event but waiting update:
 * expected peak traffic
 * geo location of end users
 * is cacheable or not cacheable content
 * for how many Saturdays they willl have this event
executive_summary:
Event Traffic seem to be highly concentrated causing overloads across highly serving a few machines/regions. One of such regions is r48991. For now, reduced the FOM and recommended to check with IAT on the traffic pattern. This have to be served via API-X kind of products if this is the expected behavior.
 ---------
02/12
----------
Blocker:
Waiting for further analysis by Akatech on the last Saturday event with reduced FOM on the region.
resolution_description: Given this is self-inflicting, reduced FOM on one region where issue reported for now. So, required to understand the traffic pattern and content being served for recommending any optimization on long-term solution.
root_cause: Concentrated Event traffic spike by Roblox causing self-inflicting issues with machine overloads.
creator: Cesar Roque Quiros
mitigation_plan: Given this is self-inflicting, reduced FOM on one region where issue reported for now.
longterm_mitigation: Required to understand the traffic pattern and content being served for recommending any optimization on long-term solution, such as considering API product

summary: {F-CS-10868490} - Errors & Stalled threads with regions reaching 100% Flit
description:
Property: ARL_com.prod-api.bitunix.com.1295682.xml

The customer reported seeing 500 errors, these are the result of wall timeouts when going forward to the EW service provider "ERR_EDGEWORKER|RP_Error".

The timeouts always happen between the edge<->Parent of the subrequest, and always towards these 2 regions:



36438 (NTTDATA-TYO2v) FF (DNS:RX)

36422 (NTTDATA-TYO2c) FF (DNS:RX)


The errors are not spiky, they're intermittent and spread throughout. I can see the regions are frequently hitting 100%+ flit utilization. Also  seeing lots of stalled threads messages.

creator: Javier Padilla

summary: {F-CS-10791504} - If-none-match requests to forward server when the object is not in cache.
description:
Account : HT Digital Streams Ltd

Cpcode: 1739400

Hostname: www.livehindustan.com



For a customer reported slowness issue, we noticed that lot of requests were going forward to parent servers or origin for the cacheable requests. 

Investigation showed that most of the googlebot requests were IMS,INM requests and 304 served back to client. 

We see most of the 304 were received from forward servers and not served from edge. 

In case the object was not in the cache and the client request had If-None-Match and If-Modified-Since header, Akamai edge was going forward with  If-None-Match request. 

Sample:

 {code}
 

By default, when IMS request is received at the edge and if the object is not in cache, a full GET request is made to forward server. However, for a  If-None-Match  this is not the cache. 

Sample curl and outputs: 
[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/hindustan_ims_inm] 

The behavior is seen when 'Validate Etag' behavior os enabled.  

We are able to replciate the behavior as well. 
Sample internal URL to test on staging: 
[https://www.bdp9.in/assets/public/images/products/green_smoothie.jpg?123] 
{code:java}
curl -k -S -s -D - -o /dev/null -H "Pragma: akamai-x-get-cache-key, akamai-x-cache-on, akamai-x-cache-remote-on, akamai-x-get-true-cache-key, akamai-x-check-cacheable, akamai-x-get-request-id, akamai-x-serial-no, akamai-x-get-ssl-client-session-id, X-Akamai-CacheTrack, akamai-x-get-client-ip, akamai-x-feo-trace, akamai-x-tapioca-trace " -H "If-Modified-Since: Mon, 02 Feb 2026 12:36:02 GMT" -H "If-None-Match: W/"3e26-19b8d796d80"" -H "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0-Akamai-Test-1770038839" -H "Accept: */*" -H "Accept-Encoding: gzip" --connect-timeout 6 -m 10 -w "DNS_Lookup_time : %{time_namelookup} TCP_Connection_time : %{time_connect} SSL_Connection_time : %{time_appconnect} TTFB : %{time_starttransfer} Total_time : %{time_total}" --http1.1 --http1.1 --connect-to ::a1.g.akamai-staging.net "https://www.bdp9.in/assets/public/images/products/green_smoothie.jpg?444" {code}
Sample Customer URL: 

[https://www.livehindustan.com/lh-img/smart/img/2026/01/28/640x360/Road_pic_1758432157176_1758432165570_1769559825806.jpg] 

Is this the expected behavior? 

Shouldn't the behavior of edge be same for IMS and INM request when the object is not in cache? 

The behavior led to receiving only 304 responses from forward server and the full object was not requested from the edge. Leading lot of forward requests and slowness.
creator: Sandeep Bhat

summary: CBS - Ghost attempts multiple ICP requests instead of configued failover
description:
Customer: CBS Interactive
Timeframe: Sporadic spikes between 1/31 23:00 - 02:01 05:00
Impact: Moderate spikes in edge 504 responses
Risk: While the spikes are mild it is a risk to traffic share as the customer has shown a tendency to remove Akamai from the CDN rotation when Akamai has more http errors than other CDN vendors
Etherpad: [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10890881]

 

Following up on some recent concerns regarding CBS Paramount +. We have solved the bulk of the issues with failover but still continue to see some unwanted errors that are noticed by the customer. The customer is monitoring Akamai performance via DS2 logs has been especially weary of continued moderate spikes in edge 504 errors that occur during their high traffic events that they are reporting other CDNs don't experience. What we observed this past weekend (the customer had a UFC event that saw heavy traffic between 01/31 23:00 - 02/01 05:00) was that these 504 edge errors are r lines that are a result of multiple failover attempts to ICP peers. 

*A few examples:*
{code:java}
[mshea@lsg-gss12 /home/shrshuk]$ zcat CP1923945-202602010300-23.33.25.197-537-1769916534.gz | awk '$2=="r" && !($16~/[ftGKPX]/) && !($11~/^(10\.|127\.0\.0\.1)/) && $14==504 {print $16}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn
    617 igHV
      1 mgHV
      1 gHV
[mshea@lsg-gss12 /home/shrshuk]$ zcat CP1923945-202602010415-23.33.25.197-521-1769922427.gz | awk '$2=="r" && !($16~/[ftGKPX]/) && !($11~/^(10\.|127\.0\.0\.1)/) && $14==504 {print $16}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn
    254 igHV 
{code}
+*The issue -*+ after walking through our findings with PS their stance was that edge making multiple ICP forward request in lieu of the configured failover tree (td0 primary, td-alt, bypass cloud wrapper direct to origin) is not expected behavior and that ICP would be disabled in a failover scenario. The concern is that ghost is not following the metadata and is making multiple requests to ICP peers which are failing for similar reasons and then returning a 504. 

Example (starting on line 1001 of the etherpad above)

Edge first requests from ICP peer 23.202.158.17 and logs a forward 503 error. This is because the peer had gone forward to 23.207.198.55 (r37045) via td0 (mch-ff-lo) where ghost was suspended and logged an SSL handshake error. Then it logs a 111 First Byte Timeout prompting failover as expected

On the forward side of that final peer request (23.202.158.15) we actually do see the server bypass cloud wrapper and make a request direct to the origin which is successful. The r line epoch has a 3.5 delta from the f line, and logs a 200 ERR_SSL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT.
{code:java}

23.202.158.15 f 1769907389.385 5 6 0 2 3 81920 82326 146.75.29.188 10.202.158.12 GET /L/618/1920493/30d/prope3db2hh9-akamai.airspace-origin.cbsivideo.com/stream_3/manifest_7_3509.ts?m=1769622335&CMCD=bl%3D18100%2Cbr%3D7291%2Cd%3D6006%2Cmtp%3D142200%2Cot%3Dav%2Csf%3Dh%2Csid%3D%22a2b61289-42a1-477e-8638-625f980eb42c%22%2Ctb%3D8941 200 video/MP2T - TLSv1.3 oGfL 0 - - f2fbc1b6 prope3db2hh9-akamai.airspace-origin.cbsivideo.com 1216 1216 3408 /L/618/1920493/30d/prope3db2hh9-akamai.airspace-origin.cbsivideo.com/stream_3/manifest_7_3509.ts?m=1769622335 7816b4b7.8b467c8b - - 1740 3364 - - - - - 0 389 - - r7f163ba290d0.-79400847.n.g - - 5568 33c0c880 - 0 0 8 e97ea4b9.d843d prope3db2hh9-akamai.airspace-origin.cbsivideo.com - mid=1000;fcv=||pct|0:Ze4JeeaCCrn7YSgXO5mNydABmkw=;mtse=g|4215566449;aid=1287287;hnd=1;m_slv=TLSv1.3;pin=JLav|amd;ssl=3001301::::;aidv=19;netp=;ccl=k;fp=848;rmap=dscw42;mle=37512;vcd=4603 - - 26939 - L1:L1::: - - prope3db2hh9.airspace-cdn.cbsivideo.com{code}
The reason for that ERR_SSL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT on the client side edge looks to be that 23.202.158.12 never receives a TCP response from 23.202.158.15
{code:java}
testgrp@23.202.158.12:~$ /a/sbin/arf -d 1769907385 /a/logs/cache.log | xargs zcat | grep '7816b4b7\|8b467c8b'
1769907389.388|7ebfc9bca880|    7ebac8993b10|8b467c8b| 15| 1|U|10550| TCP transaction to 10.202.158.15/80 failed
1769907389.388|7ebfc9bca880|    7ebac8993b10|8b467c8b| 15| 1|U|10551| Detected DEAD Sibling: 10.202.158.15/80/2002
1769907389.388|7ebfc9bca880|    7ebac8993b10|8b467c8b|119| 1|C| 7329| httpTimeout(): FWD-FAIL(8b467c8b): timed out before receiving response headers{code}
Looking at the region health I can see that flit levels are expectedly high, and there is a lot of choppy load that looks to be brought on by spiky traffic from other edge maps ([here it is MR332 - b.akamai.net|https://map.akamai.com/cgi-bin/FreeFlow/Analysis/HDB_Server.pl?startmonth=2&startday=1&startyear=2026&starthour=0&startmin=0&endmonth=2&endday=1&endyear=2026&endhour=5&endmin=0¶m1=43981&dbname=production¶m2=20¶m3=¶m4=&display=graph&normalize=none&ptdisp=normal&yaxis=y-axis&size=large&scope=all&query_name=REGION_BY_MAPRULE_FLYTELOAD&customSql=). However, I don't see servers going over flit cap that especially aligns with these errors, and we have not seen this behavior isolated to any one particular region.
{code:java}
Cache logs for r43981:FABRIC-IAD34c

[mshea@lsg-gss6 ~]$ zcat cache-GIP23.202.158.10-202602010050-23.33.25.197-74-1770047963.gz | grep "CPU: all" | grep flit | awk 'length($25)>4 && $1~/23.202.158.25|23.202.158.15|23.202.158.12/ {split($2, a, "|"); print strftime("%c", a[1]), "- server: "$1, "- flit%: "$25}' | sort | uniq | column -s "-" -t
Sun Feb  1 00:53:24 2026    server: 23.202.158.12    flit%: 100%,
Sun Feb  1 00:53:25 2026    server: 23.202.158.12    flit%: 100%,
Sun Feb  1 00:53:28 2026    server: 23.202.158.12    flit%: 111%,
Sun Feb  1 00:53:29 2026    server: 23.202.158.12    flit%: 103%,
Sun Feb  1 00:53:30 2026    server: 23.202.158.12    flit%: 103%,
Sun Feb  1 00:54:29 2026    server: 23.202.158.25    flit%: 100%,
Sun Feb  1 00:58:16 2026    server: 23.202.158.12    flit%: 100%,
Sun Feb  1 00:58:19 2026    server: 23.202.158.12    flit%: 102%,
Sun Feb  1 00:58:20 2026    server: 23.202.158.12    flit%: 101%,
Sun Feb  1 00:59:54 2026    server: 23.202.158.25    flit%: 101%,
Sun Feb  1 00:59:56 2026    server: 23.202.158.25    flit%: 102%,
Sun Feb  1 00:59:58 2026    server: 23.202.158.25    flit%: 102%,{code}
!image-2026-02-02-16-51-13-659.png|width=1373,height=541!

Worth noting as well that a driver of a number of these spikes in forward errors appeared to be MR335 (z.akamai.net) contributing sudden spiky flitload to regions on the mch-ff-lo maps which prompts these failover scenarios in short sporadic bursts. We've outlined the timeline of such during the event here: [https://docs.google.com/document/d/1V-DT4C_RL2gtaTObPb766lTe7DqyIfIGpupNkUSeCU8/edit?usp=sharing]
executive_summary: The oddity here is that, despite failover being triggered, edge servers are still making ICP peer requests, which is not expected per standard metadata logic. We should try disabling ICP globally (not tied to tier match) to ensure that peer queries do not interfere with failover logic.
root_cause: The config has a custom override metadata is designed to control failover behavior across multiple tiers (TD0, CW, Origin) and to explicitly manage ICP status during each failover step network:icp.statusoff</network:icp.status> at each fail-action step (TD0 failover, CW failover, Origin failover)
creator: Mike Shea
mitigation_plan: Disable ICP with TIER0 match then you are going to disable it at the TD0 TIER not the edge tier
longterm_mitigation: Disable ICP with TIER0 match then you are going to disable it at the TD0 TIER not the edge tier

summary: {F-CS-10827508} NBC:   Region overloaded during NFL game, Jan 18.
description:
Customer reported Higher VPF and CIRR observed during the Rams vs Bears Event on Jan 18 from 23:57 to Jan 19 00:02 GMT

Akatec extracted the logs and found region 48025 having issues. 

NOCC was engaged:
{quote}We investigated this case and had it reviewed by tier 3 and below were our findings.

We can see region was overloaded. High flits, sflag 2048, high AVG manager cycle.

On checking the mapper, we can see the machines in the region were all suspended ~23:58:48

[https://mapper.akamai.com/short/H8fOn6clzL]

Same can be confirmed in the logs:


{code:java}
23.52.15.16 2026/01/18-23:00:30+0000|7e53abd82880| 7e52e7fd8d10| 0| 80| 1|U|16212| suspendCheckCB: icpStartSuspendTime start 2026/01/18-23:00:30+0000, gSuspendFlag: 2048
23.52.15.16 2026/01/18-23:35:31+0000|7e53abd82880| 7e52e7fd8d10| 0| 80| 1|U|16212| suspendCheckCB: icpStartSuspendTime start 2026/01/18-23:35:31+0000, gSuspendFlag: 2048
23.52.15.16 2026/01/18-23:48:01+0000|7e53abd82880| 7e52e7fd8d10| 0| 80| 1|U|16212| suspendCheckCB: icpStartSuspendTime start 2026/01/18-23:48:01+0000, gSuspendFlag: 2048
23.52.15.16 2026/01/18-23:59:02+0000|7e53abd82880| 7e52e7fd8d10| 0| 80| 1|U|16212| suspendCheckCB: icpStartSuspendTime start 2026/01/18-23:59:02+0000, gSuspendFlag: 2048
23.52.15.16 2026/01/19-00:09:46+0000|7e53abd82880| 7e52e7fd8d10| 0| 80| 1|U|16212| suspendCheckCB: icpStartSuspendTime start 2026/01/19-00:09:46+0000, gSuspendFlag: 2048
23.52.15.16 2026/01/19-00:22:04+0000|7e53abd82880| 7e52e7fd8d10| 0| 80| 1|U|16212| suspendCheckCB: icpStartSuspendTime start 2026/01/19-00:22:04+0000, gSuspendFlag: 2048
23.52.15.16 2026/01/19-00:31:11+0000|7e53abd82880| 7e52e7fd8d10| 0| 80| 1|U|16212| suspendCheckCB: icpStartSuspendTime start 2026/01/19-00:31:11+0000, gSuspendFlag: 2048
23.52.15.16 2026/01/19-00:31:36+0000|7e53abd82880| 7e52e7fd8d10| 0| 80| 1|U|16212| suspendCheckCB: icpStartSuspendTime start 2026/01/19-00:31:36+0000, gSuspendFlag: 2048
23.52.15.16 2026/01/19-00:41:42+0000|7e53abd82880| 7e52e7fd8d10| 0| 80| 1|U|16212| suspendCheckCB: icpStartSuspendTime start 2026/01/19-00:41:42+0000, gSuspendFlag: 2048
23.52.15.16 2026/01/19-00:51:01+0000|7e53abd82880| 7e52e7fd8d10| 0| 80| 1|U|16212| suspendCheckCB: icpStartSuspendTime start 2026/01/19-00:51:01+0000, gSuspendFlag: 2048{code}


This is region is overloading due to spike in [non-sticky|https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/search.cgi?search=non-sticky] load for the serial 1963 on all the machines in the region which caused self-suspensions!

It's due to spike in [non-sticky|https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/search.cgi?search=non-sticky] load for the serial 1963

We can see spike in [non-sticky|https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/search.cgi?search=non-sticky] load during the same timeframe -

[https://mapper.akamai.com/short/z9CCMzwv3Y]

At this time, there are no specific preventive measures documented to mitigate or avoid sudden spikes of serials or maprules at the regional level. In the event of a spike that overloads a server, the affected server will automatically [self-suspend|https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/search.cgi?search=self-suspend], allowing traffic to be diverted to other healthy servers within the same region or to the best available alternate region.

Regarding region 48025, it is equipped with the latest hardware and operates within a safe FOM value of 0.58. During the referenced timeframe, a sudden surge in traffic overloaded the server, which appears to have been an intermittent occurrence. If this behaviour recurs frequently, we recommend escalating the issue to ERE through PSG for further investigation.
{quote}
 

Customer/IAT  is worry because this config will be used for SuperBowl traffic, is there anything we can recommend to avoid this behavior in the future?

 

Logs and investigation is here

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10827508]
executive_summary: - Region overloaded during NFL game
root_cause: Single region overload by a different customer.
creator: Cesar Roque Quiros
mitigation_plan: NA
longterm_mitigation: Suggested optimizations for problematic customer.

summary: {F-CS-10898300} AMZ: 503 because SR assignment failed between MCH and CW
description:
Amazon has reported this issue in three different cases now.  Basically, Prime Video in Europe is seeing an increase in failures to acquire VOD origin manifests (primarily DASH) via SMS -> Akamai -> S3

The last report from Amazon was yesterday (Feb 2 at 20:30 UTC):
{quote}_Hi team, we are seeing increased cdn fetching errors from our SMS service in FRA (eu-central-1) region starting at 12:30 PST_
{quote}

Same spike can be noted in grafana

https://grafana.akam.ai/org224/dashboard/db/customer-dashboard-lighthouse?from=1770043434389&to=1770076902460&var-breakdown=customer&var-myview=Amazon%20ROW%20DASH%20EU%20Manifest&var-agg=5&var-percentile=50

!image-2026-02-03-23-03-03-113.png|width=575,height=269!

 

Akatec extracted the logs and the problem is related to the error 

 ERR_NO_FWD_HOSTS fwdStartFail

The problem is between MCH and CW, for example:

!image-2026-02-03-22-39-47-126.png|width=988,height=113!

Edge goes to MCH, MCH fails to resolve CW map and then the Edge goes directly to CW.

 

Comparing to a request without issues:

 

!image-2026-02-03-22-49-18-560.png|width=999,height=86!

Edge==MCH==CW==Origin

 

From the logs, we can see a good number of MCH regions in Italy,  France, and Germay with this problem

summary: Unable to delete Delivery Policy.
description:
Customer has deleted their certificates (Slot 314606) and the associated edge hostname ([dvinci-products-demo-ipv4-eu.edgekey.net|https://dvinci-products-demo-ipv4-eu.edgekey.net/]), which caused the delivery policy status to move to Failed. There was no active traffic on this.

The customer now wants to delete the delivery policy but is unable to do so, as the Delete option is grayed out. We reviewed the tech docs, which mention that only delivery policy assignments with status Active or Delete Failed can be deleted.

The customer attempted to reactivate the policy, but the status continues to remain Failed.

From audit trail we can see that there is a difference in EHN between "Audit Trail - Delivery Policy Assignment Details - Edge Hostnames" and "Delivery Policy Assignments - Edge Hostnames" before Jan 28th.

Upon checking now we see that customer has deleted the hostname as well which was associated with the delivery policy.

The delete option remains greyed out.

Ask: Currently customer just wants to delete the policy, but we are unable to do so.
executive_summary:
Delivery Policy deletion was failing at the EHN_POLICY_REMOVE step because customers had already deleted the associated Edge Hostnames. When the workflow attempted to detach the policy, HAPI returned 404 – ehn-not-found, which was treated as a fatal error. This caused the policy to remain in Failed state and the Delete option to be greyed out.

A coordinated workaround (UI retrigger + DBMC change) was used temporarily. A permanent fix under GDPM-718 has now been deployed in DEX-DPM-26.1.2, where the removal step treats 404 as a successful, idempotent operation, preventing this issue going forward.
resolution_description:
For the permanent fix : The code-fix approach will going to be implement and planning to push it to prod around mid/late next week

Changes done by the code fix: When DPM tries to remove policy from EHN, when it calls HAPI to remove it, HAPI is returning ehn-not-found error.. so by doing the changes it will going to handle by the process , and it make the EHN_REMOVE_STEP  successful in case EHN is missing in HAPI..
root_cause: Based on orchestrator and Pulsar logs, the delete operation is failing during the EHN_POLICY_REMOVE step. The delivery policy removal workflow attempts to detach the policy from its associated Edge Hostnames; however, one or more of these Edge Hostnames (dvinci-products-demo-ipv4-eu.edgekey.net, dvinci-products-demo-ipv4-eu.edgesuite.net) have already been deleted by the customer. As a result, HAPI returns a 404 – Edge Hostname Not Found error while fetching the EHN details. Due to this inconsistency, the EHN_POLICY_REMOVE step fails repeatedly, leaving the Delivery Policy in a Failed state and causing the Delete option to remain greyed out in the UI. This indicates a stale reference or data consistency issue, where the Delivery Policy still retains references to Edge Hostnames that no longer exist.
creator: Konrad Domanski
mitigation_plan: Changes done by the code fix: When DPM tries to remove policy from EHN, when it calls HAPI to remove it, HAPI is returning ehn-not-found error.. so by doing the changes it will going to handle by the process , and it make the EHN_REMOVE_STEP  successful in case EHN is missing in HAPI.
longterm_mitigation: The code-fix approach will going to be implement and planning to push it to prod around mid/late next week

summary: {F-CS-10860301} Thron:  POC requests missing outgoing header
description:
Property: thron-static-test
Test URL : [https://test-cdn.thron.com/api/v1/content-delivery/shares/1d9o3f/contents/445a1608-606c-4cf6-ba28-6b88b5ca4ede/video/source/video?maxH=1920]

Rule name: Cache-ID on Videos

Behavior logic:
Original variable value : H264
Normalize User-Agent → Accept-Video header
value: AV1

Observed Behavior :

Initial client request (Chrome):
Accept-Video: AV1 applied correctly
First Range request (bytes=0-0):
Accept-Video: AV1
Origin responds with 206 Partial Content
Content-Range total size corresponds to AV1 asset

Subsequent internal Range requests:
Range: bytes=0-2097151, bytes=2097152-4194303
Accept-Video header reverts to H264
Origin returns H264 asset, resulting in format switch mid-delivery

 

This was implemented in May 2025, no issue found during implementation but they found this behavior recently. 

 

We were able to replicate this behavior in Staging and collected ghost and header logs
{code:java}

accept-video: H264
* FRes Mau 18920a 1770143991 23.50.53.237 206 /api/v1/content-delivery/shares/1d9o3f/contents/445a1608-606c-4cf6-ba28-6b88b5ca4ede/video/source/video?maxH=1920
X-True-Cache-Key: /L/test-cdn.thron.com/api/v1/content-delivery/shares/1d9o3f/contents/445a1608-606c-4cf6-ba28-6b88b5ca4ede/video/source/video vcd=10847 cid=maxH=1920///PMUSER_ACCEPT_VIDEO=AV1
* CReq Maa 18a747 1770147144 23.50.53.236 - /L/267/954214/15m/test-cdn.thron.com/api/v1/content-delivery/shares/1d9o3f/contents/445a1608-606c-4cf6-ba28-6b88b5ca4ede/video/source/video?maxH=1920
GET /api/v1/content-delivery/shares/1d9o3f/contents/445a1608-606c-4cf6-ba28-6b88b5ca4ede/video/source/video?maxH=1920 HTTP/1.1
accept-video: H264 {code}
All data is collected here

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10860301] line 619.

 

There is an old Jira with a very similar description

[https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOSTWEB-657?jql=text%20~%20%22device-profile.allow-for-poc-reqs%22]

However to enable this tag we will need Eng Approval.

 

Could you please review this and let us know if the best solution is to use the tag 'device-profile.allow-for-poc-reqs'?
executive_summary:
- POC requests missing outgoing header 

I can see the tag discussed here https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOSTWEB-657 is only available in IM pearl file, so the engineering tag approval is of no consequence here, Here the issue was addressed in metadata by adding the headers whose values are derived from EDC to the incoming request.
Recommended to add the header "Used-Edc-to-determine-Accept-Video: 1" to the incoming request. 
The customer tested the above provided workaround/configuration and it seems to work well!
root_cause:
The problem arises from the custom metadata implementation, where "<edgeservices:modify-outgoing-request.add-header>" manipulation was not applied to POC fragment requests. Here the issue was addressed in metadata by adding the headers whose values are derived from EDC to the incoming request.
Recommended to add the header "Used-Edc-to-determine-Accept-Video: 1" to the incoming request.
creator: Cesar Roque Quiros
mitigation_plan: Recommended to add the header "Used-Edc-to-determine-Accept-Video: 1" to the incoming request.
longterm_mitigation: Recommended to add the header "Used-Edc-to-determine-Accept-Video: 1" to the incoming request.

summary: OOC Edge using same network path on Primary TD and Backup TD map
description:
During the WPL FINAL game -  Capitals vs Royal challengers match today we observed a spike in edge 5xx for hits mapped OOC at ~15:22 UTC.

In these instances we can see FA kicked in, however the forward request on both primary (chc1) and backup (chb4) map resulted in a CONNECT_TIMEOUT. 
Edge (r45208:SDN-SINj) --> Td Primary chc1 r43829:TATA-HYDn --> ERR_CONNECT_TIMEOUT

Edge (r45208:SDN-SINj) FA Request --> TD Backup chb4 r43336:TATA-BLR2z --> ERR_CONNECT_TIMEOUT



In the etherpad between line 26 - 70 we have traces which show that the route on primary and backup are taking the same path. 

Ask to ERE:
We are seeking guidance on how we can further diversify the path on the primary and backup maps in order to reduce the impact for similar occurrences in the future. 

Etherpad
[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10913757]

Sample Log Trace line 18-21
Traceroutes line 26 - 70
resolution_description: Since international links would be highly limited b/w two countries, it is required to ensure their health beforehand and transient issues aren't completely preventable.
root_cause: Transient network issue in the international link b/w SIN and IN.
creator: Tyler Deary
mitigation_plan: Setting up lattice jobs to understand which hop in the path has the issue and try to fix it.
longterm_mitigation: Since international links would be highly limited b/w two countries, it is required to ensure their health beforehand and transient issues aren't completely preventable.

summary: Ghost doesn't respect read or client timeouts
description:
h3. Background

We have a property, ARL_alerting-prod-databattery.aclp.linode.com.1153888.xml (https://code.engr.akamai.com/cgi-bin/show.pl?file=/production/arl.data/ARL_alerting-prod-databattery.aclp.linode.com.1153888.xml&anchor=) Version 19 (production), which uses best-practice code for DataBattery G2O connections.
The advanced override metadata of interest is shown below:
{code:xml}
<network:pconn.forward>
    <status>on</status>
    <timeout>15s</timeout>
</network:pconn.forward>


<!-- Failover Action -->
<forward:availability.retry-post.status>on</forward:availability.retry-post.status>
<forward:availability.retry-post.only-on-req-abort>off</forward:availability.retry-post.only-on-req-abort>
<match:request.type value="EDGE PARENT">
<match:fail-action2-attempt value="0">
    <match:response.status value="000 500:504">
        <forward:availability.fail-action2>
            <apply-on-all-hops>on</apply-on-all-hops>
            <request-host>%(AK_HOSTHEADER)</request-host>
            <path>%(AK_URL)</path>
            <status>on</status>
            <type>serve-alternate</type>
            <max-attempts>1</max-attempts>
        </forward:availability.fail-action2>
    </match:response.status>
</match:fail-action2-attempt>
</match:request.type>

<!-- attempt 2 -->
<match:fail-action2-attempt value="1">
    <forward:origin-server>
        <dns-name>
            <value>live-fallback-prod2.dbattery.akamai.com</value>
            <status>on</status>
        </dns-name>
    </forward:origin-server>
</match:fail-action2-attempt>
<match:forward.server-type value="CUSTOMER_ORIGIN">
    <network:http.first-byte-timeout>10s</network:http.first-byte-timeout>
    <network:http.client-timeout>10s</network:http.client-timeout>
    <network:http.read>10s</network:http.read>
    <network:http.write>15s</network:http.write>
</match:forward.server-type>

<!-- This contains metadata recommended by the DataBattery team on https://collaborate.akamai.com/confluence/display/DataBattery/Connecting+to+DataBattery+via+Ghost#. -->

<cache:honor-expires>on</cache:honor-expires>
<cache:header-update.allow>on</cache:header-update.allow>
<edgeservices:modify-incoming-response.remove-header>
    <name>Vary</name>
    <status>on</status>
</edgeservices:modify-incoming-response.remove-header>
<cache:validate-etag>on</cache:validate-etag>
<forward:origin-server.port>443</forward:origin-server.port>
<forward:rate-limit.status>on</forward:rate-limit.status>
<forward:rate-limit.max-concurrent-connects>300</forward:rate-limit.max-concurrent-connects>
{code}

This represents the latest configured timeout settings. Some of these values are questionable, as certain settings are implicitly overridden by others. For example, if <network:http.first-byte-timeout> is not defined (so default 0s), <network:http.read> is used instead. However, this behavior was not observed in practice.

To remove any ambiguity, I switched to explicitly defined values, but that did not resolve the issue. As a next step, <network:http.client-timeout> was set in an attempt to recover from the problem.

The property does not use SiteShield or Origin ACL; it uses Tiered Distribution, therefore matching occurs on EDGE in addition to PARENT.

h3. Problem

Recently, we started receiving a large number of errors from the DataBattery origin
(https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/DBATTERY-8732).

After reviewing the logs, we observed that request turnaround times are ~30 seconds, which matches the origin-side timeout before it returns a 504. However, we would expect Ghost to close the connection after 10 seconds, as instructed by metadata. This does not happen.

The client connecting to Ghost has a TCP read timeout of 30 seconds and closes the connection before Ghost attempts a second retry. In some cases, the second attempt is successful, but there is no longer a receiver for the response. This leads to data loss, as Ghost acts on behalf of the client, which is no longer waiting for a response and therefore cannot acknowledge it.


h3. Expected Behavior

If a request takes longer than 10 seconds, the connection to the origin should be closed by Ghost, according to <network:http.client-timeout> or <network:http.read>.

The DataBattery team confirmed that they do not send headers or status before the body, so this should not be interpreted as a slow response with incremental bytes resetting the TCP read timeout. There may be some misunderstanding on our side, but the metadata guide does not seem to address this scenario.

I was also unable to confirm the behavior in Ghost source code. Additionally, the issue is not reproducible in my lab using a different origin backend, but it is easily reproducible in production with the property in question.


h3. Goal

We need a metadata tag that explicitly defines how long a connection may remain open before it is considered dead and forcibly closed. 
Or, TCP read timeout to work properly if it's what happens in this case.



h3. Logs

This issue has been occurring for several days, so I can provide many examples. Below are some of the most recent captured logs and ART links (assuming they have not expired).

ART: https://tools.gss.akamai.com/art/#0.4bce2d17.1770363435.589d23f2

summary: Latency spikes observed in WPL event for Jio Hotstar on Feb 3rd on MCDN workflow
description:
we observed error spikes and increased latency on MCDN Edge regions due to Flit/bit overload from Tik Tok maprule.

MCO observed error spikes and increased latency on MCDN Edge regions due to Flit/bit overload from Tik Tok maprule during the WPL events for Jio Hotstar on Feb 3rd for the below timeframes:
13:07 UTC || 18:37 IST  | 1527307
13:45 UTC || 19:15 IST  | 1527307, 1532911
14:01 UTC || 19:31 IST  | 1527307
Note: As a temporary fix, we are suspending Tik ok maprules MR 95 and 96 for the event duration(7hrs), this is included as a part of precheck for upcoming games as well.

For all the spikes we noticed the edge region was r39570:BHARENT-MAA32b.

Apart from the 13:45 UTC spike , we could notice high flits utilization from the cache logs.

We could also notice a drop in FLYTELOAD at the same time (Apart from the 13:45 UTC spike)

Drop in FLYTELOAD : [https://map.akamai.com/cgi-bin/FreeFlow/Analysis/HDB_Server.pl?startmonth=2&startday=3&startyear=2026&starthour=10&startmin=0&endmonth=2&endday=3&endyear=2026&endhour=15&endmin=0&param1=39570&dbname=production&param2=10&param3=&param4=&display=graph&normalize=none&ptdisp=normal&yaxis=y-axis&size=large&scope=all&query_name=DASHBOARD2&customSql=]

Logs are added to etherpad (Line 639):  [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10901701]

Ask for ERE: Could you please review this and suggest for next actions.
root_cause: Tiktok's spillover spiky traffic from NP
creator: Manjusha Nayak
mitigation_plan: Tiktok MR suspension
longterm_mitigation: Unless the capacity is increased in NP, we'll see such instances.

summary: {F-CS-10852035} -  ERR_ACCESS_DENIED|sbsd_challenge with Content protector due to brotli compression

Currently the issue is replicable by sending the request header Cookie: x-avf-enabled=true [On staging and production] 

We escalated this to SECESC https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/SECESC-29882 and they mentioned the issue is related to the brotli compression of the challenge URL [with arl  /L/39968/1889833/1d/bm_cyberfend.akamai.com/_sec/cpr/challenge in logs]

Note from the Secesc jira: 
Looks like CPR causes a fail-action to happen. And the fail-action page (_sec/cpr/challenge) is taken from somewhere (DataBattery?). So either that place should not serve brotli compressed OR the configuration for product (that applies on fail-action page) should account for the fact the page may be brotli compressed. 

 

The customer ARL has Brotli Support behavior set to ON.  

Currently the security engineering Jira is discussing options to best handle the situation for challenge URL with respect to brotli compression. 

Option being discussed: 
Set "edgeservices:lma.brotli.force-decompress-brotli-for-transforms = on"  

Set "edgeservices:lma.brotli.botf.status = on"  

Or Disable Brotli support [The brotli compression from origin]. 

When the brotli compression happens for the challenge URL, there is no transformation happening based on r70 [t2=a+|bm+|bv_n+|bv_s] is missing. 

SECESC team is requesting assistance from Brotli SME to understand best way to handle the issue.  

We can replicate the issue if there is a need to add any debug section for logging.  Test URL : [https://www.dior.com/fr_fr/] 

Could you assist and help understand best way to handle brotli compression based on the discussion in [here|https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/SECESC-29882?focusedId=12710470&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels%3Acomment-tabpanel#comment-12710470]  ?
creator: Sandeep Bhat

summary: {F-CS-10925398} PhonePe: Review increase in flits
description:
This request came as part of 

SI-00023428

https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/ERE-3153

Recently there was an SI (SI-00023428) for Phonepe, during which an internal thread with ERE was initiated to discuss the SI details. While the issue described in the SI has been resolved, the ERE team got back today with a separate observation to investigate a spike in Flit load since 5th Feb for PhonePe traffic. They requested Akatec to further investigate a sudden increase that they observed & escalate through the right channels.

 

We observed increase RET and TAT in Trafficpeak from Feb 6th. At the same time, API dashboard also showed an increase in flits for Phonepe traffic, without an increase in hits. In addition, there were increased score for mapping. This means Phonepe traffic had high flit utilization from Feb 6th for some reason

Worth to mentioned there was a change in the config but related to addition/change in hostnames & Origin server IP on 6th of Feb - *_[https://control.akamai.com/apps/property-manager/#/property/11078189?gid=310549]_*

 

We could not work with full log extraction as the file is too big, so we extract logs from the top 3 regions handling the customer cpcode based on HATS:

[https://grafana-hats1-epremo.akamai.com/d/e20b1f9b-85f2-4323-8590-ab10e9778885/hats-v3?orgId=1&var-load=yes&var-breakdown=srv_region&var-maprule=-&var-destination=user&var-cpcode=1631088&var-cache_hit=-&var-service=-&var-region_country=-&var-region_continent=-&var-region=-&var-client_country=-&var-client_continent=-&var-child=-&var-client_asnum=-&var-client_asname=-&var-client_asaccount=-&var-client_geo_id=-&var-proto=-&var-connection_type=-&var-ipv=-&var-tput=5000&var-rtt=50&var-size=500&var-topn=1-10&var-resolution=10min&var-hours=-&var-title=breakdown%3Dsrv_region%20%2F%20cpcode%3D1631088%20%2F%20destination%3Duser&var-title_no_breakdown=cpcode%3D1631088%20%2F%20destination%3Duser&from=now-7d&to=now]

!image-2026-02-09-20-46-37-157.png|width=804,height=68!

Comparing the logs from Feb 5 to Feb 6 we can see an increase of around 40% in aggregate flytes in general

The CPCode traffic,  filtering only India ,shows an  increase around Feb 6

[https://rome.akamai.com/cgi-bin/queryview/cptraffic.py?start=02%2F01%2F2026+12%3A00&end=02%2F09%2F2026+23%3A49&customers=1631088&countries=IN&limit=&minval=0&height=3&width=5&metric=bits&metric=hits&metric=flits&metric=f2b&src=essl]

!image-2026-02-09-20-58-47-320.png|width=389,height=265!

 

 

Looking mapper graphs for these regions, we can see around Feb 6 there was an increase in flits usage

[https://mapper.akamai.com/short/t1xqvQ4iop]

for example region 50161

!image-2026-02-09-20-56-51-909.png|width=576,height=343!

 

However, looking HATS for region 50161 the top cpcodes are the same, and the traffic levels are also the same

[https://grafana-hats1-epremo.akamai.com/d/e20b1f9b-85f2-4323-8590-ab10e9778885/hats-v3?orgId=1&var-load=yes&var-breakdown=cpcode&var-maprule=-&var-destination=user&var-cpcode=-&var-cache_hit=-&var-service=-&var-region_country=-&var-region_continent=-&var-region=50161&var-client_country=-&var-client_continent=-&var-child=-&var-client_asnum=-&var-client_asname=-&var-client_asaccount=-&var-client_geo_id=-&var-proto=-&var-connection_type=-&var-ipv=-&var-tput=5000&var-rtt=50&var-size=500&var-topn=1-5&var-resolution=10min&var-hours=-&var-title=breakdown%3Dcpcode%20%2F%20region%3D50161%20%2F%20destination%3Duser&var-title_no_breakdown=region%3D50161%20%2F%20destination%3Duser&from=1769781050000&to=1770249171000]

 

!image-2026-02-09-20-58-25-974.png|width=1373,height=160!

 

Using 50161 as exmpale, taking a timeframe where we see the peak of traffic in this CPCode during Feb 6,  with DNSP graphs we can see a difference in load and distribution comparing the same timeframe but during Feb 3

 

- Feb 6:

[https://mapper.akamai.com/short/fxYQ9aCFnn]

!image-2026-02-09-21-25-50-803.png|width=1026,height=549!

- Feb 3:

[https://mapper.akamai.com/short/UYU5nSbAO2]

!image-2026-02-09-21-26-49-345.png|width=1028,height=656!

 

We need your help to understand what is causing this behavior.  Let me know if you need any other data from our side
root_cause: Change in aggregation of Rate limitng policies increase the flit consumption and impact the Request pre processing (RET/TAT) signficantly.
creator: Cesar Roque Quiros
mitigation_plan: Security config changes  to "Removed rate control policies "Page View Requests & POST and PUT Requests" -  per recommedation in SECESC-30153,
longterm_mitigation: Security features to be better vetted for the flit impact.

summary: Bit Rate Limiting doesn't work as expected
description:
Customer ‘Kakao Corp.’ reported that Bit Rate Limiting does not work as configured.
The URL is [https://vsak1.play.kakao.com/s-daum_cafe/vod/rvukmhq4jst2ug6soekovb0lv/mp4/mp4/mp4_720P_2M_T1.mp4?px-time=1771747491&px-hash=9eedf3f50697784951c142c66e29fe65&px-path=/s-daum_cafe/vod/rvukmhq4jst2ug6soekovb0lv&px-bufahead=36&px-bps=4692872|https://akamai.my.salesforce.com/servlet/servlet.ExternalRedirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fvsak1.play.kakao.com%2Fs-daum_cafe%2Fvod%2Frvukmhq4jst2ug6soekovb0lv%2Fmp4%2Fmp4%2Fmp4_720P_2M_T1.mp4%3Fpx-time%3D1771747491%26px-hash%3D9eedf3f50697784951c142c66e29fe65%26px-path%3D%2Fs-daum_cafe%2Fvod%2Frvukmhq4jst2ug6soekovb0lv%26px-bufahead%3D36%26px-bps%3D4692872] and the threshold is following px-bufahead value in query string.

In above URL, px-bufahead value is 36 and it means threshold is 36s. This configuration has been set using advance meta in property [amd-vsak1.play.kakao.com|https://akamai.my.salesforce.com/servlet/servlet.ExternalRedirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Famd-vsak1.play.kakao.com].
When IAT tested this URL, throttling started to occur at 5~6 seconds and the bps continuously decreases from the beginning up to 4–5 seconds. (Etherpad line 11~23)

 

Configured an internal testing to see if Bit Rate Limiting feature is working but there is no difference between rate limiting and normal request.
