Can you please review this and explain how this feature is supposed to work?

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10893717] has more logs.
executive_summary:
The rate limiting is not working as expected. As per the log lines and the metadata trace, it seems like the behavior is being triggered, but when the RATELIMIT_IN_THRESHOLD is set to 17s, it does not take any impact and the throttling started just after the 5/6sec, which seems to be a default value as mentioned here https://collaborate.akamai.com/confluence/spaces/DPPE/pages/375816789/Max+Pacing+Rate+-+PM+Design It seems like the value given in the threshold in seconds is not being overriden correctly, Checking this with Engineering team.

19th Feb
Upon further investigation, we understand that the formula as per the ghost code for the rate conversion is as follows: bytes=time_sec×bitrate_bps/8, suggested to set a threshold time that's high enough with the same bitrate.
root_cause: It is working as per the ghost code, the formula as per the ghost code for the rate conversion is as follows: bytes=time_sec×bitrate_bps/8, suggested to set a threshold time that's high enough with the same bitrate.
creator: Dimitris Kim
mitigation_plan: Based upon the given formula, Use the correct values to get the required threshold
longterm_mitigation: Based upon the given formula, Use the correct values to get the required threshold

summary: High flits caused intermittent timeout to end users
description:
The customer reported that timeout issue occurred from multiple EIPB domains around Feb. 2nd 11:02 UTC.

Domains & CP Codes:
- [7ds.netmarble.com|https://akamai.my.salesforce.com/servlet/servlet.ExternalRedirect?url=https%3A%2F%2F7ds.netmarble.com] - 1953909
- [sol.netmarble.com|https://akamai.my.salesforce.com/servlet/servlet.ExternalRedirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsol.netmarble.com] - 1952876
- [fgo.netmarble.com|https://akamai.my.salesforce.com/servlet/servlet.ExternalRedirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Ffgo.netmarble.com] - 1952418
...

Customer is using EIPB and they are all using dscw34 map. And customer shared a client IP(175.207.6.0/24) and noticed r50458 and r40614 were assigned for the client.

>> r50458 It shows sudden flit drop near the issue time
[https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=start1770022500end1770038400&region=50458&network=A&maprule=&display=total&ymax=&utcoffset=9] 

>> Alerts in the region [https://www.nocc.akamai.com/miniurl/?id=295e1]

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10899322] has more details for the issue.

Akatec escalated this case to the NOCC and asked about the flit spike and flit capacity drop, and NOCC confirmed that the self-suspensions were triggered due to the high flit.
-- NOCC Ticket: [https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi?issue_id=B-V-6DKXQET|https://akamai.my.salesforce.com/servlet/servlet.ExternalRedirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fissues.deploy.akamai.com%2Fcgi-bin%2Fview.cgi%3Fissue_id%3DB-V-6DKXQET]

>> Can you please review this and let us know if the high flit on r50458 caused the timeout issue? If that's the case customer would like to know how to prevent this from happening again.
resolution_description: FOM.
root_cause: Tiktok traffic on 2047 , along side a bit of Coupang , cause brief overload.
creator: Dimitris Kim
mitigation_plan: FOM
longterm_mitigation: improvements to platform stability.

summary: Not able to add hostnames on the DPM
description:
IAT is trying to add these hostnames under "Delivery Policy manager" but it fails. Please refer to the below screenshot.
You can see these details under Audit Trail.

Click on Settings icon > Audit Trail

Checked Splunk logs as well, but couldn't see any useful information.

[https://portalsplunk.akamai.com/en-US/app/search/search?q=search%203db16cc959f451ef&sid=1770716501.3175852_9CD559D9-47CE-46AF-AC48-84E91004AECF&display.page.search.mode=smart&dispatch.sample_ratio=1&earliest=-4h%40m&latest=now]

 

From HAR, we do not see any failed status code.

Found one JIRA which has similar kind of issue : https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/PRTLDEVOPS-82102

Ask: Could you please help to check the reason for the failure.
resolution_description: Dev team removed the old EHNs which are having the same name but different EHNiDs
root_cause:
The failure is occurring in the EHN_POLICY_ADD step due to HAPI rejecting the EHNID registration request with error code EHNID_BAD_INPUT_0006. The backend response indicates a duplicate entry conflict for the Edge Hostnames (apicp1.phonepe.com.edgekey.net and apicp2.phonepe.com.edgekey.net).
The logs clearly show a duplicate EHNID registration conflict.
creator: Konrad Domanski
mitigation_plan: Dev team removed the old EHNs which are having the same name but different EHNiDs
longterm_mitigation: Validation requires between the name between different EHNiDs

summary: Mapping change causing TAT spike
description:
Hey Folks

IAT and Akatec have the following findings:

~~~
- Observed an abrupt performance regression starting Jan 19 06:00 UTC:
[https://grafana.akam.ai/org224/dashboard/db/tiktok-cpcode-dashboard?from=1768456361389&to=1769665961389&refresh=1m&var-cpcode=1872420&var-cpcode=971868]
- TAT increased significantly and TD0 miss rate also changed noticeably for TikTok image traffic (users primarily in Brazil, for CPCode 1872420 and 971868
- IAT confirmed this is not driven by any Property/config change
- No edge traffic increase
- No TD0 region suspend / maintenance during the timeframe
- Unable collect log due to rotated

Ask: Why the traffic started to increase from MCH to TD since Jan 19 06:00 UTC? How can we further check the reason of mapping changes?

~~~

Pulled some  recent logs [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10925123] line 60 - the high TAT seem fairly distributed. The graph shows the miss rate increase significantly.

Need to find out why and revert performance to previous levels

 

Thanks
executive_summary: Increase in miss rate and TAT midgress
root_cause: Multiple public regions in RIO /GRU being suspended due to various reasons impacted the available MCH capacity and caused the offload to drop.
creator: Rahul Sawarkar
mitigation_plan: IAT/Akatec to work with NOCC to get back the cap.
longterm_mitigation: NOCC /MCH to have alert to identify too much loss in capacity in a metro/country.

summary: {F-CS-10731197} Failures when handling DPM policy
description:
Hello ERE Team,

 

Can you help us fix the issues with adding and modifying DPM for Digipolis?:
 
*Policy details:*
https://control.akamai.com/apps/delivery-policy-manager/#/delivery-policy-assignments/267
Default_SNI_map (267)
Contract: V-6505RQM
Group: Digipolis Antwerpen-V-6505RQM (307575)
Status: Failed
Policy Type: Geo Opt In
Policy Attributes: European Union, Switzerland, and Great Britain (EU_CH_GB)
Certificate Partitioned: No
Created: Feb 2, 2026, 9:48:58 AM - jasper.cludts@evolane.be
Last Modified: Feb 9, 2026, 11:47:59 AM - [jasper.cludts@evolane.be|mailto:jasper.cludts@evolane.be]
 
 
The entire audit trail is all Failed, at various stages:
 
- The earlier fails seem to be legitimate: customer was using an EHN that looks corrupt (digipolis.edgekey.net created on Feb 5 but pointing to a slot that does not exist since Sep last year) and Portalsplunk logs complain of non-existing certificate. That seems fairly clear.
 
- The latest Fail from Feb 9, 2026, 11:47:59 is for the following:
EHN digipolis-antwerpen.edgekey.net
As per DPM API it did associated to the policy
[https://tower-ui.akamai.com/file?fileNo=mapping_267&channelName=ghostdir.deliverypolicy.data&page=1&page_size=25&selectedPosition=end&end_time=2026-02-10%2012%3A28%3A22]
 
But an error happened after that deployment, at the stage :
|6|EHN_POLICY_REMOVE |

 

Portalsplunk logs are complaining about "Could not remove delivery policy attributes for edge hostnames: \{6095907=digipolis.antwerpen.edgekey.net}"
Customer was trying to use that EHN previously, but since then deleted it and is now working with digipolis{+}*-*{+}antwerpen (EHN ID 6128836)

[https://portalsplunk.akamai.com/en-US/app/search/search?sid=1770734066.3155205_90853769-8DD6-4C30-B829-FE05ED97CE4A]



 

*Can you please help us clean up this config?*
executive_summary: The Delivery Policy Assignment Default_SNI_map (267) for Digipolis Antwerpen was stuck in a Failed state due to an error at the EHN_POLICY_REMOVE step. The workflow attempted to remove the policy from a deleted Edge Hostname (ID 6095907), resulting in a 404 from HAPI, which was treated as a hard failure instead of an idempotent success. Although earlier steps had completed successfully, the policy remained failed due to this stale reference. A coordinated workaround was executed on 16th Feb at 02:30 PM IST, where a UI update retriggered the workflow and a backend DBMC change skipped the failing step. The policy was then successfully deleted and a new assignment created.
resolution_description: A coordinated workaround was implemented to resolve the issue. Since the workflow was failing at the EHN_POLICY_REMOVE step due to a previously deleted EHN in HAPI, the step was skipped at the backend via a DBMC update and marked as successful. In parallel, the CSM/IAT team performed a minor UI update to retrigger the workflow. This allowed the process to complete successfully and the policy deletion to proceed from the UI.
root_cause: The failure was caused by stale Edge Hostname references in the workflow. A deleted EHN (ID 6095907) was still referenced, leading the workflow to attempt policy removal on a non-existent hostname and receive a 404 from HAPI. This 404 was treated as a hard failure instead of an idempotent success, leaving the Delivery Policy stuck in a failed state despite successful policy add steps and correct new EHN associations. Recent backend changes, long-running workflows, and similarly named EHNs further contributed to the issue.
creator: Marta Siemczonek
mitigation_plan: the step was skipped at the backend via a DBMC update and marked as successful..
longterm_mitigation: Long term fix is deployed in next release. As the step now automatically skip when EHN is not there.

summary: SI-00023487 - OOCM Out of country mapping issue for theDior Chinese users part of Moet Hennessy
description:
Tesla ramped up bit traffic within ChinaCDN CA2 map as part of a software release for map update event. This ticket is meant for general tracking and information interally 

 

cpcode - 968939

hostname - vehicle-maps.tesla.cn 

slot - 13711
executive_summary:
It was originally discussed to move Tesla host to CA map (p8 media map) to free up p10 ca2 traffic. 

However business choice where it stands is to leave this for now, since its a software release the expectation is that it will reduce in the near term - though was brought up there will be subsequent events. Dior plans on moving traffic by end of Feb from bridge details. 


monitoring - 
https://grafana-dmz.akam.ai/org1506/dashboard/db/maprule-qos-v-4-2?var-business_unit_owner=WebExperience&var-service=S&var-map_type=edge&var-ip_version=v4Only&var-map_group=All&var-map=S.ca2&var-continent=AS&var-geo_name=China&var-provider_name=ANY&var-case=All&from=now-30d&to=now

https://www.nocc.akamai.com/miniurl/?id=29413

root_cause: OOCM for ChinaCDN causing latency issues for DIOR in chinet mobile  - caused by Tesla map updates

mitigation_plan: nothing
longterm_mitigation: EOL chinaCDN.

summary: Frequent spikes on bifrost-api.hotstar.com during ICC T20 WC causing delivery impact
description:
During ICC T20 WC IndiavNamibia match, we observed frequent HTTP 503 spikes on "bifrost-api.hotstar.com" Web traffic.

Below are some spike details:
Time - 18:59 IST (13:29 UTC)
Count - 222
Top CPcodes: 938081
Hostname: bifrost-api.hotstar.com
Time - 19:32 IST (14:02 UTC)
Count - 182
Top CPcodes: 938081
Hostname: bifrost-api.hotstar.com
Time - 20:15 IST (14:45 UTC)
Count - 4.38K
Top CPcodes: 1793993, 1793992, 938081
Hostname: bifrost-api.hotstar.com
Time - 20:25 IST (14:55 UTC)
Top CPcode : 1793993, 1793992
Hostname: bifrost-api.hotstar.com
Time : 21:13 IST  (15:43 UTC)
Top CP codes : 938081, 1793993, 1793992

Upon investigation, we observed 503 *ERR_FWD_SSL_HANDSHAKE* spikes towards SiteShield hostname e35862.s250.akamaiedge.net (MR 5279) for all the spikes.

*Edge Hits:*

!image-2026-02-12-21-35-57-966.png|width=678,height=280!

 

*HTTP 503:*

!image-2026-02-12-21-36-11-905.png|width=678,height=278!

Upon checking the region, we see sudden spike in demand, causing bitload spikes. This is observed on multiple regions. To mitigate, we suspended maprule from top 2 affected regions 37488 (GPX-BOM1n) and 40052 (BHARENT-MAA32f) under NOCC tix: B-W-187OLA5

[https://map.akamai.com/cgi-bin/FreeFlow/Analysis/HDB_Server.pl?startmonth=2&startday=12&startyear=2026&starthour=10&startmin=0&endmonth=2&endday=12&endyear=2026&endhour=15&endmin=0&param1=40052&dbname=production&param2=10&param3=&param4=&display=graph&normalize=none&ptdisp=normal&yaxis=y-axis&size=large&scope=all&query_name=DASHBOARD2&customSql=]

 

[https://map.akamai.com/cgi-bin/FreeFlow/Analysis/HDB_Server.pl?startmonth=2&startday=12&startyear=2026&starthour=10&startmin=0&endmonth=2&endday=12&endyear=2026&endhour=15&endmin=0&param1=37488&dbname=production&param2=10&param3=&param4=&display=graph&normalize=none&ptdisp=normal&yaxis=y-axis&size=large&scope=all&query_name=DASHBOARD2&customSql=]

 

However, we still continue to see same pattern across other regions as well. Below are the few other regions where we see spike in demand:

40772 (SIFY-DELk)

[https://map.akamai.com/cgi-bin/FreeFlow/Analysis/HDB_Server.pl?startmonth=2&startday=12&startyear=2026&starthour=10&startmin=0&endmonth=2&endday=12&endyear=2026&endhour=15&endmin=0]¶m1=40772&dbname=production¶m2=10¶m3=¶m4=&display=graph&normalize=none&ptdisp=normal&yaxis=y-axis&size=large&scope=all&query_name=DASHBOARD2&customSql=

40773 (SIFY-DELj)

Maprule Flyteload: [https://map.akamai.com/cgi-bin/FreeFlow/Analysis/HDB_Server.pl?startmonth=2&startday=12&startyear=2026&starthour=11&startmin=0&endmonth=2&endday=12&endyear=2026&endhour=16&endmin=0]¶m1=5279&dbname=production¶m2=10¶m3=¶m4=&display=graph&normalize=none&ptdisp=normal&yaxis=y-axis&size=large&scope=all&query_name=MAPRULE_BY_REGION_FLYTELOAD&customSql=

Ghost logs and Cache logs are updated in Etherpad.

Etherpad: [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/bifrost]

Ask: We understand there have been optimizations made to help with the traffic spikes on the SS regions under [https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GSPMAPSTRA-5118]. Can you please review these spikes and make any recommendations to resolve this before Sunday's IndvPak game?
resolution_description: FOM, elife, split traffic with SS and oipacl
root_cause: Spiky traffic on API workflow hitting SS layer on large event causing overload
creator: Rahul Haridwari
mitigation_plan: FOM, elife, split traffic with SS and oipacl - also workflow with FOM.
longterm_mitigation: possibility of moving all to oipacl

summary: {F-CS-10860301} Thron:  CW POC requests to Origin
description:
We have a configuration using MCH, CW and POC; customer noticed CW doing duplicate requests for the first chunk of the range. 

 
We can see that the request for the first chunk is duplicated,  for example, the range {{bytes=0-2097151}} appears twice in customer logs and we can see the same in our logs.  
 
Here is the log showing this behavior:
[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/poc-test]
 
Line 11-76. 
 
CW is in the configuration using  default PM behavior, nothing in advance
!image-2026-02-16-17-12-46-576.png|width=340,height=280!
 
The only difference between the 'duplicate'range request is the object status in the second request:
 
!image-2026-02-16-17-13-46-557.png|width=793,height=94!
 
 
Need your help to understand this behavior.
executive_summary:
16th Feb
CW POC requests to Origin. 
There are two requests from CW to Origin, one request from CW Hop1 to Origin and another request from CW Hop2 to Origin due to which,  there is more traffic delivered due to the duplicated requests. As per the document, both the hops should not be going to the Origin. The issue is only observed for 1st fragment request. 
We enabled the debug sections and fetched the logs, there are cache-H error, after which the FWD list is updated to ORIGIN. After analyzing the logs internally with the team, it seems like a ghost reporting. Hence raised a ghost ticket GHOST-7453

16th March
Ghost Dev analyzed the logs and suspected that the issue is due to the recent rollout of below tag. They suggested to test the issue by disabling it.
We set the tag "OFF" in the advanced override of the Property and did not find the issue in Production.
<forward:cache-parent.sureroute.contact-multi-protocol-next-hop-on-failure>off</forward:cache-parent.sureroute.contact-multi-protocol-next-hop-on-failure>
root_cause:
There are two requests from CW to Origin, one request from CW Hop1 to Origin and another request from CW Hop2 to Origin due to which, there is more traffic delivered due to the duplicated requests. As per the document, both the hops should not be going to the Origin. The issue is only observed for 1st fragment request. Ghost Dev analyzed the logs and suspected that the issue is due to the recent rollout of below tag.
forward:cache-parent.sureroute.contact-multi-protocol-next-hop-on-failure
creator: Cesar Roque Quiros
mitigation_plan: <forward:cache-parent.sureroute.contact-multi-protocol-next-hop-on-failure>off</forward:cache-parent.sureroute.contact-multi-protocol-next-hop-on-failure>
longterm_mitigation: Followup on the linked ghost ticket GHOST-7550

The customer has reported an issue during a large-scale event on 04/02/26, where their monitoring indicates errors related to “unable to source content.” According to the customer, this maps to their proprietary 844 error code , which represents an inability to retrieve content from Akamai.

“The 844 error is a timeout retrieving content from Akamai, meaning no response was received — it took too long to get content from Akamai.”

After we disabled the DOM, we saw large suspensions for the region reducing the flit utilization to 40% sometimes. 

After investigating, we found out the customer responsible for this is Disney with violating DNSP serial: 1880.

Can we have a look on urgent basis and do something to resolve this at the earliest so we can utilize the capacity better for the large region in Germany.

summary: F-CS-10933019 - Slow Handshake Caused by Suspendflag 2048
description:
Account: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
Hostname: api-ms.payments.jpmorgan.com
Propety: api-ms-mtls.payments.jpmorgan.com_pm v.24
Maprule: 1326

 

The customer recently setup monitoring for api-ms.payments.jpmorgan.com, and noticed 40-50 failures a day related to SSL Handshake. The client is aborting the handshake after 2 minutes of not receiving the Server Hello. From the packet captures, we can see that the delay is from the amount of time between the edge ACKing the Client Hello, and sending the Server Hello. This is not isolated to one edge server, it is spread across many. Each occurrence I have checked shows the edge server had high CPU/FLIT, self suspended with suspendflag 2048, and when checking flit usage by MR for the regions, there is an increase in flits from MR-1326.

 

I have included 4 examples with analysis in the etherpad: [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/ER-261666]

 

On the individual edge servers, the CP Code that seems to have the highest swing in flits is not always the same, but when looking at the region as a while, it is clearly CP Code 1759000 that causes the upward swing in flits across the region. I am not sure that this is the root cause, possibly the increase in traffic from CP Code 1759000 causes self suspensions, which reduces the region capacity, causing issues on servers that aren't handling a ton of traffic for the offending CP Code.
root_cause: Paramount and Verizon causing instabilities for JPMC
creator: Ted Frost
mitigation_plan: Paramount and Verizon are optimizing their traffic. Paramount was asked to use OIPACL and use prespread slot. Verizon is looking to optimize flits and their slot is prespreaded as well.
longterm_mitigation: The traffic profile should be reviewed before onboarding a customer, and our in-region loadbal should be more aware and robust to spiky traffic.

summary: [F-CS-10958106]-iCloud pvt relay - Increased 504s for gb-gmt
description:
Timeframe: Apple noticed the 504s spike on 16th Feb for gb-gmt and it has been at elevated levels since then. 

Screenshot shared by Apple is attached in Jira(timestamp is PST):

Checks-

ProxyB Regions handling traffic for gb-gmt
{code:java}
region	maprule_id	maprule_name
42811	16666	q;gb-gmt.gh-g.v1.akaquill.net
44732	16666	q;gb-gmt.gh-g.v1.akaquill.net

50415	16666	q;gb-gmt.gh-g.v1.akaquill.net
50416	16666	q;gb-gmt.gh-g.v1.akaquill.net

{code}
 

Grafana - Edgehog Metrics by Maprule- Does not show any issues

[https://grafana.akam.ai/org2270/dashboard/db/edgehog-metrics-by-maprule?refresh=5m&var-mapruleid=0&var-name=q;gb-gmt.gh-g.v1.akaquill.net&from=now-7d&to=now|https://grafana.akam.ai/org2270/dashboard/db/edgehog-metrics-by-maprule?refresh=5m&var-mapruleid=0&var-name=q;gb-gmt.gh-g.v1.akaquill.net&from=now-7d&to=now)]

 

Grafana - Edgehog - Quill Connection Performance Dashboard - shows a increase in retransmits under proxy-origin connections on 16th. But this subsided automatically on same day and has not continued. This does not seem to have correlation with the elevated 504s in the graph customer has shared, where we see spike is sustained to 17th Feb and onwards.

[https://grafana.akam.ai/org2270/dashboard/db/edgehog-quill-connection-performance-dashboard?from=1771200782704&to=1771274964966&var-region=42811&var-region=44732&var-region=45043&var-region=45375&var-region=45693&var-region=45694&var-region=50415&var-region=50416&var-cpcode=1185419&var-regregion=gb-gmt&var-client_proto=All&var-client_ip_version=All&var-client_traffic_type=All&var-client_category=All&var-method=All&var-origin_ip_version=All&var-origin_traffic_type=All&var-origin_category=All&refresh=5m|https://grafana.akam.ai/org2270/dashboard/db/edgehog-quill-connection-performance-dashboard?from=1771200782704&to=1771274964966&var-region=42811&var-region=44732&var-region=45043&var-region=45375&var-region=45693&var-region=45694&var-region=50415&var-region=50416&var-cpcode=1185419&var-regregion=gb-gmt&var-client_proto=All&var-client_ip_version=All&var-client_traffic_type=All&var-client_category=All&var-method=All&var-origin_ip_version=All&var-origin_traffic_type=All&var-origin_category=All&refresh=5m)]

 

Grafana - Edgehog Metrics by Ecor - looks clear around that time

[https://grafana.akam.ai/org2270/dashboard/db/edgehog-metrics-by-ecor?refresh=5m&var-ecor=FABRIC-PAR2&var-ecor=FABRIC-LON3&var-ecor=FABRIC-LON2&var-ecor=FABRIC-LON&var-ecor=FABRIC-FRA3&var-quill=0&var-chive=0&from=now-7d&to=now]

Grafana - Edgehog Quill Request Error Stats - shows large spike in errors on 16th  [https://grafana.akam.ai/org2270/dashboard/db/edgehog-quill-request-error-stats?from=1771132775088&to=1771200996100&panelId=52&fullscreen&var-region=42811&var-region=44732&var-region=45043&var-region=45375&var-region=45693&var-region=45694&var-region=50415&var-region=50416&var-origin_hostname=All] 

Grafana - Edgehog Quill Request Error Stats - There are consistent origin errors for some specific origins filtered seen in following grafana, which show constant errors continue on 17th and 18th feb, although these were present before 16th feb also. Could. this be the reason for 504s noticed by customer ? 

[https://grafana.akam.ai/org2270/dashboard/db/edgehog-quill-request-error-stats?from=now-30d&to=now&panelId=52&fullscreen&refresh=5m&var-region=42811&var-region=44732&var-region=45043&var-region=45375&var-region=45693&var-region=45694&var-region=50415&var-region=50416&var-origin_hostname=ad.360yield.com&var-origin_hostname=lexicon.33across.com&var-origin_hostname=crl.cybertrust.ne.jp&var-origin_hostname=ssc-cms.33across.com]

 

We do not see any issues/alerts or RHM losses on these regions on 16th.

[https://perf.akamai.com/cgi-bin/mahollan/rhm.cgi?region_input=50415&start_date=2%2F16%2F26+6%3A53&stop_date=2%2F16%2F26+18%3A53]

[https://perf.akamai.com/cgi-bin/mahollan/rhm.cgi?region_input=45693&start_date=2%2F16%2F26+6%3A53&stop_date=2%2F16%2F26+18%3A53]

[https://perf.akamai.com/cgi-bin/mahollan/rhm.cgi?region_input=44732&start_date=2%2F16%2F26+6%3A53&stop_date=2%2F16%2F26+18%3A53] 

[https://perf.akamai.com/cgi-bin/mahollan/rhm.cgi?region_input=42811&start_date=2%2F16%2F26+6%3A53&stop_date=2%2F16%2F26+18%3A53] 

 

Could you please review this and help confirm the reason for the 504s Apple reported?
creator: Sameer Gupta

summary: Increase in Pristine Latency during T20 World Cup for JioStar
description:
{*}Account Name{*}:  JioStar India Private Limited

*CPCODES* : 1644840(live-ssai-pristine-hindi), 1644842 (live-ssai-pristine-rol)

*Summary:*

During the T20 World Cup fixture on Feb 22nd, between India vs South Africa, the customer’s Pristine workflow saw spikes in latency (p99th percentile) intermittently. Upon analyzing this spikes in latency individually we have come across multiple sets of TD regions that saw spikes in chc1 (MR:387) traffic leading to ERR_SSL_CONNECT_TIMEOUTS

 

We have added below the details of the investigation for the timestamps on which latency was noticed today. Below is the screenshot which customer shared showcasing spikes in Manifest Fetch Time (polling of the pristine manifests by the SSAI clients) 

!Screenshot 2026-02-22 at 7.50.34 PM.png|width=520,height=199!

Below is the screenshot for TrafficPeak at the same time where we saw spikes in latency (p99).

!Screenshot 2026-02-22 at 10.28.06 PM.jpg|width=522,height=303!

Details of the spike and analysis performed on the same,

|{quote}{{[https://map.akamai.com/cgi-bin/FreeFlow/Analysis/HDB_Server.pl?startmonth=2&startday=22&startyear=2026&starthour=11&startmin=0&endmonth=2&endday=22&endyear=2026&endhour=16&endmin=0&param1=37399&dbname=production&param2=&param3=&param4=&display=graph&normalize=none&ptdisp=normal&yaxis=y-axis&size=large&scope=all&query_name=REGION_FLYTELOAD&customSql=]}}{quote}|

 

*Asks for ERE:*
 * We have noticed that these spikes in latency that are being caused are related to the spikes in *_chc1_* traffic across a diverse set of TD regions. Also, we can see that *_chc1_* traffic has been flit intensive and this is noticed since the beginning of the match start time. 

 
 * We also see that TD regions with MR:387 have overlapping Edge Map rules. If we check the query : 
{noformat}
https://bonfire.akamai.com/cgi-bin/sql2.py?agg=map.devbl.query.akadns.net&other_agg=&format=&query=select+distinct+edge+region_key%0D%0Afrom%0D%0A%28%0D%0A++++select+region+os_td%0D%0A++++from+MCM_RegionPreferences%2C+MCM_RegionLocation%0D%0A++++where+mapRuleId+in+%28387%29+and+tier+%3D+1+and+physicalRegion+%3D+region+and+country+%3D+%22India%22+%0D%0A%29%2C%0D%0A%28%0D%0A++++select+region+edge%0D%0A++++from+MCM_RegionPreferences%2C+MCM_RegionLocation%0D%0A++++where+mapRuleId+in+%282940%2C+2941%2C+2942%2C+2943%2C+4776%2C+4779%2C+4782%2C+4785%29+and+tier+%3D+1+and+physicalRegion+%3D+region+and+country+%3D+%22India%22+%0D%0A%29%0D%0Awhere+os_td+%3D+edge+and+os_td+not+in+%28select+region+from+MCM_RegionPreferences+where+mapRuleId%3D387+and+region+in+%28select+physicalRegion+from+mcm_regions+where+name+like+%27RJIL%25%27%29%29%0D%0Aorder+by+region_key{noformat}
we can see that most of the Edge Maprules had a chunk of traffic (bit/flit wise on the TD regions), hence, we would like to check with ERE if the overlapping TD (MR:387) regions can be cross-verified with the edge map rules for suspension. 

 
 * We would also like to know/report that we are seeing spiky *_chc1_* traffic across regions and hence wanted to understand if there is any suggestion from ERE to make sure these are not impacting the customer’s pristine workflow in the upcoming high profile matches
resolution_description:
Recommended to use zch3 maprule as TD0 for pristine to not overlap with SSAI manifest workflow.
As the traffic pattern of SSAI is spiky, currently we are figuring out the solutions to handle the spikiness effectively and this would take time to get a right workaround.
root_cause:
Demand Spikes in SSAI config leads to sharp flit load increase in chc1 maprule causing region overloads.
Since pristine workflow too uses chc1, experienced latency issues.
creator: Pratik Salunke
mitigation_plan: Recommended to use zch3 maprule as TD0 for pristine to not overlap with SSAI manifest workflow.
longterm_mitigation: As the traffic pattern of SSAI is spiky, currently we are figuring out the solutions to handle the spikiness effectively and this would take time to get a right workaround.

summary: {F-CS-10970826} VIP routing traffic to suspended machines in region 49661
description:
*Issue*: We see the traffic getting routed to the suspended machine(23.211.241.21 and 23.211.241.32) behind VIP 173.222.100.174 in region 49661

*Details*:
Hostname: api.mastercard.com
CP code: 673069
Map: e14192.cd.akamaiedge.net.

Customer was impacted between 18:00 to 20:00 UTC on 18th Feb as the request was getting timedout. 

Based on NOCCs update[B-V-6E7RLLF]: 
We could see an alert on the region  "ESSL [r49661:FABRIC-EWR34y]Region with service B suspended with suspend flag 32768
After restarting service ccid2 on region at 02/18/2026 19:51 ,the alert cleared
The above alert tells us that the machines in the region had issues communicating with each other on map updates ( DNSP.llmap)

Alert details: https://www.nocc.akamai.com/miniurl/?id=29724

Taking on the sample nameserver IP(199.67.137.16) of citi, for which the VIP 173.222.100.174 from region 49661 was handed out.

summary: Ghost release breaking chunked encoding?
description:
*Background:*

CREACT-4211

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10957492]

EE-00023514:

[https://akamai.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/SC_SI_Service_Incident__c/aXea70000000KMvCAM/view]

 

*Investigation:*

I am able to consistently reproduce the issue when spoofing to Edge having:

potentially problematic ghost version being:

SWLABEL=software-FF-18700.0.0

 

compared to:

SWLABEL=software-FF-18670.0.0

 

*Problem Statement:*

Client sends PUT requests with Transfer-Encoding: chunked including trailing headers in the end.

When hitting machine with SWLABEL=software-FF-18700.0.0 NetStorage would consistently return 400 indicating we have changed/removed/broke something on the CTE trailers.

 

*How to reproduce:*

Run below sample command
{code:java}
root@localhost:~# for i in $(seq 10 30); do date; time; rclone copyto ./file.json mmns:/914513/rclone-testing/file$i.json -vv --dump headers &>> rclone-logs-2; done{code}
 

Outcome:

*Without spoofing: 2 400's returned only when machine hit had  SWLABEL=software-FF-18700.0.0 

*Spoofing machines with release-freeflow-18739.0.0 returns no errors

*Spoofing Example 23.3.69.157 with  SWLABEL=software-FF-18700.0.0 getting 100% failures + retries

 

*Ask:*

Did we change / break anything in regards of Transfer Chunked Encoding handling by Kernel or Ghost with the recent release?
creator: Maciej Maciaszek

summary: Delay in activating the Cloud Wrapper configuration (tiktik-sg)
description:
Delay in activating the Cloud Wrapper configuration (tiktik-sg). Upon reviewing the Splunk logs, we observed the following error: "Tuning parameters in previous version are still in ACCEPTED or INQUEUE state."

Splunk Log;
[https://portalsplunk.akamai.com/en-US/app/search/search?q=search%20%22contextMap.traceId%22%3D929c7071e2787b31%20AND%20Error&display.page.search.mode=smart&dispatch.sample_ratio=1&earliest=1772064000&latest=1772236800&sid=1772088198.3918571_C4D33747-398F-4DCC-963D-6C88E2682BEA]



When checking mromId: 2218, we identified two tuning parameters currently in ACCEPTED and BLESSED states.

MROM - \{id: 2821, configurationId: 2821, mromId: 2218, fpId: 340, prefix: "cw-F-AC-4915861-",…}
[https://mrom.akam.ai/app/v1/footprintConfig/2218]



We found the related KB 000091726, which suggests that the issue may be caused by a high volume of Cloud Wrapper (CW) processing Update & Activate requests.

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-10996202]

>> "It has been confirmed that the error may occur when there is a delay in processing the push due to a large number of CW Update & Activate requests. And also engineering team was already aware of the issue regarding this error, and they took action to improve it."



ASK;
Could you please help to investigate the exact root cause and advise on how we can mitigate this issue to prevent it from recurring? Customer is actively using Cloud Wrapper (CW) and frequently adding new hostnames to both CW configurations, so we would like to ensure this does not happen again in the future.
executive_summary: the configuration process was completed at 17:31 IST. Based on my understanding, there were likely multiple processes queued on the MROM side (tower), which resulted in the extended waiting period we observed.
resolution_description: We have to wait for a little more time as the resources for handling the process in a queue is limited
root_cause: Multiple processes queued on the MROM side (tower), which resulted in the extended waiting period we observed.
creator: Konrad Domanski
mitigation_plan: Nothing from ERE Side
longterm_mitigation: We have to wait for a little more time as the resources for handling the process in a queue is limited

summary: Sudden flit drop causing intermittent timeout to end users
description:
This escalation is a followup of https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/ERE-3201

Background: tiktok traffic seems to line up well with some of the flit spikes and suspensions.However tiktok is already on a spread serial 2047 to account for load influx, so FOM should help further.

After reducing FOM of r50458 , the number of sudden flit capacity drop is decreased.
However, the customer reported the same timeout occurred on 2/27 07:02 UTC.

According to the customer's shared log, the timeout occurred on multiple EIPB domains on 2/27 07:02 UTC. EIPB is using "dscw34.akamai.net" and no request found during the issue time from the ghost logs

Collected aggregatedconns.log from r52991, and found that r50458 and r40614 were assigned for client "175.207.6.0/24" . Checked the loadgraph of r50458 and r40614 and found that flit capacity of r50458 suddenly dropped right after the issue time

Checking the cache log and found:
- Multiple timeouts after the Slow Handshake(Etherpad Line 286)
- Flit was high(Etherpad Line 305)

CSE Created NOCC ticket to check if the region entered self-suspended state https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi?issue_id=B-W-19N7PTH

From NOCC T3

Mapper.akamai.com -
similar spike in traffic noticed at the period 7:02 UTC feb 27, which caused all machines to self suspend on region
https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi?issue_id=B-V-6DKXQET

Region DNSP Serial Spike -
Tiktok traffic spike on region noticed exactly at 7:02 UTC Feb 27 , region overload by tiktok traffic caused all machines to self suspend on the region
- http://grafana-hats2-epremo.akamai.com/goto/NwFHxxOvR?orgId=1

Tik tok traffic spike noticed at 7:02 -> 7:03 ( 1 min), even though tiktok traffic is on spread serail (2047).

FOM reduction was done to 70% under Jira case - https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/ERE-3201 on Feb 10 2026.

The spike caused region wide self suspensions and this in turn caused the tcp connect timeouts noticed by the customer.

Please check with ERE team, whether they can implement CRP (Client Robustness Protection) / FF (Fast Feedback) on the region , is that a possibility or further FOM reduction is required ?

This is the same recommendation shared already by ERE on previous escalation. Could you help in recommending appropriate measures that can done to avoid such issues?

Etherpad: https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11002115
resolution_description:
FOM as an immediate workaround. TikTok is already on a pre-spread serial and has hostname sharding. TWC as well is on pre-spread. 

Identified an issue of internal serial misreporting with some EHNIDs and we are working with dnsp-dev on this.

Similar traffic pattern was observed from one of Coupang's hostnames. If they expect to see spiky traffic pattern, IAT can review if hostname sharding is feasible.
root_cause: Short lived flit spikes from multiple Cpcodes - TikTok,Coupang,TWC.
creator: Senthilkumar Rajendiran
mitigation_plan: FOM
longterm_mitigation: platform stability improvements on mapping spiky traffic

summary: Forward Errors with Multiple ECORs in India
description:
HTTP forwarding errors were observed affecting multiple ecors starting from Feb 27 12:20 UTC. Preliminary investigations indicate a problem when traffic traverses AS9498. 
There are no general outage reports for AS9498, but the issue appears to be ongoing. We have seen impacts to below ecors [1] and losses towards BOM and MAA compute data centers. 

NOCC actively investigates and escalates the issue to Airtel (B-W-19N82A3). Mitigation steps are being applied where possible.
[1] 
BHARENT-BLR3 
BHARENT-BLR6 
BHARENT-BOM10 
BHARENT-BOM6 
BHARENT-CCU4 
BHARENT-DEL12 
BHARENT-DEL3 
BHARENT-DEL4 
BHARENT-HYD 
BHARENT-MAA3 
BHARENT-PNQ2 
FABRIC-BOM 
FABRIC-MAA 
GPX-BOM

This would be causing impact at multiple layers (Edge/TD/CW/MSL Infra) for VOD and Live event traffic, specifically T20 WC series
resolution_description: Airtel fixed the issue on their end. Manual suspension done on the affected ecors (BHARENT and GPX) with deny-all being applied for Airtel links in FABRIC-MAA and FABRIC-BOM ecors.
root_cause:
Airtel is serving internet traffic from India towards European destination through Dubai and London router via different subsea cable including EIG, SWM4, IMEWE etc.
During EIG repair, once the link was up on 27th Feb, a big chunk of traffic running through Singapore built the service from mumbai towards London router, unfortunately repair was in final stage and multiple flaps were observed on EIG cable which caused the packet drop and congestion on this route during Feb 27th to 28th evening.
Unfortunately Airtel London pop got isolated during same time and we could not cost out the traffic at that moment which caused the service disruption for multiple other customers intermittently.
creator: Aruna Uthayakumar
mitigation_plan: Airtel fixed the issue on their end. Manual suspension done on the affected ecors (BHARENT and GPX) with deny-all being applied for Airtel links in FABRIC-MAA and FABRIC-BOM ecors
longterm_mitigation: NA

summary: Greenseer alerts for week of 2026-03-02

creator: James Morris

summary: {F-CS-10919410} Warner: High CPU resource consumption Region 51513
description:
Custome reported an increase in latency, we reviewed logs from two different days and this is the region causing slow requests, seems to be reaching capacity very often. 

!image-2026-03-02-16-10-19-962.png|width=552,height=304!

 

NOCC checked this region and the recommendations was to engage ERE to review FOM:

 
{quote}_We checked on this and also got it checked by Tier3._
_As per our investigation, the current FOM value of this region is 72%, and all the issues of high flit utilisation, high turnaround time of requests were because of "High CPU resource consumption", which is choking the machine's processing capability._
_It is advisable for Akatec to further check on this with ERE, if the FOM value can be further reduced to avoid such an issue with the machines and, in turn, the traffic._
{quote}
 

NOCC case [https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi?issue_id=B-W-19DWBLT]

 

Logs and investigation

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/ER-261443]
executive_summary: High CPU resource consumption Region 51513
resolution_description:
Reduced FOM on the overlapping regions b/w Warner and Airbnb traffic.
Airbnb traffic is pre-warming requests and hence recommended to switch to SNI map to get a broader allowlist in place.
root_cause: Concentrated traffic from Airbnb using e;a2 maprule caused flit spikes in r51513 resulting in other customers to see performance concerns.
creator: Cesar Roque Quiros
mitigation_plan:
Reduced FOM on the overlapping regions b/w Warner and Airbnb traffic.
Airbnb traffic is pre-warming requests and hence recommended to switch to SNI map to get a broader allowlist in place.
longterm_mitigation: NA

summary: 5xx Spikes on Delivery Segments, TD – OS Timeouts and Negative Caching on FA
description:
* Recurring 5xx spikes on delivery segments observed on 1st March (Hindi / ROL), primarily correlated with OS timeouts and subsequent negative caching behavior impacting both primary and FA legs.
 * Primary leg consistently shows TD(p) → OS(p) timeouts. Failover (FA) leg, instead of mitigating impact, in several cases served 5xx due to negatively cached responses from OS primary. Primary returns 5xx from negative cache, while FA serves from parent. 

*18:37 IST – ROL*

*Primary Leg*
 * TD: r30814 (RJIL-BLRc)
 * OS: r38805 (BHARENT-MAA31s)

 * Behavior: TD(p) → OS(p) connect timeout

*FA Leg*
 * OS backup: r29178 (RJIL-BLRb)
 * Observations:

 * 
 ** Connect timeouts with OS primary

 * 
 ** SSL handshake errors with OS backup

 * 
 ** 5xx served due to negative cache

*NOCC Ticket:* B-W-19WW5Y3
*NOCC Observation:*
Flapping interface with PRIVATE-PEER__RJIO_PNI on router bharent-maa3. Jio confirmed link flap due to span issue; restored.

Succesful FA trace
{code:java}
https://crawly-aps.akamai.com/#!/?poll-id=790a96_223.196.92.45_1772382099.074_False_False{code}
 

Etherpad : [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11013130_19_05]

Across spikes:
 # TD(p) → OS(p) timeout (connect / first byte).

 # Failover triggers.

 # Instead of a fresh attempt, FA leg frequently serves negatively cached 5xx.

 # Delivery-level spike amplified due to caching.

 

 

Ask: 

Please assess the negative caching behavior impacting delivery during OS timeouts and failover, and advise on potential improvements .
executive_summary:
Primary OS gives 5xx error while the FA legs instead of mitigating, also delivers 5xx from negative cache.
I am also not sure if the  configured Failover logic has ever worked before https://collaborate.akamai.com/confluence/spaces/%7Enhoffman/pages/1047596193/PSA+Issues+with+tier+failover+logic

while investigating, we see in the "working" example, we do not see bypass-cache being evoked, also in working condition MCH hostname is used  and in non-working reporting "a340.novi-lc-segment-ind.td0.pri.lw2.akamaicache.net"  which is patterned to chapacp  which is strange,

We also see the Edge retrying the request though negatively cached, here the request is negatively cached with 5xx and FA kicked in. we observed that the request latching to be the main concern with the negative caching. 5xx has been issued with both negative caching and while serving from system cache (obj status: 'i') for the requests arrived within a few ms which are latched together.

Checking this behavior internally, meanwhile we suggested a tag if this can help, but I don't see the tag is ever used for any ARL config before.

After Analyzing the code, it seems like the Fail Action code path ignored bypass cache and returned the negatively cached response. Raised a ghost ticket https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOST-7541
creator: Shwetha Sudhakarshetty

summary: Spike in Flits on Region 51474 on Edge Map 1418 (Not SiteShield)
description:
Spike in Flits on CPcode 1487071 under the hf-apix workflow, March 1st ~9:51 PM IST (16:21 UTC) isolated to Region 51474 on Edge Map 1418 (Not SiteShield)

Seeking guidance from ERE to review this Edge map scenario. The IAT has raised concerns since the customer would like to reduce the polling frequency from 60 seconds to 30 seconds for the upcoming Semi-Finals and Finals in the T20 Worldcup event.

*Observations:*

• Other than a few CLIENT_ABORT hits there were no significant spikes in edge errors at the time.
*•* Suspend flag was triggered due to a significant increase in FLIT demand originating from the *MR1418 edge map (e;i6ts.akamaiedge.net)* at approximately *9:51 PM IST (~16:21 UTC)* — Region 51474
*•* The spike appears to be a short-duration, isolated instance of elevated FLIT demand.

!Screenshot 2026-03-03 at 1.01.20 PM.jpg|width=755,height=370!

[Nexus|[https://prod-nexus-app01.bos01.corp.akamai.com:5601/s/nocc/app/dashboards#/view/225da872-7956-401a-b6e6-3c105aa14421?_g=(filters:!((%27$state%27:(store:globalState),meta:(alias:!n,controlledBy:%271682577660596%27,disabled:!f,index:%270a955572-0a35-4a28-8533-565a6416dbdc%27,key:machineip,negate:!f,params:(query:%2723.1.241.44%27),type:phrase),query:(match_phrase:(machineip:%2723.1.241.44%27))),(%27$state%27:(store:globalState),meta:(alias:!n,controlledBy:%271682577691846%27,disabled:!f,index:%270a955572-0a35-4a28-8533-565a6416dbdc%27,key:TicketID,negate:!f,params:(query:B-W-1A5I68D),type:phrase),query:(match_phrase:(TicketID:B-W-1A5I68D)))),refreshInterval:(pause:!f,value:2000),time:(from:%272026-03-01T16:17:00.000Z%27,to:%272026-03-01T16:23:00.000Z%27))]]

Load Graph
[https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=start1772380860end1772383680&region=51474&network=S&maprule=&display=maprule&ymax=&utcoffset=5&diskcpu=on]

Current FOM
[https://bonfire.akamai.com/cgi-bin/sql2.py?agg=map.dev.query.akadns.net&other_agg=&format=&query=%23%23%23+Replace+%3CFOM+Requested+Regions%3E+with+comma-separated+regions+requested+for+FOM+reduction+%23%23%0D%0A%0D%0Aselect+case+when+private%3D1+then+%27private%27+else+%27public%27+end+prp%2C+a.region+regionID%2C+flit_overflow_mark+%0D%0Afrom+m2RegionOverflowMarks+a%2C+mcm_regionpreferences+b%2C+m2rmregionsummary+c%0D%0Awhere+mapruleId+%3D+1418+%0D%0A%0D%0A%23Replace+with+comma-separated+regions+Below%0D%0A%0D%0Aand+a.region+in+%28+51474+%29+%0D%0A%0D%0A%0D%0Aand+a.region%3Db.region+%0D%0Aand+a.region%3Dc.region+%0D%0Aand+country%3D%27IN%27]] 

Etherpad
[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11015677]

Logs:
*r51474 - cache logs* - lsg15/mnayak - 
cache-GIP23.1.241.44-202603011615-23.33.25.197-415-1772513989.gz
*Region ghostlogs* - lsg15mnayak 
GIP23.1.241.44-202603011618-23.33.25.197-116-1772515122.gz

*Actions taken:*
 - We raised a NOCC ER which was reviewed by T3 [B-W-1A5I68D|[https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi?issue_id=B-W-1A5I68D]]
 - NOCC pointed out that region *34609* also had high flit and suspensions during the same timeframe for the same event. 
{code:java}
Region 34609 with high flit and suspensions - http://grafana-hats2-epremo.akamai.com/goto/4pyXs9Ovg?orgId=1
Region 51474 - http://grafana-hats2-epremo.akamai.com/goto/mcX2srdvR?orgId=1 {code}

 - Their recommendation was to raise the concerns with ERE to check if FCC or FOM changes to region might help. 

*Ask to ERE:*
 - Would the customers reduction in polling frequency from 60s to 30s increase the likelihood of similar spikes?
 - Would FCC or FOM changes be a viable solution if these issues were to repeat during future events?
resolution_description: FOM capped region
root_cause: event cause flit based issue on single region, also customer wanted confirmation on change
creator: Tyler Deary
mitigation_plan: FOM / consult
longterm_mitigation: spikey traffic outcome

summary: {F-CS-10988822} Versant: Fandango app errors in TVs
description:
Customer found streaming errors affecting certain Fandango living room devices (Hisense, Vizio, PS5, Xbox One, Samsung, LG TVs).
Errors were observed after routing traffic to affected devices; initial tests on less popular devices (Hisense) did not reproduce the issue, but Vizio showed ~500 errors out of ~20k sessions in a 5-hour window.

executive_summary:
- Fandago showing errors in Tvs

11th March
Fandango living room TV devices (Vizio, Samsung, LG, Hisense, PS5, Xbox One) experienced intermittent DASH playback stalls and network errors. The player logged failed byte-range requests with ERR_POC_FWD_NOT_206|validatePocRangeResponse, causing visible playback interruptions — approximately 500 errors across ~20k sessions in a 5-hour window. I can see the requests are prefetched, suggested to disable the prefetch and observe.

12th March
We traced a few more errors in our logs based on customer output and the behavior is always the same, first the READ_ERROR before the error at customer logs, ERR_READ_ERROR for poc seems to be known, observed since long time GHOST-303, it is an old ticket and I've asked for it to be assigned, but regardless, Suggested to try the 2 recommendations made earlier i.e. a) disabling pre-fetch (including prefetch-fragments-during-request' and 'prefetch-fragments-after-request assuming they are 'on' based on a metadata trace) and b) enabling cache:poc2.make-masters-public-early.

4th May, 
Cesar confirmed that the issue has been resolved and we are good to close the ticket
root_cause:
When a cache node is missing a POC fragment, it attempts to cache the object by issuing a probe request (range: 0-0) to origin and it responds with a 403 instead of a 206, triggering ERR_POC_FWD_NOT_206.
The Ghost POC prefetch path bypassed those cached fragments and unnecessarily forwarded all the way to origin for a 0-0 probe which origin rejected with 403. This caused the entire range request chain to fail rather than serving from the warm cache tiers above.
creator: Cesar Roque Quiros
mitigation_plan:
a) disabling pre-fetch (including prefetch-fragments-during-request' and 'prefetch-fragments-after-request assuming they are 'on' based on a metadata trace) 
b) enabling cache:poc2.make-masters-public-early.
longterm_mitigation:
a) disabling pre-fetch (including prefetch-fragments-during-request' and 'prefetch-fragments-after-request assuming they are 'on' based on a metadata trace) 
b) enabling cache:poc2.make-masters-public-early.

summary: [SI-00023579] Wrong byte range retry on purge sequence out of date error causing corrupted content
description:
The customer reported an MD5 mismatch for a recently uploaded file, which apparently happened before, too. The files are not in-place updated, and they're new uploads with new filenames. 
Example URL: [https://ispinext-prod.bmwgroup.com/BMW_ISPI_ISTA_SDP-RSU_4.58.84.istapackage.zip.001?stime=20260304224049&etime=20260403224049&outletid=4062610&encode=1a8f7805965aac8eaa9d6]

This time, we were able to reproduce the issue on 2 different machines and identify the hex bytes being different between the files that have different MD5sum values.
||Edge||MD5sum||Matches with Origin||
|89.27.247.13|df3c8041dbce5b452167bebe67e3d2bb|No|
|89.27.241.4|8cf90674d08ad025d1d72aa89452839d|No|
|23.52.66.134|a1568897324a4bbfac12b55f119dc64a|Yes|

The hex byte difference between all 3 tests starts at a specific point, which is the beginning of a new POC file.
{code:java}
[adidebul@lsg-gss5 /tmp]$ diff 3.list-incomplete 1.list | head -n 20
2991c2991
< #2991 bytes=6270484480-6270484499 0000000 d267 5766 db4b d1a8 b49b 046c 6004 586e
---
> #2991 bytes=6270484480-6270484499 0000000 4f8c 5969 446e 0a5f cea9 d328 bcd5 1efa <===


[adidebul@lsg-gss5 /tmp]$ diff 3.list-incomplete 2.list | head -n 20
2991c2991
< #2991 bytes=6270484480-6270484499 0000000 d267 5766 db4b d1a8 b49b 046c 6004 586e
---
> #2991 bytes=6270484480-6270484499 0000000 4f8c 5969 446e 0a5f cea9 d328 bcd5 1efa <===


[adidebul@lsg-gss5 /tmp]$ diff 1.list 2.list
9135c9135
< #9135 bytes=19155386368-19155386387 0000000 1b46 ce0e 9f9f dc07 58bb d3b0 6cf4 2cd2
---
> #9135 bytes=19155386368-19155386387 0000000 4f8c 5969 446e 0a5f cea9 d328 bcd5 1efa <===
{code}
We collected the logs and traced them to see what's different between these requests on that POC file request. 
POC request ending with byte range 6270484479 had a purge sequence error, but there was a retry. However, the next POC request (starting with 6270484480) had no retry on purge sequence error.
{code:java}
[seselvan@lsg-gss7 ~]$ zcat CP1558363-202603041600-23.33.25.197-814-1772738680.gz | grep "6270484479" | awk '$2=="f" && $1=="23.55.160.63"'

{code}
Complete logs along with tests can be found in etherpad: [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11033041]
 * Line 452: Spoofing tests reproducing the issue, and a direct test that gives the right MD5sum.
 * Line 774: Retrieving the hex bytes and doing a comparison between those files
 * Line 917: Comparing the 1st and 3rd tests to identify the difference around the POC request, where the hex bytes don't match
 * Line 993: Comparing the 1st and 2nd tests to identify the difference around the POC request

In both instances, the edge that didn't retry was 23.55.160.63; that edge did retry until the POC that matched, though. 
It's important to note that this is a recurring issue for them, and they usually purge to fix the issue. For this example, they've let the files be corrupt so we can further investigate. They're planning to purge tomorrow, so please let us know if any further info needs to be collected while the issue is still reproducible.

Ask: Can we further investigate why the parent didn't retry on the purge sequence error, but served a response to the edge?
executive_summary: The customer reported getting a download with a different MD5sum compared to the file at the origin. We were able to reproduce the issue with different MD5sums returned from different edges. The files had different hex bytes at different POC chunks. Logs showed no retry on a purge sequence error, but a response was served downstream, which likely corrupted the large file.
creator: Senthuran Selvanayagam

summary: ERR_FIRST_BYTE_TIMEOUT on Cloud wrapper API
description:
*Issue:* Customer reported that they experienced failures via Cloud wrapper API requests.

*Findings:*
 * Upon investigating API logs, [origin.pulsar.akamai.com|https://akamai.my.salesforce.com/servlet/servlet.ExternalRedirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Forigin.pulsar.akamai.com] returned error 0 status with ERR_FIRST_BYTE_TIMEOUT and many 5xx responses between March 5th 03:50 ~ 05:20 UTC.
 * Splunk portal shows 5xx error for fms.

 

Etherpad link with all the logs :- [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11032278|https://akamai.my.salesforce.com/servlet/servlet.ExternalRedirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fetherpad.akam.ai%2Fp%2FF-CS-11032278] 

*API Usage Details report*

[https://control.akamai.com/apps/reports/#/predefined/api-usage-details-2?current_contract_type_id=1-2RBL&current_account_id=F-AC-5048269&purge_users=solyee.heo@samsung.com,udclrdnf4kkjfx37,3gqnnju53ujovnow&response_code=400,403,404,409,422,429,500,503,504&purge_apis=cloud-wrapper%2F1&start=2026-03-05T00:00:00Z&end=2026-03-05T23:59:59Z&timezone=Greenwich] (screenshot attached)

*Splunk portal*

[https://portalsplunk.akamai.com/en-US/app/Portal/search?earliest=1772679600&latest=1772690400&q=search%20ServiceProvider%3D%22fms%22%20%7C%20search%20status%3E499&display.page.search.mode=smart&dispatch.sample_ratio=1&sid=1772771002.3996819_9CD559D9-47CE-46AF-AC48-84E91004AECF] 

Ask: Could you please investigate why [origin.pulsar.akamai.com|https://akamai.my.salesforce.com/servlet/servlet.ExternalRedirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Forigin.pulsar.akamai.com] returned error responses for CW API during the time?
executive_summary: ERR_FIRST_BYTE_TIMEOUT on Cloud wrapper API between March 5th 03:50 ~ 05:20 UTC.
resolution_description: The application team is currently working on a long-term architectural improvement to decouple the heavy synchronization tasks from the main request path, and in the interim they are adjusting rate limits on the Open API endpoints to prevent update requests from exhausting the connection pool.
root_cause: the root cause for the errors observed between 03:50–05:20 UTC on March 5th according to him is due to that timeframe where a high number of update and activate requests triggered Global Sync operations that collect data from multiple downstream sources. Some of these downstream services were responding slowly, which caused the sync transactions to take longer to complete
creator: Floyd Francis
mitigation_plan: Adjustment of rate limits on the Open API endpoints to prevent update requests from exhausting the connection pool.
longterm_mitigation: Working on long-term architectural improvement to decouple the heavy synchronization tasks from the main request path

summary: Recurring 503 - SSL Handshake Timeouts on Bifrost Workflow During IND vs ENG (Semi Final 2)
description:
During the ICC T20 WC Semi Final 2 (IND vs ENG) match, we observed frequent HTTP 503 with  *ERR_FWD_SSL_HANDSHAKE|err_conn_strict_cert* spikes impacting web traffic for {{{}bifrost-api.hotstar.com{}}}.

We have identified multiple regions that experienced sudden max load drop along with spikes in flit utilization. These events correlate with the observed {{ERR_FWD_SSL_HANDSHAKE}} spikes and resulting HTTP 503 responses at delivery. The affected regions, along with the corresponding timestamps and maprules, are listed below for further investigation.

Platform Traffic Split (Expected)

STLS → map ch7 → ~20% traffic share

ESSL → maps ch42 & s250 → ~80% traffic share (expected 50-50 split)

 
 * *Time:* 18:58 IST (13:28 UTC)
 ** 200: ~982K
 ** 503: ~3.92K
 ** Region: 43830
 ** MR: 5697

Observation:

We observed a surge in FLIT load along with a drop in ra_capacity, which resulted in errors. This map is responsible for approximately 20% of bifrost-api.hotstar.com traffic.
 * {*}Time: 1{*}9:08 IST (13:38 UTC)
 ** 200: ~1.30M
 ** 503: ~21.8K
 ** Region: 40722
 ** MR: 5279

Observation:

Around 13:38 UTC, the majority of traffic was directed to the s250 map, whereas the expected behavior is a 50-50 split between s250 and ch42.

During this time:

MR16218 and serial 4025 showed significant spikes, breaching ra_capacity.
 * *Time:* 19:17 IST (13:47 UTC)
 ** 200: ~1.48M
 ** 503: ~14.6K
 ** Region: 40052
 ** MR: 5279

Observation:

We observed another surge in FLIT load on MR5279 during this interval. Ideally, traffic should have remained evenly distributed between ch42 and s250. It is possible that ch42 was receiving a comparable share but handling the traffic more efficiently, resulting in fewer visible errors. 
 * *Time:* 19:27 IST ({*}13:57 UTC{*})
 ** 200: ~1.90M
 ** 503: ~6.92K
 ** region —> 40724 ; MR: 5279

 * 
 ** region —> 15853; MR1042

Observation:

Behavior remained consistent with the previous interval, with continued imbalance toward s250 and associated load spikes. 
 * *Time:* 19:42 IST ({*}14:12 UTC{*})
 ** 200: ~1.37M
 ** 503: ~2.28K
 ** Region: 40622
 ** MR: 5279

Observation:

Similar pattern observed with FLIT load surges and traffic skew toward s250. 
 * {*}Time :{*}20:05 IST ({*}14:35 UTC){*}
 ** Region: 51441
 ** MR : 1042
 ** Observation: Spike in 8656, 8652 API traffic and flits cap was crossed exactly during the issue spike
 * {*}Time :{*}21:31 IST *(16:01 UTC)*
 ** Regions : 37657
36096
 ** MR : 1042
 ** Observation: Max load dropped at the same time for both the parent regionssame set of regions affected apix at the same time
 *  {*}Time :{*}22:52 IST (17:22 UTC)
 ** Region:40256,40622,41431 
 ** Map :  majorly on 5279 also on 1042
 ** max load dropped at the same time for all the parent regions

Same data in Gsheet - [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1HxECtRUW2q6Y_ffHLZkwmAsr6svIfNIbMH3fOMSQiOU/edit?gid=203244305#gid=203244305]

Etherpad : [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/bifrsot_march5th] 

Given the anticipated higher traffic demand during the Final, we request ERE assistance to review the following items to ensure capacity readiness and avoid recurrence of similar delivery impact:
 * We had previously raised a similar issue under ERE-3207. Based on the learnings from that investigation, we would like to revisit the traffic distribution across {{s250}} and {{ch42}}
Observations indicate that error spikes between 18:58 – 19:42 IST and 22:52 IST were predominantly concentrated on {{{}s250{}}}, while spikes at 20:05 IST and 21:31 IST were mainly observed on MR 1042 ({{{}ch42{}}}).

 * Capacity and flit utilization review on impacted regions associated with these maps, as several spikes coincided with sudden max load drops and flit utilization surges, leading to {{ERR_FWD_SSL_HANDSHAKE}} and HTTP 503 responses at delivery.

 * Review STL map {{ch7}} for any possible optimizations or adjustments to improve stability and traffic handling during peak match traffic.

 * Please help us establish a solid mitigation and readiness plan ahead of the Final.
resolution_description:
Including FA setup in the config to handle failures on primary path would help here.
Reduced FOM on regions which showed up issues with spiky traffic.
root_cause: Spiky event demand of flit intensive traffic causing flit overloads in the machines leading to suspensions. This results in error being sent downstream impacting end users.
creator: Shwetha Sudhakarshetty
mitigation_plan:
Reduced FOM on regions which showed up issues with spiky traffic.
Recommended to include FA setup in the config with default SCM maps to handle failures on the primary path.
longterm_mitigation:
1. Ghost MPGs of an ecor to be served from different TD regions which splits the traffic across multiple parent regions - by using MPG ID as key rather than PP while forming scorelist.
2. DNSP to handle the traffic spikes smoothly and spread the requests across machines anticipating the demand.

summary: {F-CS-10949057} - 286.x.x.x reference ID without info in logs - possible CRP issue
description:
The customer has a client who started receiving 503 errors on the hostname [https://api.schwabapi.com|https://api.schwabapi.com/]

The client shared reference IDs; these start with an error code 286, here is a list they shared last week:

[^referenceids.csv]

According to the [documentation|https://qtable-docs.akamai.com/static/cp_error.html], 286.x.x.x refers to an error related to Client Robustness Protection (CRP) - *err_crp_deny*

We tried getting logs in QGREP using the host CP Code and we didn't find any single request ID of the ones the customer provided, so we extracted full logs of the edge IPs of the reference IDs matching 503 status code, and this is what we found:
{noformat}
[jugaldeg@lsg-gss7 ~/F-CS-10949057]$ zgrep "api.schwabapi.com" GIP173.222.249.148-202603051600-23.33.25.197-771-1772758059.gz | awk ' $2=="S" {print $16, $17}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr
  16651 503 286{noformat}
Sample log lines:
{noformat}
[jugaldeg@lsg-gss7 ~/F-CS-10949057]$ zgrep "1772727181" GIP173.222.249.148-202603051600-23.33.25.197-126-1773081368.gz | grep "34.34.225.73"

173.222.249.231 S 1772727181.333 0 0 0 0 280 280 498 34.34.225.73 GET v1 accounts orders 503 286 - text/html - D 10 0 0 - - - - WJ 8 ^ ^ api.schwabapi.com ^ ^ ^ ^ ^ ^ ^ ^ ^ ^ - - ^ ^ - ^ - ^ ^ ^ sip=1;mle=16261;crp=d;rx=23566||23555:371:2 - - ^ 16382. 581:7797:0:0 UNSET:UNSET::UNSET:: - - - ^ - - 116 116 607 61148 0 - - - 173.222.249.148|1|2|1|16382|606|19288|37|0 - - ^{noformat}
The sample log line shows a "Error number: 286" which matches the refIDs 286.x.x.x, but those logs don't have request IDs nor path, so matching by reqIDs or by path in either DDC or cache.logs is not possible.

However, in cache.logs, filtering logs by epoch time AND ARL_ID we found this:
{noformat}
[jugaldeg@lsg-gss7 ~/F-CS-10949057]$ zgrep "173.222.249.231" cache-GIP173.222.249.148-202603051600-23.33.25.197-762-1773080859.gz | grep "1772727181" | grep "arlid:442085"
173.222.249.231 1772727181.293|7f5f76a2c640|               0|       0|276| 1|N|27914| crp::OverloadManager::updateActiveRuleMap(): rule:'rule_deny_req_lat50' trigger_pct:100.0% deny_pct:100.0% action_bitmask:2 target (arlid:442085 ehnid:0) metrics (cpu:1239ms mem:2157KB reqs:0), added to active set
173.222.249.231 1772727181.495|7f5f76a2c640|               0|       0|276| 1|N|27916| crp::OverloadManager::replaceActiveRuleMap(): rule:'rule_deny_req_lat50' trigger_pct:100.0% deny_pct:97.0% target (arlid:442085 ehnid:0) metrics (cpu:1239ms mem:2157KB reqs:0), decay factor cycle:1 of 25
173.222.249.231 1772727181.495|7f5f76a2c640|               0|       0|276| 1|N|27914| crp::OverloadManager::updateActiveRuleMap(): rule:'rule_deny_req_lat50' trigger_pct:100.0% deny_pct:97.0% action_bitmask:2 target (arlid:442085 ehnid:0) metrics (cpu:1239ms mem:2157KB reqs:0), added to active set
173.222.249.231 1772727181.700|7f5f76a2c640|               0|       0|276| 1|N|27916| crp::OverloadManager::replaceActiveRuleMap(): rule:'rule_deny_req_lat50' trigger_pct:100.0% deny_pct:93.0% target (arlid:442085 ehnid:0) metrics (cpu:1239ms mem:2157KB reqs:0), decay factor cycle:2 of 25
173.222.249.231 1772727181.701|7f5f76a2c640|               0|       0|276| 1|N|27914| crp::OverloadManager::updateActiveRuleMap(): rule:'rule_deny_req_lat50' trigger_pct:100.0% deny_pct:93.0% action_bitmask:2 target (arlid:442085 ehnid:0) metrics (cpu:1239ms mem:2157KB reqs:0), added to active set
173.222.249.231 1772727181.908|7f5f76a2c640|               0|       0|276| 1|N|27916| crp::OverloadManager::replaceActiveRuleMap(): rule:'rule_deny_req_lat50' trigger_pct:100.0% deny_pct:89.0% target (arlid:442085 ehnid:0) metrics (cpu:1239ms mem:2157KB reqs:0), decay factor cycle:3 of 25
173.222.249.231 1772727181.908|7f5f76a2c640|               0|       0|276| 1|N|27914| crp::OverloadManager::updateActiveRuleMap(): rule:'rule_deny_req_lat50' trigger_pct:100.0% deny_pct:89.0% action_bitmask:2 target (arlid:442085 ehnid:0) metrics (cpu:1239ms mem:2157KB reqs:0), added to active set
{noformat}
We found that, apparently, those requests are being blocked and failing by the "{*}rule_deny_req_lat50{*}"

I tried to find more info about the rule and see why it's being applied and if that is indeed causing the 286.x.x.x errors.

I traced metadata and didn't find it being applied:
{noformat}
173.222.249.231 /usr/local/akamai> metadata --mode=metadata3 --type=GET 'https://api.schwabapi.com/trader/v1/accounts/'  | grep -i "rule_deny_req_lat50"
173.222.249.231 /usr/local/akamai>

173.222.249.231 /usr/local/akamai> metadata --mode=metadata3 --type=GET 'https://api.schwabapi.com/'  | grep -i "rule_deny_req_lat50"
173.222.249.231 /usr/local/akamai>

173.222.249.231 /usr/local/akamai> metadata --mode=metadata3 --type=GET 'https://api.schwabapi.com/'  | grep -i "subnet.W.crp"
173.222.249.231 /usr/local/akamai>{noformat}
According to [https://collaborate.akamai.com/confluence/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=1353361073&spaceKey=~savkumar&title=Deny%2Banalysis%2B-%2Brule_deny_req_lat50] the rule "{*}rule_deny_req_lat50{*}" is applied in the following rule:
{noformat}
<match:region.attribute value="subnet.W.crp-rule-req-deny-lat50" result="true">
<config:overload-manager.rule>
<name>rule_deny_req_lat50</name>
<warn-mode>off</warn-mode>
<actions>
<reject-incoming-requests>on</reject-incoming-requests>
</actions>
<target>
<min-cpu-usage>20</min-cpu-usage>
<decay-factor>25</decay-factor>
</target>
<trigger>
<threshold>
<from>0.1</from>
<to>0.15</to>
</threshold>
<phantom-runnable-queue-latency>
<status>on</status>
</phantom-runnable-queue-latency>
</trigger>
</config:overload-manager.rule>
</match:region.attribute>{noformat}
But I don't see it anywhere for this hostname, neither in the PM Config nor in baseline.

Request your assistance checking:

#1. if the cause of the 286 is indeed CRP ("{*}rule_deny_req_lat50{*}")

#2. if those requests are being blocked by "{*}rule_deny_req_lat50{*}" then where it's being applied and why it's blocking the requests?

Checking customer's spreadsheet, the 2 IPs with errors were 173.222.249.231 and 173.222.249.148 (r38631). Checking load, errors, etc during the time the users had errors (Mar 5th, 16:00 - 16:13 GMT), we can see a little spike in errors and load: [https://region360-aps.akamai.com/?inputValues=38631&startDateTime=2026-03-05%2015:00:00&endDateTime=2026-03-05%2017:00:00] (Attaching images)

Investigating a bit further, looks like the issue was caused by:

=====================

_Overload Manager saw the node in that region (for ARLID 442085) with:_
 * _High run queue: triggered by runQ:0.149_
 * _Latency/overload metrics crossing the rule's internal threshold._

_That combination pushed the rule's trigger condition to ~97% of cycles, so CRP activated the deny rule._

_Once active, rule_deny_req_lat50 is configured to *reject incoming requests* for that target ARLID._

_deny_pct:100% → during that period, it attempted to deny essentially all new requests it considered in scope for that rule._

_The logs shows *decay* (decay factor cycle:1 of 25, deny_pct dropping from 100% to 97%, then further in subsequent cycles), meaning as the box recovered, CRP gradually reduced the deny rate._

=====================

If that was the cause of the errors, how can we determine what caused the high CPU utilization and overload in the edge IPs? And how to determine if the errors were caused by that client IP or others?

I found [https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOST-7481] and this analysis [https://collaborate.akamai.com/confluence/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=1353361073&spaceKey=~savkumar&title=Deny%2Banalysis%2B-%2Brule_deny_req_lat50] of this error being caused by high CPU utilization caused by Customer A but affecting Customer B...

c.c [~savkumar]
creator: William Ugalde

summary: Transient OCSP verification error with ERR_OCSP_STAT_MALFORMEDREQUEST from external responder
description:
The client certificate OCSP verifications are failing sporadically with the error response 1 (malformed requests)

Please see : [https://docs.google.com/document/d/1rzHDcvnHyvyOquIUTm7zVTkMQnke1J64RZ5I8jySHn4/edit?usp=sharing|https://docs.google.com/document/d/1rzHDcvnHyvyOquIUTm7zVTkMQnke1J64RZ5I8jySHn4/edit?usp=sharing]

There were five periods where the customer observed 403 with ERR_OCSP_STAT_MALFORMEDREQUEST.

①2026/3/4 11:01～11:06  

②2026/3/4 22:06～22:08

③2026/3/5 23:12～23:16

④2026/3/9 03:20～03:50

These issues happened on the different edge servers, at the different time periods. 

Comparison of the OCSP logs (CP 622243) on an edge server showed the same size of request size and header size (470 bytes).

Clients distribution is widespread.

Those indicated, the error happens at the OCSP verification site at the low probabilities.

Comparison tables are provided.

[https://docs.google.com/document/d/1rzHDcvnHyvyOquIUTm7zVTkMQnke1J64RZ5I8jySHn4/edit?usp=sharing|https://docs.google.com/document/d/1rzHDcvnHyvyOquIUTm7zVTkMQnke1J64RZ5I8jySHn4/edit?usp=sharing]



Please find out the root cause for the error response from the OCSP responder (GlobalSign).

The customer is in financial industry and one error means one end-user who cannot pay.   It is relatively serious issue.
resolution_description: OCSP request is structurally valid. The request and response should be reviewed directly with the external OCSP responder.
root_cause: Malformed error was a responder reply to the OCSP request that was passed through.
creator: Prerna Singh

summary: Investigation request for regional traffic spike and preventive actions
description:
Customer reported significant spikes in connect latency on March 5th and March 8th, which triggered multiple automated alerts between 6:30 PM and 10:00 PM IST suspecting the surge in viewership during cricket finals match time may be causing regional congestion that is affecting their traffic performance.

For now we are only checking instances on March 8th.

13:38:30–13:41:28 UTC
14:02:48–14:04:25 UTC
15:13:21–15:17:41 UTC

In all the above three time stamps 2 parent regions were common-52808 TATA-BOM32o and 36327 BHARENT-MAA31j.Although none stands out exceptionally but these were the common top 2 with higher number of requests

[https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=2w&region=52808&network=A&maprule=&display=maprule&ymax=&utcoffset=9]

[https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=2w&region=36327&network=A&maprule=&display=maprule&ymax=&utcoffset=9] 

We can see MR1042 spike for both regions and this is TD region for PhonePe and Jiostar. 
{code:java}
ISSUE TIMESTAMP: WHEN SUSPENSION IS SEEN IN REGION: 52808 : 13:39-13:40 GMT 8th MarchPrinting host header

[sshridha@lsg-gss13 ~]$ zcat GIP23.218.249.118-202603081339-23.33.25.197-56-1773138765.gz|  awk '{if ($2=="r") print $18; if ($2=="S") print $33}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -25
1509201 apicp1.phonepe.com     >> We see spike in this domain
1439399 bifrost-api.hotstar.com
 859202 apix.hotstar.com
 212260 live12p-ssai-akt-mum-child.cdn.hotstar.com
  52964 live12p-sgai-n-akt-child.cdn.hotstar.com 
...
...

MR: 1042 --> e;ch42.akamaiedge.net -->second choice primary for S.ch43(Supernet IP ACL maps for API Acceleration ESSL){code}
Mapper tool shows the region load and suspended status as well.

[https://www.nocc.akamai.com/miniurl/?id=29818] 
[https://www.nocc.akamai.com/miniurl/?id=29819] 

For the nature of this traffic IAT updated;
{quote}These are payment requests and impact of latency causes delayed payments and compliance issues to the customer and are answerable to the local regulatory authority. Given their traffic volume, it is very hard for us to look into the logs and confirm latency impact since 5 min of logs amount to multiple GB's but as per customer the issue becomes a regulatory compliance and has its buz impact due to the same that may not be directly quantifiable.

Since these are payment requests, 90% of them are POST requests methods. Hence no caching and no FA or retry in place either. Hence the impact is direct. Akatec has already suggested IAT to implement retry for POST incase of hard failure against origin (5xx) and also to reduce the reconnect value from platform default (1+3) to 2, since we have seen instances where  origin connect failures causes reconnect attempts and that causes the ghost to get loaded due to customer traffic volume and reconnect attempts that mount.
{quote}
[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11051787] has more information on the issue.

Questions:
 # Can you please review and confirm if the event traffic caused the region stability issue for the TD map(mr 1042)?
 # We don't have any information on the next event but is there any workaround to mitigate the issue if it happens again? Would lowing FOM help in this case?
 # Can ERECSO-104  help as a long term prevention plan?
resolution_description: FOM reduction would be the immediate workaround. Since JioHotstar event is completed by now, not proceeding with FOM reduction. FOM can be reduced based on the regions' stability in the upcoming events.
root_cause: Spiky demand of TD IPACL map, e;ch42 caused region overloads leading to performance concerns for the customer.
creator: Dimitris Kim
mitigation_plan: FOM reduction would be the immediate workaround. Since JioHotstar event is completed by now, not proceeding with FOM reduction. FOM can be reduced based on the regions' stability in the upcoming events.
longterm_mitigation:
Handling the traffic spikes effectively in the platform.
Using MPG ID as key while constructing scorelist for Ghost MPGs to make sure regions are placed in different position for different MPGs of a ecor.

{*}Ask{*}:

- How to resolve these alerts and get the machines with current metadata, so that customer can roll out the changes that they would like to make? 
- with the recent increase in stale metadata, is there something that is missed after installs? as the IPs impacted where recently installed as in on 11th March 2026. This would potentially all customers that would have made config changes and have traffic on these machines.
