Would like to get help to understand why we don't see any f line and content-type and if any specific debugs need to be added to investigate the same.
executive_summary:
Incorrect unzipped Content served to end-user with no Content-type header

21st April
The customer observed this string abcd1234efgh5678 whenever they see the corrupted content, while analyzing the logs, we understood that it is not a random strong, it was a ghost internal upload buffer. 

29th April 
upon further investigation, we found that the working requests has only one IP while the non working has multiple Akamai IPs along with localhost IP, it indicates that the request had looped through Akamai once.

30th April,

We further enabled the CLIENT debug ID 24665 to analyse the client headers, we observed that the test object header was present in the cache logs, and tried reproducing the issue with this header and we could see it matches with what customer mentioned as the incorrect content and the client IP 183.110.97.251 is a part of netmarble and this problematic request arrived at Akamai from this client. So it seems like the customer has looped sure route requests back to Akamai.
root_cause: we observed that the test object header was present in the client requests and while reproducing the issue with that header, we could see it matches with what customer mentioned as the incorrect content
creator: Suman Mishra
mitigation_plan: Test object header was present in the client requests, suggested to check with client and adjust this header
longterm_mitigation: Test object header was present in the client requests, suggested to check with client and adjust this header

summary: [F-CS-11054139]-ESI parse tree cache-hit is resulting in unexpected/wrong content when using Edge Side Includes(ESI).
description:
Issue -  ESI parse tree cache-hit is resulting in unexpected content caching when using Edge Side Includes(ESI).   Environment - PM config - opendata_pm Test URL: [https://election-municipale.linternaute.com/test-04032026-fred?cookie&esi=actu] The Cache behavior is set to "no-store" by default in the configurations; Customer transmit caching directives from the origin via the Edge-Control header. They have two main use cases: Anonymous access, for which they send: Edge-Control: dca=esi, !no-store, max-age=XXX, donwstream-ttl=0 Authenticated access (presence of a LINTERSESSID cookie) for which they send: Edge-Control: dca=esi, !no-store, bypass-cache (in order to avoid deleting any cache that may exist for anonymous users) Bypass cache in criteria cookie LINTERSESSID exists (the rule "Advanced Metadata" > "Non Static objects" > "Cookies" that includes this behavior triggers also when other cookies exist)Without this behavior, the responses stored in object cache for anonymous access may be read from the object cache for authenticated access. So the requests coming in with cookie should be treated to bypass the cache.  Problem scenario - When they have many authenticated accesses to a URL (presence of cookie), the cache becomes poisoned with the content served for these requests (the headers served to the client are indeed those of an anonymous version of the page, but the content corresponds to the content served to the authenticated request). This occurs as soon as they activate the ESI preprocessor via the Edge-Control header: dca=esi (whether or not there are ESI elements in the page).   Investigation - To differentiate the content in responses - They have a request ID set by origin in the headers. (Example: x-ccm-req-id: 1772803284.69aad4d489d427.46939635) This same ID is written in the body of the page: (Ex:

Reqid: 1772803284.69aad4d489d427.46939635

) They also have an authentication indicator in the page code: for anonymous access:

Auth: pas authentifie

for authenticated access:

Auth: Authentifie jODInsituEdit

  Tests- A good example - In this request we are not sending a cookie, so the request will be from cache and we see the req id "x-ccm-req-id: 1773238074.69b1773a8881c8.82439875" is same in header and the body.  [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/ER-263297] line 10 A bad example for comparison. Shows that the value in header x-ccm-req-id: 1773238074.69b1773a8881c8.82439875 and the body are different. Also the body should not contain "Auth: Authentifie jODInsituEdit", as this is only seen when the request comes in with a cookie and bypass-cache is applied in that case. However, in this instance, it gets returned as part of the response.  [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/ER-263297] line 50 Checking the log line for this request, we can see that object status logs "r - An esi parse tree cache was hit." Additional logs and http captures are in - [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/ER-263297] There were some old CRs that talk about a similar issue with esi-parse-tree/responses not honoring bypass-cache resulting in a cache hit and affecting responses/cache poisoning. [https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1S39j4fDKXRqWzNr6OwQ97JesttuWz2jO] These tags were discussed as part of a solution in those CRs - edgecomputing:esi.limits.tree-pin and edgecomputing:esi.tree-caching.disable-cache Could you please confirm if these tags can be used as a workaround in this scenario? Why does esi-parse-tree cache hit happen intermittently, resulting in an incorrect object being returned in response?
creator: Sameer Gupta

summary: 503 spikes for bolt-cp1.phonepe.com - Websocket review
description:
Requesting you to help with any recommendations that can be done for this configuration: bolt-cp1.phonepe.com. 

This is also app-based end-user traffic but on websockets, the overall concurrency connection is 500k for websockets. 

The customer's traffic is at 20% on Akamai. Account level grafana  details for websocket(search by account name).

Here is a summary of the technical issue and its resolution based on the investigation:

What went wrong?
The domain bolt-cp1.phonepe.com experienced significant 503 error spikes between April 1st and April 2nd, 2026. While the errors were visible on the traffic dashboards, they did not appear on the origin server graphs, indicating the issue was occurring at the network edge.


Why did it happen?
The root cause was a WebSocket connection bottleneck at the edge server level:
Socket Limits: The number of active WebSocket connections exceeded the 32K threshold per edge server, triggering an LRU (Least Recently Used) connection pruning.
Traffic Hotspots: Because SureRoute (performance-based routing) was enabled, the system was funneling the majority of traffic through a few high-performing regions (specifically TATA-BOM32p and TATA-BOM32o).


Result: These specific regions became "hotspots" that hit their connection limits, resulting in ERR_CONNECT_FAIL and "Cannot assign requested address" errors.
What fixed it?


A metadata (MTD) change was implemented on April 6th to change how traffic is distributed, this metadata is the same fix that we applied for SR+OIPACL issue:
Disabled SureRoute: This stopped the system from concentrating traffic in a few "fastest" regions.


Enforced CH42 RA Map: This shifted traffic to a more direct, distributed mapping.
Outcome: Traffic is now more evenly spread across a larger number of servers. This prevents any single server from reaching the 32K socket limit, which has eliminated the 503 errors for now.
creator: Thilaga Raju

summary: g2g handshake error rate spiked on 22.5.0 release
description:
During the special regions deployment after ESSL phase V,  NOCC noticed the special regions of IG7 has ssl handshake error rate over 15% for a long time (3/17 21:00 - 3/18 04:00 UTC). 
!image-2026-03-18-15-10-15-581.png!
creator: John Pan

summary: g2g handshake error rate spiked on 22.5.0 release
description:
The issue started on 10:30 AM PST on 4/9/2026 when we started the phase 1 deployment. The high rate persists till we suspend all the 22.5.0 machines on 4/14/2026.   To debug this handshake issue, we need to run wire capture on 3~4 selected 22.5.0 ESSL machines. 
| |
executive_summary: During the ESSL 22.5.0 phase 1 and phase 2 checklisting, we observed large increase in g2g handshake error rate.
creator: John Pan

summary: {F-CS-11159213} X-Cache showing TCP_HIT when request is not served from cache
description:
Hi Team, 

Hostname: [www.newsuat.com.au|https://www.newsuat.com.au/] 
PM configuration: www.newsuat.com.au_simplified v143 
URL: https://www.newsuat.com.au/services/personalisation/recommendations
etherpad: [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11159213] 
Metadata for caching + EdgeWorker for the above URL added in etherpad line: 578 



 

On same server intermitent request shows TCP_HIT where md5 hash is different though request goes forward to EW and then to origin.  Issue is seen with POST request where customer has POST caching enabled and to Include an post body MD5 hash in cache key. 

customer triggers a high volume of request 100+ per second against a staging IP to replicate the issue with around 10+ times that showing TCP_HIT (which should not be showing TCP_HIT as md5 hash are different). 

Every Test is using different unique parameter in POST body so as to ensure the request are unique and every new request won't use the same cache. compared TCP_HIT and TCP_MISS, the difference on object status is "i", which means "it is found in file system buffer cache", but regardless of cache presence, the request still go and contact origin.


Issue need to be replicate by sending numbers of request in a auto script, hence we cannot immediate stop the new request when the issue observed, that's blocking our capability to check immediate on the cache manager on why the content was cached.

Debug log was enabled on the edge IP over staging network, and we were able to collect the cache.log  but based on the section 55, we see TCP_HIT entry created and destroyed and cache-key entry formed, nothing apart from that.

summary: 5xx Spikes and failover errors from ch970(r40321)
description:
*Issus summary:*

Between April 10 and April 12, 2026, multiple performance issues and 5xx error spikes were observed for CPcode 1881644. While failover mechanisms were triggered in most cases, they were either unsuccessful due to regional resource exhaustion (flits) or rendered ineffective by Origin-side errors.

-------------------------------------------

*1. Incident on April 10, 2026:* Midgress Performance Degradation
Timeframe: 04:20 PM – 04:22 PM GMT (09:50 PM – 09:52 PM IST)

The primary issue was concentrated at the Midgress (Parent) layer, specifically within regions serving the Indian market. While Edge-to-Client 200 OK responses remained high (~99.4%), the internal forward request success rate dropped due to parent server saturation.

Error Types: Significant spike in 503 (Service Unavailable) and 0xx (Connection Errors/Timeouts).

Root Cause: Several parent machines (Regions r36987 and r40321) reached critical CPU utilization levels (>95%).

Specific Impacted Nodes:

23.212.99.108 (r36987 - TATA-DEL): CPU hit 98%.
23.55.245.71 (r40321 - GPX-BOM2): CPU hit 97%.

Result: The high load led to ERR_LIFETIME_EXP|clientTimeout and ERR_SSL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT, as parent servers could not complete handshakes or process requests in time.

{*}2. Incident on April 11, 2026{*}: Origin-Level Errors
Timeframe: 02:34 PM – 02:36 PM GMT (08:04 PM – 08:06 PM IST)

Unlike the previous day, the errors on April 11th were largely traced back to the Origin infrastructure.

Error Distribution: Post-failover (FA) statistics showed that 31.4% of requests resulted in 503 errors.

Origin Performance: Log analysis confirmed that the Akamai TD1/Cloud Wrapper layers were receiving 503 responses directly from the Origin IP (34.8.232.57).

{*}3. Incident on April 12, 2026{*}: Region Suspension and Traffic Spikes
Timeframe: 02:03 PM – 02:05 PM GMT (07:33 PM – 07:35 PM IST)

Primary Failure: A single machine, 23.213.0.128 (Region r37451), was suspended, causing immediate ERR_SSL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT for a large block of traffic.

Failover Impact: Traffic successfully failed over to secondary regions(ch970), but this caused a sudden traffic spike in Region r40321 (GPX-BOM2e).

Load Spike Details:

Servers 23.55.245.22 and 23.55.245.76 saw CPU usage jump to 98-100%.

flits levels exceeded 110%, leading to high latency and ERR_LIFETIME_EXP (Client Timeouts) during the handshake phase.

-------------------------------------------

>>> Specifically IAT would like to know how to mitigate and prevent for the issue happened on Apr. 12th(3)

Observations :
Error seen on primary leg due to 2 reasons where FA recovery was not great:
a) Small amount of timeouts between CW and TD0 but MPE has amplified the error rate - 14 timeouts on CW led to 4K+ errors at edge \{primary contributor}
b) Timeout on single machine at TD0 tier

For (a) - failover triggers to TD1 alternate ch970 - request is concentrated hitting 2 machines and ends up with connect timeout.
For (b) Failaction to alt TD0 - chapacb kicks in and response is successful
{quote}Ask: Post failaction, the number of successful requests are very less. Hence, please help us on the recommendations with the load spreading and CH-970 map.
{quote}

summary: [ghostdir.hd.data/amos_best_practices_v1.xml] Cascading timeouts, read higher than fbt
description:
*Background:*

The customer reported some incomplete delivery for FULL GET with POC enabled over MultiCDN-CW config.

 

*Investigation:*

There are some first byte timeouts taking place due to latency between OOC (out of country) CW0 and CW1 hops.

When such FBT triggers for POC Byte-Range related requests created by ghost there is an built-in retry, reforward.

However there is also is read timeout before response body triggering downstream due to http.read threshold being lower than fbt, such results in incomplete delivery and lack of successful midgress retry capability delivered downstream to the Client req. 

 

*Example:*

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11211392_mandreol]

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/ER-265999_timeouts-multicdn]
{code:java}
[mmaciasz@lsg-gss17 /a/tools/metadata/ghostdir.hd.data]$ less -N amos_best_practices_v1.xml
   107     <!--
    108       Cascaded timeouts for CW-MCDN
    109     -->
    110     <!-- timeouts applicable on edge -->
    111     <forward:cache-parent.non-tail-connect-timeout>2s</forward:cache-parent.non-tail-connect-timeout>
    112     <network:http.first-byte-timeout>6s</network:http.first-byte-timeout>
    113     <network:http.read>2s</network:http.read>
    114     <akamai:choose>
    115       <!-- timeouts applicable on SRO1 region -->
    116       <match:tier name="CW-1">
    117         <network:http.first-byte-timeout>3s</network:http.first-byte-timeout>
    118         <network:http.read>2s</network:http.read>
    119       </match:tier>
    120       <!-- timeouts applicable on SRO2 region -->
    121       <match:tier name="CW-2">
    122         <network:http.connect-timeout>2s</network:http.connect-timeout>
    123         <network:http.first-byte-timeout>1s</network:http.first-byte-timeout>
    124         <network:http.read>2s</network:http.read>
    125       </match:tier>
    126     </akamai:choose>{code}
 

*Ask:*

While the value of <network:http.read> can be controlled in CW Multi-Config through UI:

after enabling: `Advanced Override (internal only)`

!image-2026-04-16-16-34-31-715.png|width=412,height=192!

and PS just increased the customer config value to 7s to mitigate the integration problem.

 

is it expected and is there any particular reason that the current default values of cascading timeouts for:
{code:java}
network:http.first-byte-timeout{code}
are higher than
{code:java}
network:http.read{code}
?

 

Typically best practice is to have network:http.read higher than network:http.first-byte-timeout and network:http.first-byte-timeout the lowest available.

Is it intentional to allow signaling third party CDN down-stream that we could have delivered incomplete for range request?

Or maybe is it pure gap that requires adjustment?
executive_summary:
Ticket was raised to understand setting the read timeout lower than first-byte timeout, 
Consulted the team that originally established these cascading timeout values. 
As per the following breakdown details, it is perfectly acceptable for the FB timeout to be set higher than the Read timeout. The configuration is intentional and does not represent an error. 
For more details, please refer to the attached email FW- Cascaded Timeouts for CW-MCDN.eml
root_cause: NA, Ticket was raised to understand setting the read timeout lower than first-byte timeout in ghostdir.hd.data/amos_best_practices_v1.xml, the configuration is intentional and does not represent an error.
creator: Maciej Maciaszek
mitigation_plan: NA
longterm_mitigation: NA

*Issue Summary:*

Starting April 18th during the WrestleMania Night One live event, Disney reported VPF and CIRR spikes seen on Disney and ESPN live channels.

[https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=start1776506040end1776579480]®ion=38977&network=A&maprule=&display=maprule&ymax=&utcoffset=-5

However, we continued to observe degraded performance metrics at the delivery with random spikes in timeouts towards MCH regions.

For ESPN as well, we validated a couple of instances and observed ERR_SSL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT and FIRST_BYTE_TIMEOUT errors towards multiple MCH regions. Top impacted regions were not the same for each instance.

We checked this with NOCC and as per them, machines are getting suspended due to high load and flits caused due to disney traffic itself.

We also performed client session analysis on the sample affected clients shared by the customer and we see HTTP 500 GZIP errors along with SSL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT errors with high Transfer/Turnaround time. 

[https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/11_u3PZHwGyMbvZwiaX-qhXgfnxxpzDXfIzD4O8svPYE/edit?gid=1303816929#gid=1303816929]

 

The issue recurred yesterday (19th April) during the WrestleMania event, affecting the NA-Central configuration. While the map rule for the top affected region (r41925) was suspended, performance degradation continued, with the issue being observed from other MCH regions in ORD. We also noticed impacted machined with high AVG manager cycle values.

*Etherpad:*

Disney 18 April: [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/Disney_Wrestlemania_VPF]

Disney 19 April: [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/Disney_Wrestlemania_VPF]  => Line 738 and [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/ms_04_19_26] 

ESPN 18 April: [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11226513]

*Log files:*

Qgrep logs, cache logs and region logs are collected and location is updated in the respective etherpad.

 

*Ask:* Could ERE please review these frequent spikes of timeouts observed across multiple MCH regions that are causing performance degradation and advise on any optimizations or recommendations to mitigate the issue?
root_cause: MCH region 46563 experienced persistent flit overload primarily driven by elevated flit consumption from Verizon's WAF policy change (pisces.cdn.fios.tv added April 9th), causing frequent region suspensions. The WrestleMania live event traffic surge (4× on April 18–19) exposed this contention, resulting in ERR_SSL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT and ERR_READ_TIMEOUT errors for Disney Streaming live channels.
creator: Rahul Haridwari
mitigation_plan: FOM was reduced on region 38977 and MCH/zch3 maprules were suspended on region 46563 to redistribute load. NOCC investigation ticket B-V-6HMM2ZR was raised for region 46563. Verizon IAT deployed a custom bot configuration to bypass InBrowser Impersonator rules to reduce WAF-induced flit overhead as an interim step.

Looks like we went forward through ICP, RDC and cached 4096-2097151 for the 1st POC fragment.

The 1st POC fragment with the default setting of fragment-size 2MB, should contain 0-2097151.

 

Attached debug log enabled with following sections to determine why ghost hasn't attempted to go forward and just drop the req instead:

GUIDE
POC
RDC
ICP
TCP
HTTP
CM
SOCKET
DISK
CLIENT

 

While the focus of the investigation should continue on why did we cache, broke the cache entry when there are two potentially racing forwards towards ICP/RDC:

0-3434883

4096-3434883

 

*Ask:*

Seems it's further continuation of N issues that shown up after POC perf improvements.

Can't we implement such optimizations as opt-in in the future or the features are as reliable as earlier post such improvements?

 

Advised to utilize following tag under advanced-override in order to ensure tunnelling ranges is disabled and follow a purge delete after activation:
{code:java}
<security:range-offset-from-active-download.status>off</security:range-offset-from-active-download.status>{code}
 

Should we utilize different workaround?

Is it known or new issue, what is the Ghost Jira #?
creator: Maciej Maciaszek

summary: Optimize maprule load on r50258
description:
Hello GPO

As per  NOCC#B-W-1DPTN1T spiky flit traffic was observed - "walmart seems to be culprit for 9680 MR traffc".  Is there a scope to optimize the load on this region without having to lower FOM in the long-term?

The region was maprule suspended under NOCC#B-W-1DQ5245 as a quick mitigation  but we want to smoothen the traffic load on this region before unsuspending the maprule again.

Original problem statement:

~~~

Property: mobile.ak.omni.bankmandiri.co.id_optimized

Customer reported that they observed elevated latency (~5s) in their traffic. Based on log analysis, the latency is attributed to ERR_SSL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT (SSLTimeout) occurring at midgress, predominantly within region 50258. Refer etherpad line (https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11227293 line 235-275)

Further validation via load graphs indicates that this region is consistently operating at or near capacity, with intermittent flit drops:

[https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=2d&region=50258&network=A&maprule=&display=total&ymax=&utcoffset=8]

Although the issue is intermittent, it is impacting our customer, PT Bank Mandiri, who closely monitors traffic performance 24x7 and is highly sensitive to any degradation. Similar impact from this region has been observed in previous cases (F-CS-11212894 / B-V-6H9CCRX).

As a mitigation, we have temporarily suspended the customer’s map rules from this region. This issue has also been escalated to NOCC under B-W-1DPTN1T, where initial findings suggest that traffic spikes from map rules (e.g., MR 9680, MR 1626) are contributing to the overload condition.

~~~
executive_summary: Optimize spiky traffic on r50258
root_cause: Region overload due to attack on Walmart (CP1450274)
creator: Rahul Sawarkar
mitigation_plan: NA
longterm_mitigation: NA

Note: 

#1 On original request the path doesn't have the NS CP.

#2 When going forward to NS there is forward path modification to add it.

#3 During ghost-reforward in this case out of ERR_POC_FWD_NOT_206 ghost utilizes the modified path.

#4 When going forward to NS again it modified the path again adding Netstorage CP: 275596.

 

*Ask:*

As per the title: MultiCDN-CloudWrapper generated delivery property is appending CP Code twice on forward path modifications during re-forwards, resulting failure on delivery (404) as the forward path is incorrect compared to what was originally requested.

Beside the product is counting on re-forwards for POC + NS4 instead of chasing the redirects.

 

Can we fix the template to avoid appending the CP Code twice on path modification?
executive_summary:
There is a metadata template for forward path modification, when a request is re-forwarded (e.g., after an error or redirect), the path already contains the CP Code from the previous modification. The template runs again and prepends the CP Code a second time, resulting in /275596/275596/...
We may need to add a match condition to check for the CP Code before modifying the path.
CW team is working on this.
root_cause: NA from ERE, There is a metadata template for forward path modification, when a request is re-forwarded (e.g., after an error or redirect), the path already contains the CP Code from the previous modification. CW team is looking into it
creator: Maciej Maciaszek
mitigation_plan: NA
longterm_mitigation: Follow-up on https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/ORS-16385

summary: Missing EHN IP on Staging Network
description:
Customer reported a DNS resolution issue which is specific to the {*}Staging network{*}. While the Production environment is resolving correctly to Edge IPs, the Staging environment stops at the CNAME and does not return any A records.
{code:java}
--- Production Query (Normal):
$ dig gcdn.ntruss.com.edgekey.net +short
e234294.e73s.akamaiedge.net.
23.44.173.10
23.44.173.18

--- Staging Query (Issue, NXDOMAIN):
$ dig gcdn.ntruss.com.edgekey-staging.net +short
e234294.e73s.akamaiedge-staging.net.
# No A records returned. 

gdimitri@sel-mp5rx ~ % dig e234294.e73s.akamaiedge-staging.net
; <<>> DiG 9.10.6 <<>> e234294.e73s.akamaiedge-staging.net
;; global options: +cmd
;; Got answer:
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: NXDOMAIN, id: 19491
;; flags: qr rd ra; QUERY: 1, ANSWER: 0, AUTHORITY: 1, ADDITIONAL: 2;; OPT PSEUDOSECTION:
; EDNS: version: 0, flags:; udp: 512
; CLIENT-SUBNET: 192.168.0.16/28/32
;; QUESTION SECTION:
;e234294.e73s.akamaiedge-staging.net. IN  A {code}
h3. Investigation to date
 * IAT Verification: Confirmed that the Edge Hostname is correctly mapped to all Property Managers (PMs) under the {{*.gcdn.ntruss.com}} domain.

 * Deployment Status:
 * Some PMs: Deployed on both Staging & Production.
 * Other PMs: Deployed on Production only.

Sample hostname:

1. Deployed to Staging Only

1-1. Hostname: cxkothjzjkra11160090.gcdn.ntruss.com
 - Deployment Date: Jun 8, 2022
 - Lookup Result: No A Record found

1-2. Hostname: studio-static.gcdn.ntruss.com
 - Deployment Date: Sep 8, 2025
 - Lookup Result: No A Record found

*2. Normal*
 - Hostname: jwwdmqvgwzvq18585989.gcdn.ntruss.com
 - Deployment Date: Oct 26, 2023
 - Lookup Result:
$ dig jwwdmqvgwzvq18585989.gcdn.ntruss.com +short
jwwdmqvgwzvq18585989.gcdn.ntruss.com.edgesuite.net.
a658.d.akamai.net.
23.46.155.141
23.46.155.145
$ dig jwwdmqvgwzvq18585989.gcdn.ntruss.com.edgesuite-staging.net +short
a658.d.akamai-staging.net.
23.50.62.16
23.50.62.11

*3. Abnormal*

3-1. Hostname: epxjhxilyvvv11190753.gcdn.ntruss.com
 - Deployment Date: Jun 13, 2022
 - Lookup Result:
$ dig epxjhxilyvvv11190753.gcdn.ntruss.com +short
gcdn.ntruss.com.edgekey.net.
e234294.e73s.akamaiedge.net.
121.254.136.82
121.254.136.146
{color:#ff0000}$ dig e234294.e73s.akamaiedge-staging.net +short{color}
{color:#ff0000}--> No A Record found{color}

3-2. Hostname: sbosirdwzbyw9257399.gcdn.ntruss.com
 - Deployment Date: Apr 8, 2026
 - Lookup Result: 
$ dig sbosirdwzbyw9257399.gcdn.ntruss.com +short
gcdn.ntruss.com.edgekey.net.
e234294.e73s.akamaiedge.net.
121.254.136.82
121.254.136.146
{color:#ff0000}$ dig e234294.e73s.akamaiedge-staging.net +short{color}
{color:#ff0000}--> No A Record found{color}

Can you please review if the "Production only" PMs are causing resolution conflicts on Staging or if there are any mapping or propagation issues on the Staging platform for the edge hostname?
executive_summary:
I see it is not resolving direct to the SNI map, I tried without directly using e73s and it worked fine. 
The container map e73 should suffice. It will auto assign the SNI map, we not need to specify it directly,
Mapping picks up the correct map based on the slot type https://mapper.akamai.com/ip/#from%3D1776752257095%26to%3D1776838657095%26treeType%3DA%26mrule%3D11531%26viperSlot%3D234294%26cidr%3D23.8.0.14%26resolver%3Dip%26useGMTTime%3Dtrue%26
root_cause: The container map e73 should suffice. It will auto assign the SNI map, we not need to specify it directly.
creator: Dimitris Kim
mitigation_plan: use e73 not the custom e73s
longterm_mitigation: use e73 not the custom e73s

summary: Investigation of increased midgress traffic since April 2026
description:
*Issue Summary:*

Since April 2026, there has been a significant increase in midgress traffic (bytes) for CP 479242 (apps.samsungcloudcdn.com), despite no changes to the configuration and stable ingress request volume. Initial analysis suggests that .img files are not being cached correctly, leading to an influx of 200 OK responses from Parent to Child edges instead of expected 206 (Partial Content) or 304 (Not Modified) responses.

*Findings:*

1. March 2026: Higher volume of 206 and 304 responses from Parent edges.

2. April 2026: Significant increase in 200 OK responses from Parent edges, even when the Origin returns a 304.
{code:java}
# Midgress Response & Object status (in r / S line)

1) 03/30
[dbae@lsg-gss6 ~/case/F-CS-11192025]$ zcat mid-rS-0330.gz | awk '{print $14,$16}' | sortc | head
1217056 206 isoSPXMV
1190173 200 isoSPXeMV
1171836 304 msoSPXMV
1053101 304 isoSPXMV
965963 206 msoSPXMV
823037 200 msoSPXeMV
110113 200 dsoSPXeMV
  80446 304 isoSPV
  71661 206 isoSPV
  59357 200 isoSPeV
 
2) 04/09
[dbae@lsg-gss6 ~/case/F-CS-11192025]$ zcat mid-rS-0409.gz | awk '{print $14,$16}' | sortc | head
2066210 200 isoSPXeMV
1859150 200 msoSPXeMV
212429 304 msoSPXMV
183624 206 msoSPXMV
132225 200 dsoSPXeMV
130184 200 isoSPeV
  98160 200 msoSPeV
  90535 206 isoSPXMV
  61421 200 dsoSPeV
  60456 304 isoSPXMV
{code}
3. POC2 Setting: <min-object-size>100MB</min-object-size> is configured thus observed: High volume of ERR_POC_FWD_OBJ_TOO_SMALL.

4. The .img files being requested are smaller than the 100MB threshold potentially rendering these objects uncacheable or forcing inefficient revalidations.
{code:java}
<match:uri.ext case="off" result="true" value="exe bz2 dmg gz iso mov pkg tar tgz wmv wma zip webp jxr hdp wdp x86 mp4 wgt img tmg">

        <acs-etag-to-acs>on</acs-etag-to-acs>

        <save-inconsistent-fragments>off</save-inconsistent-fragments>
        <trust-etag>on</trust-etag>
    </cache:poc2>
    <comment:note value="End Feature largeFileOptimization"/>
</match:uri.ext> {code}
5. In below sample instance, the Parent receives a 304 from Origin but serves a full 200 OK to the child with object status e, leading to higher midgress byte transfer.

[https://collaborate.akamai.com/confluence/spaces/~pbyakod/pages/1336248400/POC2+Reduce+Round+Trips+Enhancements+Ramp+up+POC2-RRT]

summary: {F-CS-11237447} c2g mtls broken, suspecting ghost 22.5.0
description:
SI-00023683:  [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11237447]     Is this issue intermittent or always observed for the client cert flow Customer: Idaho Central Credit Union *Customer reports c2g MTLS authentication failing since 20th April.* {*}It seems ghost may not be requesting client certificate in handshake{*}, so then due to no client cert, the request is denied with ERR_ACCESS_DENIED|ptk_cc_not_present / r70 mTLS FAIL|NO_CLIENT_CERT No changes in customer's configs - we confirmed it worked fine before Apr 20.   h2. Setup details: hostname : cdn.propaydna.rp.iccu.com Property : cdn.propaydna.rp.iccu.com v3 The slot is 345827 mtls is enabled in slot as {*}optional{*}, and enforced in the property adv metadata for all CLIENT_REQ:                                                                                             on                                             16KB                         on                                                                             *When I test in Cracker, the tags on in property *are executed* - but still we don't see any client cert req. But, we have doubts if we're able to truly replicate the issue, see below.*     h2. AkaTec analysis: h3. +*In customer's organic traffic in DDC logs:*+ the error pattern points to install of release-essl-18851.0.1 / ghost 22.5.0 There is one highly active client in the traffic, {*}52.39.40.108{*}, requesting to {*}23.34.83.73{*}. *Until 23.34.83.73 was installed on Apr 20 logs were all mTLS OK, after 23.34.83.73 woke up from install it's 100% FAIL|NO_CLIENT_CERT* => etherpad from l. 615 . When the same client was requesting intermittently to 23.34.83.73 and 23.34.83.78 (older ghost version), only. 73 has the errors, .78 was fine There's little other ghosts with the install and customer's traffic to compare. There was 23.53.242.131, but hard to tell if the errors are due to ghost, or legitimate: {{ msiemczo@lsg-gss12 {~}$  zstdcat mtls_recent_CP1764792-202604211500-23.33.25.197-819-177684819-1776848050.gz creator: Marta Siemczonek

summary: Increased SSL connection time during 21st April Match
description:
*Background:*

Increased SSL connection time during Match [T20 World Cup and IPL] days.

Context shared by customer:
for the host apix.hotstar.com , we are seeing increased ssl connection time for past couple of months, we compared our data from dec 2025 till april 2026, there was an increased SSL connection time during this time period , particularly during match days, t20 worldcup , IPL matches etc,
there has been 50% increase in connection acquired time which is the time taken for dnsstart->dnsEnd->connectionStart->secureConnectionStart->secureConnectionEnd->connectionEnd->connectionacquired.
the time was increasing till march 8 which was end of t20 world cup , went back to normal from march 9 till IPL start and again increased.

 

*CSE Findings:*

1) We are seeing high SSL overhead time logged in multiple Edge regions. 

2) Checking Edge region health for top 6 regions, I do not see any high load/suspensions/RHM losses in the region.

3) From sample traces, i could see high SSL time in r line. However, there was no slhds field seen in ddc log nor cache log for the client IPs considered. Further, cache logs and ghostmon logs showed average load on the server and no server suspension seen.

 

*Further Investigation:*

Both ssl overhead time increases seems to follow req endtime.

Do also follow the traffic pattern, indicating itis load induced (comparing with 10th of April trafficPeak with APIx dashboard confirms the same)

 

What is concerning though is ongoing ESSL release having problems and keysigning slowness surge specific to 21 April not seen earlier.

Therefore raising the ERE ahead of time as data is indicating an either capacity or release problem / both altogether.

 

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11239961]

15:45 UTC
16:45 UTC 

 

[https://grafana.akam.ai/org1528/dashboard/db/apix-country-level-traffic-new?from=1776737731502&to=1776822164180&var-end_user_country=IN&var-server_country=India]

21st April:

!image-2026-04-22-16-09-17-905.png!

Comparing with 10th April:

!image-2026-04-22-16-10-22-423.png!

*Ask:*

Assist on investigation and prevention.

Is it more of ESSL capacity problem or with - ESSL 18851-22.5.0  or together / other?

What is the reason behind keysigning surges, deviation seems specific to 21st?

 

Note: there is an risk of re-occurrence while the event is ongoing.
root_cause: Customer traffic increased since ~28th March and correlates with the increase in SSL times seen for the cpcode.
creator: Maciej Maciaszek
mitigation_plan: Use of multiple shards on ESSL setup for the hostname .
longterm_mitigation: customer to use API shards for spiky flit intensive traffic .

summary: {F-CS-11248225} OOC in sdweb.ib.mizuhobank.co.jp
description:
*Environment:*

Product: Dynamic Site Accelerator
Property Hostname:  sdweb.ib.mizuhobank.co.jp
Edge Hostname:  sdweb.ib.mizuhobank.co.jp.edgekey.net
CPcode: 1892866
Slot: 369364, Enhanced TLS (SNI)
Maprule ID: 1327

The same mapping related configuration property, web.ib.mizuhobank.co.jp (CP1893420), is also affected this time. 

This is the customer's internet banking service, which is highly sensitive to performance/connection issues.

*Issue:*
 * The customer started observing connection fail in c2g, performance degradation and lower Offloading rate from 2026-04-23 22:00 UTC. The most of requests in the service come from three client IPs, which are located in the same ISP (SOFTLAYER-R) in Tokyo.
 * Note that the most of traffic in the service comes from those three IPs which are the one for API GW of IBM. 
 * Checking the ghost.ddc.log in the affected time, 22:40- 22:50 UTC, most of the client’s traffic went to r43112 (CHT-TPEa) or r51627 (FABRIC-STO21z).     

*DNS Resolver* - ns019-mia5j8idr5vt2805.m.d-53.info  (202.32.219.50), looks static and unicast.

resolution_description:
Added the PP CIDRs currently in unpingable list. 
For now, included the manual PP IP which belong to a router of JP Ecor to let the PPs always available to map to JP regions.
Long term solution is getting IBM IPs to not filter Akamai ASN for ICMP pings.
root_cause: Pingpoint IPs part of IBM network filters out ICMP pings from Akamai servers, so JP regions seen consistent heavy loss leading to OOC regions to get placed in front of the scorelist.
creator: Yasuhiro Oyabu
mitigation_plan:
Added the PP CIDRs currently in unpingable list. 
For now, included the manual PP IP which belong to a router of JP Ecor to let the PPs always available to map to JP regions.
longterm_mitigation: Netservices to work with IBM to not filter Akamai ASN for ICMP pings.

summary: {F-CS-11266511} - Intermittent connection timeouts reported by customer for EIPB hostnames from Apirl 27th
description:
Account: Citigroup Global Markets Inc

Hostname: tts.apib2b.citi.com 

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11266511]

Customer is reporting intermittent connection timeouts for the tts.apib2b.citi.com since 27th April. 

From the DDC logs we identified the brave region 51027 . 

The region seems to be seeing increased traffic since April 27th around 14 UTC. 

[https://grafana.akam.ai/org172/dashboard/db/brave-kpi-monitoring?from=now-2d&to=now-10m] 

 

When looking at Total default destiantion, conncurrent connection , Kernel default destination per second in above grafana. 

Lot of alerts observed as well

[https://alerts.akamai.com/cgi-bin/reporter/alertreporter?__option_summary_collate_by=percent&report_type=instance_detail&__option_all_attributes=no&__option_over_time_groupby=hour&__option_over_time_groupby_definition=no&__option_over_time_use_graph=yes&__option_most_frequent_groupby=Alert+Instance+Key&__option_most_frequent_use_defs=no&__option_most_frequent_threshhold=50&__option_p1_thres=1&__option_p2_thres=20&__option_p3_p5_thres=50&__exact_Definition+ID=exact&__exact_Alert+Name=substring&__exact_Ecor+Number=exact&__pattern_Region+Number=51027&__exact_Region+Number=exact&__exact_Machine+IP=exact&__exact_Alert+Instance+Key=exact&__exact_Owner+Email=substring&__exact_Ticket+ID=exact&__exact_AMS+Alert+Definition+ID=exact&__dynamic_filter_=Install+Group&__exact_alert_data_value_1_=exact&__exact_alert_data_value_2_=exact&__exact_alert_data_value_3_=exact&__exact_alert_data_value_4_=exact&gmtoffset=19800&__time_options_=__use_start_&__month_From=4&__day_From=21&__year_From=2026&__hour_From=12&__min_From=46&__month_Until=4&__day_Until=28&__year_Until=2026&__hour_Until=12&__min_Until=46&__time_relation_Age=1&__time_unit_Age=Days&output_format=HTML&useNewWindow=on&timezone=gmt&datasource=Production&Action=Create%20Report] 

 

We see there is a release in progress on Brave 

[https://release.akamai.com/ui/networks/BRAVE/releases/18646] 

Is this related to the issue? 

What is causing the increased routing to FABRIC-FRA ? 

We have 2 customers reporting the issue in Europe.  (Worldpay: F-CS-11266235)

summary: Packet analysis review request between brave and edge server
description:
*Issue summary:* 

Netmarble has experienced recurring intermittent timeout errors across several API domains using EIPB. While initial escalations confirmed that platform-side issues—specifically regional flit overload and self-suspension in KR regions—caused TCP and TLS handshake delays, a residual stream of errors persists that cannot be fully explained by platform congestion alone. Please refer to previous jiras which engaged performance team.

ERE-3201
ERE-3226

A major concern has emerged regarding whether EIPB is the technically appropriate product for Netmarble’s specific traffic profile, which is characterized by aggressive application-side timeouts (2-3 seconds), HTTP/2 multiplexing, and heavy traffic concentration from kubernetes pods.

More over, current diagnostics indicate that a deeper investigation into the transport layer is required. Specific latency anomalies observed within the EIPB pcap data necessitate an investigation by the EIPB engineering team. This investigation is essential to determine if the delays are inherent to the EIPB mapping mechanism under this specific traffic shape or if an alternative solution (such as a different sharding or API accelerate product) would better serve the customer’s sensitivity to transport-level latency.

Checking qgrep logs it shows there is only one client IP and they were hammering one edge server mostly at that time.
{code:java}

summary: {F-CS-11223522} Riot Games: 400 from Origin + MPE causing cache poisoning
description:
Test URL: [https://lolstatic-a.akamaihd.net/rso-authenticator-ui/0.123.4/rso-authenticator-ui.js]

Product: DD

 

=> US West
URL: [https://lolstatic-a.akamaihd.net/rso-authenticator-ui/0.123.4/rso-authenticator-ui.js]
Config: [https://control.akamai.com/apps/property-manager/#/property-version/10329754/54/edit?gid=64631]

Issue:

Customer was informed of a bug in their bug-bounty program of a possible DoS attach
The way it works is that customer uses a program called Turbo Intruder which can simulate requests at many requests/sec, but the request header size is >24K.  Causing a 400 from Origin.

This program is hitting the Edges, simultaneously, they will simulate a real-user (normal size request headers) hitting the same machine and that person will get a 400 as well instead of a legit 200

From logs we can see samples where the real-user gets the 400 from parent-cache. Please check the logs in the etherpad

The configuration have advanced metadata to disable 400 caching. We can see the object status "k" in the requests but it still has S in it which means it is detecting the object as cacheable. 

<!--Disable caching for 4xx and 5xx-->
<match:response.status value="400:599">
<cache:bypass>on</cache:bypass>
</match:response.status>



Etherpad showing MPE working for path "assets/riot_desktop_background_2x.f1100272.jpg" (used to replicate): [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11223522#L1257]

 

Attached is a HAR capture with both the 200 showing the no-store and private heading and the 400 that's being cached by MPE.

 

This looks like cache poisoning vulnerability that impacts any property configs not explicitely disabling MPE as we would cache the 4XX/5XX that's caused by an end-user sending invalid requests and if this is done continously,  a DOS will be caused.

 

Based on the logs and config; could be related to the static files rule taking priority, for example:
 * *Match:* {{.jpg}} -> {{{}cache:max-age = 365d{}}}.

 * The file  was not in cache.

 *  S3 responds with a 400.

 * Because the object matches the 365-day rule and the config has 'negative-ttl  off'  the Edge assumes the origin response is valid content that should be stored for one year.

 
{code:java}
 <comment:note value="Start Feature cacheError"/>
            <cache:negative-ttl2>
                <status>off</status>
            </cache:negative-ttl2>
            <cache:preserve-stale-objects>on</cache:preserve-stale-objects>
            <cache:fwd-ims-flag-fix>off</cache:fwd-ims-flag-fix>
            <comment:note value="End Feature cacheError"/> {code}
Full investigation is here

https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11223522
executive_summary: 400 from Origin + MPE causing cache poisoning
creator: Cesar Roque Quiros

summary: BBC | Bitrate drop and performance impact due to Liberty API traffic
description:
_emphasized text_Customer reported drop of bitrates and performance degradation on 29th April between 17:48-17:50 UTC.
{code:java}
Customer : BBC public services
Property : vod-uhd-uk-live
CP Code 901559  {code}
h3. *Investigation*

Log analysis identified edge region r48819:FABRIC-LON3c  as the primary source of performance degradation. All machines in the region self-suspended following a spike traffic Liberty's API traffic NOCC ticket : B-W-1EOGP7J

!Screenshot 2026-05-01 at 9.54.59 AM.jpg|width=541,height=77!

!Screenshot 2026-05-01 at 9.57.26 AM.jpg|width=386,height=240!

DNSP graphs : [https://mapper.akamai.com/short/ztNcL6F8Yy]

*_Liberty is on object delivery product but its edge maps are already on APIX sharded maps and prespread serial._*
{code:java}
Liberty property : epg-prod-gb.gnp.cloud.virgintvgo.virginmedia.com 
Cpcode : 1285104

asivakum@lon-mprhi ~ % dig +short epg-prod-gb.gnp.cloud.virgintvgo.virginmedia.com
epg-pl.gnpwlb.cloud.upctv.pl.akadns.net.

Comparing APIX maps with BBC edge map (dscv), there is around 50 regions that are overlapping. Only VM regions were bit cap reduced from 98-90 percent in past mitigations.

Past ERE :[https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/ERE-2353]

Ask ERE,

Looking for a long term solution to avoid future impact as its been a reoccuring issues and past mitigations has not helped.

[https://collaborate.akamai.com/confluence/spaces/~nyasumi/pages/1024440089/BBC+SI-00022006+-+F-CS-8886511+-+Performance+Degradation+in+Virgin+Media+Regions]

1) Only VM regions were bit-capped , do we need to consider the same solution of FABRIC regions and other overlapping regions between APIX and dscv map. If so how can this be maintained if there are map allowlist changes?

2) Liberty is using Object delivery with API shards and pre-spread serial , is it optimal setup? Do you recommend they have to use APIX product for proper migration. Also any other solution to help better spread of the traffic.
root_cause: Liberty Set-top boxes EPG traffic
creator: Archanna Sivakumar
mitigation_plan: Maprule load reporting across shards
longterm_mitigation: STB EPG traffic should be randomized instead of being synced and instantaneous.

description:
Tiktok is using Websockets (WS) connections for their delivery product ION - webcast-frontier16-normal-alisg.tiktokv.com and cpcode: 1877422 and they are seeing persistent client aborts ~84%. Hence, we would need some guidance to understand what can cause this issue. 

+*Investigation details:*+
 * The hostname is map-shared and the slot is pre-spread. 
[usahani@lsg-gss3 ~]$ dig webcast-frontier16-normal-alisg.tiktokv.com +short

Cpcode stats: [https://grafana.akam.ai/org224/dashboard/db/tiktok-cpcode-dashboard?from=now-7d&to=now&var-cpcode=1877422&refresh=1m] 
Edge hps: 24.2K peak, Edge bps: 103Gbps, peak, Edge f2h: 1.8M peak, Edge f2bx1K: 699 peak, Edge Flits = 38Gbps at peak (Traffic is on ESSL
 * Websocket Stats:
> None of the ghost ip connections are > 64k, hence the pruning are not happening due to ghost limit being reached. 
>Max age for these websocket connections = 9.5days

*[https://www.nocc.akamai.com/miniurl/?id=29bf3]*
 * Based on the log analysis, we found out that most of the client aborts are from *Origin* and Robust stats for those machines around the time of aborts shows healthy utilization (flit levels are ~60%). *The ask is to confirm, if there are any other causers which can provoke client aborts or we can finalize this as being origin generated?*
 * Sample ghost and cache logs can be found over here:

summary: PS having issues using spread EIPB serial - Verizon
description:
Summary :

ERE-3305 was raised for Disney Streaming where the customer reported VPF and CIRR leading to performance impact during their live event. MCO reached out to ERE to seek recommendations regarding the possible mitigations and it was identified that the regions underwent spiky flits due to a particular hostname i.e *_pisces.cdn.fios.tv_*

The spiky nature from the above hostname lead to the oscillations in load assignment due to high flit demand and hence, ERE suggested to reach out to the Verizon IAT to make few changes that could help in mitigation of the underlying issues due to the hostname : *_pisces.cdn.fios.tv_*

 

Below were the recommendations provided from ERE

_{{From a platform perspective two things could be tried}}_
 # {{Try the full prespread serial - 2047 for the edge serial - have PS help migrate the edge serial from 1955 to 2047 serial could help  ( [https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/ERE-2802?focusedId=11061563&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels%3Acomment-tabpanel#comment-11061563] we have done so in the past on EIPB it seems, likely worth the try here too)}}{{ }}

 # {{One other thing that could be entertained from a platform perspective, I have noticed that these requests seem to be synchronized every minute causing spikes in load and throwing off how our platform reacts (dnsp inflate and deflate) , would highly encourage a desync of these requests with customer to lessen the hot spotting, especially since the f2h is much higher.  }}

{{!Screenshot 2026-05-05 at 1.38.07 AM.jpg|width=660,height=213!}}

 

PS tried to implement / change the serial number from EIPB interface and were unable to do so as they lacked the proper access to implement those changes. Hence, MCO tried to open a MPDEVOPS ticket as per the documentation : [https://collaborate.akamai.com/confluence/spaces/MPEFPS/pages/78654204/EdgeIP+Binding+Change+Management+Workflow] 

 

Ask : Since the Media Portal Devops ticket is obsolete, reaching out to _*ERE-Delivery-Portal*_ team to check and assist PS in their queries / implementation of the serial changes and any other information need by them.

 

More details can be found under ERE-3305 (https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/ERE-330 )
executive_summary: Verizon experienced performance degradation for Disney Streaming due to traffic spikes on the hostname pisces.cdn.fios.tv. To resolve this, the EIPB Edge serial was migrated from 1955 to 2047, which stabilized load assignment and optimized traffic distribution.
resolution_description: The ERE-Delivery-Portal team manually updated the backend configuration for CP code 1817883 to utilize the full pre-spread serial 2047. This change ensures traffic is redistributed effectively across the GHOST network to prevent regional hot-spotting.
root_cause: Technically, synchronized minute-by-minute traffic spikes triggered aggressive platform load oscillations, specifically dnsp inflate and deflate cycles. Procedurally, Professional Services lacked the EIP_BINDING_ADVANCE permissions required to implement the fix, and the legacy request workflow was obsolete.
creator: Pratik Salunke
mitigation_plan: The team successfully implemented the serial 2047 update and advised the customer to desynchronize their request intervals to reduce sudden flit demand spikes.
longterm_mitigation: Future efforts will focus on evaluating "Serial Update" access for Professional Services to reduce manual intervention and updating internal documentation to replace obsolete Media Portal DevOps workflows with current processes.

summary: F-CS-11300443 - Please help to increase EIPB hostname limit to 40
description:
Customer Name: Verizon Wireless

Please help to increase the EIPB hostname limit to 40 on the contract- V-5BKDUXX
Verizon Wireless has a $13M annual ELA that includes the Edge IP Binding product under contract V-5BKDUXX. The customer has currently reached the limit of 20 configurations on this contract.
They are now planning to onboard a new end customer and require an increase in the existing configuration limit.
Product Manager - Alex Balford has approved to increase the limit for this customer, see attached email for the approval.

Please do let me know if any additional details are required.

 

!EIPB_Approval.jpeg!
executive_summary: To unblock a new customer onboarding for Verizon Wireless, the Edge IP Binding (EIPB) hostname limit was manually increased from 20 to 40 following Product Manager approval.
resolution_description: To resolve this immediately, the limit was manually increased to 40 following Product Manager approval, successfully unblocking the client’s operations.
root_cause: Verizon Wireless reached their contract limit of 20 Edge IP Binding (EIPB)
creator: Ted Frost
mitigation_plan: Manual changes has been done from application properties side by assigning new variable.
longterm_mitigation: For long-term prevention, the team should evaluate increasing the baseline hostname limits for high-value ELA contracts and explore automating the approval workflow to reduce manual "toil" and expedite future capacity requests.

gd=//3@304

 

3==The request was served from a ghost in premium GeoX  with a Premium Opt-Inside GeoX policy active (in-geo).
Policy 304
SKorea-GeoLocation (304)

 

[https://techdocs.akamai.com/delivery-policy-mgr/docs/welcome]

I wasn't able to find any commitment on 100% in country as well as any word on 100% OOC for KR when outside of KR.

 

FAQ still unclear but kind of highlighting the fact we "can" map outside of the selected GEO especially for premium like KR:
{code:java}
"Karin: What is the SLA? how accurate are these policies? For example for South Korea can we guaranty that we will stay in country?
For strict opt-in/out policies, the SLA is that the mapping system will not direct requests outside the configured policy boundary.  It does not guarantee that a client won’t make a direct connection to a ghost outside the policy boundary.  For premium capacity policies, enabling the policy allows requests to be directed to high cost capacity in addition to the global capacity."{code}
 

Tried to debug the chain / other specific but started to run into permission induced problems and lack of perforce access:

 

dd (ipv4)

s-10039112.mr-290.maprulechain.debug. 0 CH TXT  "A:geo=0;chain=290->290"


comparing with dual for dsa
s-10039112.mr-4326.maprulechain.debug. 0 CH TXT "A:geo=1;chain=4326->332->332" "AAAA:geo=1;chain=4326->4322->4322"


comparing with dual of dd (4329)
s-10039112.mr-4329.maprulechain.debug. 0 CH TXT "A:geo=0;chain=4329->290->290" "AAAA:geo=0;chain=4329->4323->4323"

comparing with in country NS
unable to connect to any in KR country NS, getting DNS refused/servfail/nxdomain.

 

*Ask:*

is it expected non Korean User, example US/EU will always get non Korean Edge for KR Specific GEO Policy?
executive_summary:
Yes it is an expected behavior, in the best possible way Mapper tries to return the regions in Korea, in some cases where the end user is sending requests from non-Korea regions, then it depends. As per the scope of Geo Delivery, our aim is to not deny any requests, even if the requests with strict opt-in policy are served from out of geo regions.
Since there is a complict between the document and the product actual behaviour, the team is working to update tech docs so that its clearer to customers what the KR premium policy is designed to do.
root_cause: NA, it is an expected behavior,
creator: Maciej Maciaszek
mitigation_plan: NA
longterm_mitigation: NA

summary: {F-CS-11306079} - ERW regions flit overloaded
description:
NBA is reporting several instances high rates of connection timeouts towards their *.nba.com domains, particularly on ERW regions.

The Akamai IPs they report belong to flit-overloaded regions each time.

summary: {F-CS-11265790} Duplicate nonce value in CSP header
description:
Account: Cathay Pacific (15R-Q7YD)
Property: www-t0.ete.cathaycargo.com/en-us/home.html

Etherpad: https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11265790

URL: https://www-t0.ete.cathaycargo.com/en-us/home.html

Snippet of response below: can see duplicate nonce value ('nonce-201e121fbd999f1d86c29d2e5a176950' 'nonce-201e121fbd999f1d86c29d2e5a176950') in CSP header

Command to reproduce:
** it is required to add x-ws-request-id: test header to bypass ACL.
{noformat}
$ curl 'https://www-t0.ete.cathaycargo.com/en-us/home.html' \
-H 'accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/avif,image/webp,image/apng,*/*;q=0.8,application/signed-exchange;v=b3;q=0.7' \

header.log for "forward response" did not have any nonce value, looks like the duplicate nonce has replaced 'self' after default-src and script-src:
{noformat}
Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' blob: 'nonce-aem' 'unsafe-eval' https://analytics.cathaypacific.com https://zn3pkepy1qwtlejyq-cathaypacific.siteintercept.qualtrics.com https://tags.cathaycargo.com https://tags.tiqcdn.com https://siteintercept.qualtrics.com https://www.googletagmanager.com https://js.adsrvr.org https://ad.doubleclick.net https://connect.facebook.net https://api.mapbox.com https://cgocms-pat.ete.cathaypacific.com https://*.jsdelivr.net https://assets.cathaypacific.com; style-src 'self' api.mapbox.com 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https://metrics.cathaycargo.com https://cm.everesttech.net https://ade.googlesyndication.com https://www.facebook.com https://*.qualtrics.com; connect-src 'self' https://assets.cathaypacific.com https://siteintercept.qualtrics.com https://insight.adsrvr.org https://www.google.com https://ad.doubleclick.net https://api.mapbox.com https://events.mapbox.com https://insight.adsrvr.org https://dpm.demdex.net https://metrics.cathaycargo.com https://www.facebook.com https://collector-px1wbvgfnl.px-cloud.net https://b.px-cdn.net https://*.mapbox.com https://*.akamaihd.net https://tzm.px-cloud.net https://collector-px1wbvgfnl.px-cdn.net https://t0.api.osc1.ct1.cathaypacific.com; font-src 'self' data:; frame-src 'self' https://insight.adsrvr.org https://13315781.fls.doubleclick.net https://match.adsrvr.org https://asiamiles.demdex.net https://*.adsrvr.org https://cgocms-pat.ete.cathaypacific.com https://cathaypacific.eu.qualtrics.com; frame-ancestors 'self';
{noformat}

Full request & response + header info had been captured in etherpad starting line 83. 
https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11265790


CSE, [~jchuah], worked with the SA to compare the CSP header from origin and when it goes through Akamai servers:
{quote}
the part of the config that we see earlier should be generating the 16 bytes random hex and that will be replaced into "nonce-aem", from latest test, this looks to be working fine [highlighted in yellow]
{quote}
 !image-2026-05-08-15-50-14-389.png|thumbnail! 
The top one is the CSP header sent from origin, and bottom is the one that goes through Akamai

In this sample, the "ESI - Dynamic nonce" is only configured to generate the nonce and replace with the "nonce-aem" sent from origin. This part seems to be working correctly and no duplicate found.

SA feedback the following:
{quote}
changing variable MPULSE_CSP_ADD_NONCE to false (which should prevent mpulse from inserting nonce) -> did not work, same duplicate is still happening [www-t0.ete.cathaycargo.com v317 on Staging, can be tested via Cracker using same reproduction steps stated in ER]
mpulse is also used for Adaptive Acceleration in this config hence unable to fully disable mpulse
at this point, it seems we are still unsure how that nonce is being inserted, and why the duplicates..
{quote}

To prevent running into corner case, SA also tested by adding the PEER_REQ tag and it didn't change anything as well
 !image-2026-05-08-15-53-03-289.png|thumbnail! 
this is in www-t0.ete.cathaycargo.com v314
test result for v314 in etherpad: line 240-383

Ask:
Could you help us to identify what is causing the duplicate nonce value?



CC: [~bliu], [~jchuah]
creator: Alan Boon

summary: {F-CS-11311709} Cont. ERE-3147 - Interim solutions are still required
description:
+*Environment:*+

Product: Dynamic Site Accelerator

Property Hostname:  gw.aeonpayment.com

Edge Hostname:  gw.aeonpayment.com.edgekey.net

Slot: 26017, Enhanced TLS (SNI)

Maprule ID: 1327

CPcode: 826437

Client IP: 52.197.137.56, 52.197.158.90, 52.198.9.30, 52.198.79.125 (All are located in AS16509 of Amazon Data Services)
h2. +*Issue:*+

The customer’s payment transaction in POST from the clients above which is an API server aggregating the end users application request intermittently fails in C2G connection level. 

*The timeframe is:*

2026-05-02 6:33:37-6:34:54 UTC

2026-05-02 6:48:22-6:49:03 UTC

2026-05-03 6:46:03-6:47:09 UTC

After the discussion in ERE-3147 last January, the effort to implement solutions has been paid in IAT side, but it’s not easy to force the change of map to Japan Racing Association due to business/customer engagement reasons. Instead of that, IAT has proposed Cache/Downstream cache tuning to alleviate the spike of JRA’s traffic itself. The task is actually ongoing but it will take more months to complete. 

Log analysis and mapper tool links are in [https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11311709]. The root cause is same with ERE-3147. The pre-spread in MAPPINGENG-8122 is still effective, but FOM change in B-V-6A6ZYT1 has been reverted in the last February.    

The affected regions which the clients were assigned this time were r52909, r48512 and r36439.
h2. +*Ask:*+

AEON Financial Service Co.,Ltd is a critical service provider for shop payment transactions, and very sensitive to this type of error. IAT is looking for any mitigations by the time that  the cache tuning project completes (ETA: July 2026). 

Can you please consider FOM reduction as the temporary alleviation by the end of June 2026?
root_cause: Platform flit overload in Japan regions (r52909, r48512, r36439) caused by JRA horse racing event traffic spikes. The flit load spike triggers Ghost self-suspension, which directly causes C2G connection failures for AEON Financial Service payment API traffic on maprule 1327 (DSA, gw.aeonpayment.com). The FOM change (B-V-6A6ZYT1) that had been pre-spreading AEON traffic away from these regions was reverted in February 2026. This is a continuation of ERE-3147.
creator: Yasuhiro Oyabu
mitigation_plan: FOM reapplied under B-W-1G7SZ1D  until the JRA /Netdreamers account/customer makes the needed caching changes at their end.
longterm_mitigation: Better mechanism to track api like traffic on other product/ maps impacting regions/platform.

summary: The initial parent request is routed directly to the failover map
description:
*Issue summary:* Mediacorp is running the tests on the test environment under controlled network. These tests are performed on the TVs and other mobile devices. These android TVs are the predominant TV models that are used by the endusers in Singapore. Mediacorp expect these streams to be stable, before doing the next production staging tests. During the test they got some errors like
ERR_LIFETIME_EXP|clientTimeout, ERR_CLIENT_ABORT|stream:cancel and would like to troubleshoot why this happens and how to prevent this from happening.
194.223.128.154 is customer's test IP and 2600:3c15::2000:8ff:fed0:e3e1 is Akamai internal test IP. Mostly those two IPs are used during the test.
{code:java}
[gdimitri@lsg-gss12 /home/ssaravan]$ zcat CP1995693-202605080410-92.122.120.135-509-1778239140.gz|grep "mwlsmpn01stgpp.akamaized.net"|awk '{if ($2=="r" && !($11~/^(10\.|127\.0\.0\.1)/)  && !($16 ~ /[ftGKPX]/)) print $11 ; if ($2=="S" && !($11~/^(10\.|127\.0\.0\.1)/) && !($18 ~ /[ftGKPX]/) ) print $11}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr |head -20
   3965 2600:3c15::2000:8ff:fed0:e3e1
   1824 194.223.128.154
    911 150.129.60.85
     17 49.200.100.126
      5 13.251.164.188[gdimitri@lsg-gss12 /home/ssaravan]$ zcat CP1995693-202605080410-92.122.120.135-509-1778239140.gz| awk '$11=="194.223.128.154" {print int($3/30)*30, $1, $11}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nk2 | time_convert | head
     39 2026/05/08-04:10:00+0000 23.59.80.70 194.223.128.154
     40 2026/05/08-04:10:00+0000 23.52.131.193 194.223.128.154
     40 2026/05/08-04:10:00+0000 23.59.80.60 194.223.128.154
     78 2026/05/08-04:10:00+0000 23.52.131.134 194.223.128.154
     39 2026/05/08-04:10:30+0000 23.59.80.60 194.223.128.154
     40 2026/05/08-04:10:30+0000 23.52.131.193 194.223.128.154
     40 2026/05/08-04:10:30+0000 23.59.80.70 194.223.128.154
     79 2026/05/08-04:10:30+0000 23.52.131.134 194.223.128.154
     39 2026/05/08-04:11:00+0000 23.52.131.193 194.223.128.154
     39 2026/05/08-04:11:00+0000 23.59.80.70 194.223.128.154 {code}
This is for low latency streaming so the configuration(mwlsmpn01stgpp v.27) has shorter timeout settings configured. Recently the config has been updated to increase the client time to 1250ms for 1s segments & 2500ms for 2s segments but the issue still happens intermittently.

*One pattern* we noticed when there was slowness from an Edge server there was no forward request and then the next request went to failover map directly. 23.52.131.134 got request at 1778213609 with object status p (p - we contacted another ghost) but there is no f line. And then when there was another request at 1778213612.491 it went to chapacf map directly. QR triggered with the same FA map but client already got ERR_CLIENT_ABORT|stream:cancel as it has shorter timeout.
{code:java}

creator: Dimitris Kim

summary: ERR_NON_SUPERNET_IP from FF parent to Origin using OIPACL
description:
Hello

The customer reported error on traffic: [api.zomato.com|https://api.zomato.com/]

Based on our analysis user seeing error since our system routed traffic to out side of ACL Region (ERR_NON_SUPERNET_IP )

Mostly from region 39641 ( we see some requests on other regions as well )

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11321906] Line#323

Impact was significant with 14% of hits DoS'ed after 3x fwd retries.

ERR_NON_SUPERNET_IP                        459407   14.608% (line#237)

At this moment as workaround we suspended the region
[https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi?issue_id=B-W-1G4D46R]

Request flow was from FF edge 23.193.114.5 to FF parents 104.91.59.x which then decided to try direct origin to log ERR_NON_SUPERNET_IP. As per 2025 update 104.64.0.0/10 was removed from OIPACL:  [https://techdocs.akamai.com/origin-ip-acl/docs/update-your-origin-server] which also covers the 104.91.59.x IPs.

The /a/etc/ghost/maprules.data/allowlist.xml on a sample 104.91.59.x IP does not match on r39641 for any assign:maprule-member either. Yet these IPs tried to go direct Origin (no D - DR direct policy no SR races seen). 

OIPACL metadata is in advanced override and sample logs attached to this case for reference. 

i suspect that this map needs to be updated:
{code:java}
<match:region.attribute value="network-freeflow">
    <reporting:maprule>a2047.dscapi5.akamai.net</reporting:maprule>
    <assign:variable>
        <name>T0</name>
        <value>a2047.ch7.akamai.net</value>
    </assign:variable> {code}
Can you confirm?

(api.zomato.com v411)

[https://control.akamai.com/apps/property-manager/#/property-version/10325093/411/edit?gid=104815&view=review]

Can you help us understand why machines in r39461 as an example tried direct origin instead of a valid OIPACL region?
executive_summary: FF parent tried direct origin instead of OIPACL region and logged ERR_NON_SUPERNET_IP  with significant impact (14%)
creator: Rahul Sawarkar

summary: Disney Traffic Impact Due to MCH Timeouts and Flit Spikes
description:
*Account Name:* Disney Streaming Services

*CPcodes:*

1257566 (live-akc-na-central-1.media.dssott.com)

1759486 (live-espn-akc-na-west-2.media.dssott.com)

*Summary:* **
*Time        :* 00:24 UTC 05/10/2026
Disney reported VPF spikes in na-central-1 during WWE event seen around 9th May 20:27 ET (10 May - 00:27 UTC)

VPF Spike (ET Timezone):

!image-2026-05-11-17-07-52-975.png|width=593,height=118!

Upon investigation, we observed HTTP 504 errors at the delivery correlating with forward ERR_READ_TIMEOUT|before_resp_body errors along with 0xx ERR_SSL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT|SSLTimeout and ERR_FIRST_BYTE_TIMEOUT errors.

Machines looks to be sporadically overloaded due to below CPcode:
 
{code:java}
CP_CODE   NAME                       CUSTOMER
-------  ------ -----------------------------
1871677  APIX - api16-normal-useast8.tiktokv.us-US-Aggregation  TIKTOK USDS JOINT VENTURE LLC
{code}
 
 
[https://grafana-hats2-epremo.akamai.com/d/d398c61b-4cb5-4498-ada8-dc08c4962553/region-dnsp-serial-spikes-and-cpcode-traffic?orgId=1&var-service=W&var-dnsp_serial=-&var-region=48617&var-hats_serial=-&var-account_regex=.&var-cpcodes=-&var-top_n=10&var-flit_util_thres=90&from=1778321699195&to=1778440027143]
 
Upon checking traffic patterns for this CPcode, we see two noticeable jumps in traffic. 
One in the beginning of March and then again around April 15th.

!image-2026-05-11-17-44-08-541.png|width=597,height=224!

[https://grafana-hats2-epremo.akamai.com/d/d398c61b-4cb5-4498-ada8-dc08c4962553/region-dnsp-serial-spikes-and-cpcode-traffic?orgId=1&var-service=W&var-dnsp_serial=2047&var-region=-&var-hats_serial=-&var-account_regex=TIKTOK%20USDS%20JOINT%20VENTURE%20LLC&var-cpcodes=1871677&var-top_n=10&var-flit_util_thres=90&from=1771519070628&to=1778453284431]
 
We also checked forward requests from MCH towards CW and observed no issues.
 
*Etherpad:* 
[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11321389_ER] 
*[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11321389]*
*Ask:* Can ERE review the observed traffic patterns/ flit spikes and validate whether the TikTok traffic is contributing to these flit spikes/timeouts? Additionally, please suggest any recommendations or mitigation steps to help prevent recurrence.
root_cause: MCH region r48617 (FABRIC-ORD3k) experienced flit overload during the WWE live event (May 9-10, 2026). MCH regions are getting overwhelmed with the Tiktok midgress traffic,
creator: Rahul Haridwari
mitigation_plan: Apply FOM reduction on MCH region r48617.

Could see the change [https://gripe.akamai.com/rollout/subnet.W.akadpeactaka-enable-ohd-api] in phase 5

[https://git.source.akamai.com/projects/MDE/repos/hd.data/compare/diff?sourceBranch=refs%2Ftags%2Ftags%2Fhd.data-8.53.0-20260415.1&targetRepoId=12119&targetBranch=refs%2Ftags%2Ftags%2Fhd.data-8.46.10-20260324.1#metadata%2Fdelivery_api_best_practices_v1.xml]

The new values are :
<badip-retry-count>5</badip-retry-count>
<bad-ip-retry-interval>10s</bad-ip-retry-interval>

This is amplifying the error, how can we have this disabled per configuration wise?
executive_summary:
OHD was not enabled before in v152, but as per the log line if I am checking correctly, can see OHD is enabled so seems related, even the count i see is 5 times for ERR_NO_GOOD_FWD_IP.
After that there is an increase in 0xx error, so maybe OHD function marked it bad it seems. 

I had a word with Pramod ( owner on this rollout ), he will pause the rollout for now and see if there are additional issues. He will further check this internally, meanwhile suggested the mitigation tag to disable the OHD.
After further discussion and checks, OHD for DSA/API/ION was reverted completely at 05/13 17:46 UTC.
root_cause: OHD Rollout and OHD function marked origin as bad.
creator: Rohit Sharma
mitigation_plan: Suggested the mitigation tag to disable the OHD.
longterm_mitigation: Rollback the changes

summary: FIPS 140-3 in Ghost 22.5.1.1 requires EMS, impacting non-compliant customers
description:
Two customers - IBM MaaS and ADP have reported issues that have required  disabling of FIPS to prevent dos.  IBM MaaS saw this on the previous attempt at a release pushing the new OpenSSL 3.5.0 FIPS release. IBM disabled fips for now but wants to re-enble it and we are working with ADP on if that is possible now.

ADP: Slot: 189134,  [www.workmarket.com|http://www.workmarket.com/]

IBM Slot 4301, license.fiberlink.com



[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11337224]


We see in cache logs OpenSSL errors: "Provider routines::ems not enabled"

At a minimum we need to not world tomorrow until we understand this
creator: Billy Tiemann

summary: Increased TCP Connections to Origin Servers
description:
Issue summary: Starting at approximately 2026-05-09 14:00 UTC, Zomato has observed a sharp change in Akamai's edge-to-origin TCP/TLS connection behavior on our property [api.zomato.com|https://akamai.my.salesforce.com/servlet/servlet.ExternalRedirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fapi.zomato.com]. The origin AWS ALB scaled from 13 nodes to 100 nodes (hitting the AWS internal max) to absorb the additional handshake load.
{code:java}
Comparison

>> Before issue
Total origin reqs = 4,465,040
req == 0 count = 29,210
Rate:
(29210/4465040)×100≈0.654%

>> During issue
Total origin reqs = 6,372,597
req == 0 count = 56,148
Rate:
(56148/6372597)×100≈0.881%
Percentage increase in req == 0 rate
((0.881−0.654)/0.654)×100≈34.7% 
Percentage increase in number of reqs
((6372597−4465040)/4465040)×100≈42.7%  {code}
So far what we know is there were new parent servers(traffic change) hitting customer's Origin -> Customer's ALB scaler noticed increased tcp connections and added more origin nodes -> Akamai parent servers got mapped to those new nodes -> it increased more tcp connections.

!parent.png|width=582,height=93!

[http://grafana.opal.akamai.com/goto/nYvZzYADR?orgId=1] 

There wasn't major traffic shift around May 9th 14:00 UTC but there were some changes for parent servers.
