There is an advanced metadata in the config and customer suspects if this is causing more tcp connections.
{code:java}
<comment:note value="1. Force parent edge to go to the origin ip determined by child 2. Make this resolution as round robin."/>
<forward:cache-parent.sureroute2>
    <force-origin-ip-from-edge>on</force-origin-ip-from-edge>
    <round-robin.status>on</round-robin.status>
</forward:cache-parent.sureroute2>
<comment:note value="End Feature advanced"/> {code}
Customer has below questions and would like to know how to mitigate the issue. 

1. Did the parent-IP set for api.zomato.com (cpcodes 1575923, 1411589, 1436182, 1055672, 1403227) change around 5/9 14:00 UTC? We see visible parent IPs in 23.210.74.0/24 dropped from 36 to 22 across this window and have stayed in that range since. - our findings on this from logs on line 1169 - we see the drop in IPs 23.210.74.0/24 from 26 to 17.  What is causing the change in parent Ip set from within a CIDR and the change in parent CIDRs?

2. During the change, were existing TCP/TLS sessions to our origin severed at the parent edge (e.g., parent shutdown, process restart, or configuration reload that flushed pools)? That would explain the transient new-conn rate burst that first tripped our ALB autoscaler.

3. Mitigation - looking for your guidance. Based on our analysis, the cleanest unwinding path appears to be temporarily disabling <round-robin.status>on while keeping force-origin-ip-from-edge=on, which would concentrate traffic onto ~23 ALB nodes (since each parent would pin to 1 DNS-resolved IP per TTL). Our pre-incident baseline served this same traffic on 13 ALB nodes, so 23 should comfortably absorb the load and let the ALB scale back down over the next few hours. Would that work safely from your side, or do you see a less invasive Akamai-side lever we should consider instead?

 

Please review this and let us know if there is any workaround to mitigate the issue. It looks like the only change was those new parent servers when the issuse started to happen and nothing changed from Akamai config and customer's AWS ALB.

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11331153] has more information on the issue.
creator: Dimitris Kim

summary: End users getting timeouts before establishing a connection with EdgeServers via EIPB
description:
Domain: oboqbr-eipb-prod-be.khp.cloud.dmdsdp.com
Issue Time: 13 May 2026 around 10:30 CET (08:30 GMT)

Customer have STB clients connecting to AnyCast IPs: 104.94.220.194 (IPv4), 104.94.221.194 (IPv4) but their requests are not being forwarded to our Edge Servers before 5-8 seconds which is their limit.

Akamai EIPB Anycast IPs connected to:
2600:1480:1000:19d:: (IPv6)
2600:1480:2000:19d:: (IPv6)
104.94.220.194 (IPv4)
104.94.221.194 (IPv4)

Issue is experienced by Belgium users at random and there is no pattern. Latency of around 8 seconds is seen by the clients before they saw a 499 error on their end.

Since the issue is intermittent Traffic Reports doesn't show any skews
Collected the CP Code logs for the timeframe: 2026-05-13T08:30 to 2026-05-13T08:45 and checking for one of the given client IPs by the customer: 2a02:1810:cc1a:9a00:de73:6ff:fe15:877a. we found the EIP connection: 2a02:26f0:6d00:19::217:e284 --> Brave Region: r50750

arcd.aggregatedconns.log and arcd.log shows
[netlink] Netlink: removed connection in bad state 2a02:1810:cc1a:9a00:de73:6ff:fe15:877a

Upon running mtr traces and RHM graphs we can clearly see RHM loss at brave region. https://perf.akamai.com/cgi-bin/mahollan/rhm.cgi?region_input=50750&start_date=5%2F13%2F26+05%3A00&stop_date=5%2F13%2F26+21%3A00

Traces and logs under
Etherpad: https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11336988

Region is drained under https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi?issue_id=B-W-1GN01SR and customer is no longer seeing the issue

Need ERE's help in escalating to NOCC to find the root cause of RHM loss and fix.
executive_summary:
Customer observed failures for the property - "oboqbr-eipb-prod-be.khp.cloud.dmdsdp.com" starting 13 May 2026 ~10:30 CET, with STB requests are not reaching to Akamai - looks EIPB is terminating the requests. and the terminations correlate mainly to these Akamai EIPB Anycast IPs:

2600:1480:1000:19d:: (IPv6)
2600:1480:2000:19d:: (IPv6)
104.94.220.194 (IPv4)
104.94.221.194 (IPv4)
creator: Senthilkumar Rajendiran

summary: Increased transfer time of CMAPI(api1 map, mr15211)
description:
Customer Coupang Corp. reported that transfer time of CMAPI has been increased on Apr 26 and it has been increased one more on May 8.
This service’s transfer time is affected by high flits of edge regions.

!coupang01.jpeg|width=584,height=109!

For the instance on Apr 26,
When Akatec checked Hats, the traffic serving rate of r47270 has been increased since around Apr 25.
And CMAPI’s traffic has caused most of the traffic of r47270 regions since around Apr 25.

!coupang02.jpeg|width=579,height=93!

[https://www.nocc.akamai.com/miniurl/?id=29cc4] 

For the instance on May 8,
When Akatec checked traffic peak for CMAPI, r31010, r47270, r48921 affected transfer time.
These regions’ flit has reached flit soft cap, but CMAPI’s traffic has caused most of the traffic of these regions since around May 7. And the traffic serving rate of r31010 has been increased since May 7.

!coupang03.jpeg|width=565,height=92!

[https://www.nocc.akamai.com/miniurl/?id=29cc5] 

[https://www.nocc.akamai.com/miniurl/?id=29cc6] also shows increased high flits and F2B.

Akatec also observed a drop in traffic volume on region 51456 starting around May 7th. We are expecting restoring CMAPI traffic to its previous levels on this region could help optimize and further reduce latency.

!coupang04.png|width=569,height=90!

 

FYI. There were some works done for this account/TWC as overall flits for api1 has been being increase.

https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/ERE-3276 Traffic distribution on mercury.coupang.com

https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/ERE-3269 Reviewing API regions with flit heavy traffic

Please review this and let us know if there is any contributing factor for the issue. Customer threatened to de-akamaize if there is no improvement. 

[https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11270385] has more information
creator: Dimitris Kim

summary: Edge went fwd to incorrect parent regions  and got ERR_NON_SUPERNET_IP
description:
Hello

The customer reported error on traffic: api.zomato.com

Based on our analysis user seeing error since our system routed traffic to out side of ACL Region (ERR_NON_SUPERNET_IP ).

Both r48005 and r39641 are in edge map a2047.dscapi5.akamai.net yet Ghost(s) in child r48005 went forward to r39641 instead of any ch7/ch8 region like 39640.

Most errors were  from parent  region 39641 ( we see some requests on other regions as well )

https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11321906 Line#323

Impact was significant with 14% of hits DoS'ed after 3x fwd retries.

ERR_NON_SUPERNET_IP                        459407   14.608% (line#237)

At this moment as workaround we suspended the region
https://issues.deploy.akamai.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi?issue_id=B-W-1G4D46R

Sample request flow is from FF edge 23.193.114.5 to FF parents 104.91.59.x which then decided to try direct origin to log ERR_NON_SUPERNET_IP. As per 2025 update 104.64.0.0/10 was removed from OIPACL:  https://techdocs.akamai.com/origin-ip-acl/docs/update-your-origin-server which also covers the 104.91.59.x IPs.

The /a/etc/ghost/maprules.data/allowlist.xml on  sample IPs in edge r48005 ex: 23.193.114.5 and also the bad parent r39641 ex: 104.91.59.x IP does not match on r39641 for any assign:maprule-member either. Only r39640 exist for [ch7|ch8].akamai.net. 

We need help to understand why the child routed to a wrong parent region when allowlist looks good.
executive_summary:
Regions in edge map a2047.dscapi5.akamai.net like r48005 went forward to r39461 (instead of a valid OIPACL region like 39460 in a2047.ch7.akamai.net) which then tried direct origin as there is no other Tier configured -  and hence returned ERR_NON_SUPERNET_IP  with significant impact (14%)

OIPACL metadata is in advanced override and sample logs attached to this case for reference.
creator: Rahul Sawarkar

This happens mostly in */graphql* requests:
{noformat}
[jugaldeg@lsg-gss12 ~]$ cat bc_with_issue_choiseHotels | awk '$2=="r" && $71~/c=c/ {print $13}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | head
     70 /L/210160/1112232/0s/www.choicehotels.com/dxapi/graphql?m=CommitShoppingCart
     19 /L/210160/1112232/0s/www.choicehotels.com/dxapi/graphq{noformat}
... in POST requests:
{noformat}
[jugaldeg@lsg-gss12 ~]$ cat bc_with_issue_choiseHotels | awk '$2=="r" && $71~/c=c/ {print $12}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | head
     89 POST{noformat}
Sample log file:
{noformat}
[jugaldeg@lsg-gss12 ~]$ ls -lhr
total 21G
-rw-r--r-- 1 jugaldeg install 645K May 13 22:04 bc_with_issue_choiseHotels ===> Filtered by the ones with BC issues
-rw-r--r-- 1 jugaldeg install 607M May 13 21:55 CP1112232-202605062115-23.33.25.197-635-1778707533.gz ===> ALL Log lines{noformat}
The problem is that TrafficPeak, since it's not getting correctly the BC info, is presenting wrong or incomplete data, which is a big issue for this customer.

Based on [https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/ERE-3208] we understand that the flag ``add-reply-header`` needs to be turned on explicitly in the PM Configuration, but the problem is that this customer uses Breadcrumbs in many of their PM Configs, and other customers are complaining about the same issue. 

So we wanted to ask you fi you can:

#1 take a look at this request to determine if the issue is the same as reported previously

#2 check if there is a way this fix can be added to a gHost version instead of needing to apply a flag manually in every config with issues.

c.c [~efonaas] [~jurui]

r47484 - Reaching cap frequently, traffic seems to have increased recently
[https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=7d&region=47484&network=A&maprule=&display=total&ymax=&utcoffset=-6&diskcpu=on]

Mapper DNSP r47484 - Some sflags due to load
[https://mapper.akamai.com/short/LSrCLWUzFB]
 [https://deadpool.akamai.com/edgelogminer/modern-view?task_id=a8c058e3-41cb-4a1c-ae69-84e49a445b87]

We should probably FOM cap  r47484 , JP doesn't seem to be the only customer fighting over that region:

[https://grafana-hats1-epremo.akamai.com/d/d398c61b-4cb5-4498-ada8-dc08c4962553/region-dnsp-serial-spikes-and-cpcode-traffic?orgId=1&var-service=S&var-dnsp_serial=-&var-region=47484&var-hats_serial=-&var-account_regex=.&var-cpcodes=-&var-top_n=10&var-flit_util_thres=90&from=1778771585100&to=1778783478828]

===

Now here's the problem: The customer traffic is VERY concentrated. Clients that experienced the issue are AWS east and were going to an AWS east Origin too (as seen in sample slow request).

Most of their traffic hit the same Edge region:
{noformat}
====================================================================
### Top 10 Edge server IPs connected for client requests with count and status code
====================================================================

That Edge ecor had 2 sureroute parents assigned during the issue: 
1.1. r47484: FABRIC-IAD4r  -> Saw issues
2.1. r51667: FABRIC-PHLi  -> Had room to spare
[https://mapper.akamai.com/sr/assignments/#from%3D1778703720000%26to%3D1778790120000%26dataFrom%3D1778790117416%26useGMTTime%3Dtrue%26timeRange%3D%7B%22min%22%3A1778703720000%2C%22max%22%3A1778790120000%7D%26searchBy%3DmapId%26searchByValue%3D310920147%26searchCategory%3DwithEcor%26ecor%3D9211%26]

r51667 - Philly Parent had room to spare, whereas the other region was overloaded
[https://lg.netarch.akamai.com/cgi-bin/loadgraphs?period=7d&region=51667&network=A&maprule=&display=total&ymax=&utcoffset=-6&diskcpu=on]

 
We did tend to prefer going forward to Philly 23.217.201.x parents, but also sent a lot of traffic through r47484

 
{noformat}

  27379 23.220.106.139 23.48.203.135
  17595 23.220.106.138 23.48.203.137 {noformat}
===

Now my question is, what can we do to improve reliability & performance here?. Specially since they mentioned they plan on ramping up traffic & don't want to see perf issues when they do.
 - Pconns are being used
 - Their SS map is set to handle 1001 Mbps it doesn't seem like much of a bottleneck Since they funneled everything through the same Edge region we were limited to the selected SR parents at the time. 

Maybe increase SR max-parents to 3 to have more options from the edge?.

Not sure if considering prespreading should also help prevent issues on the edge regions further down the line (their traffic is mostly non-cacheable going by ACC dashboard)

Thanks.

Would like to understand why exactly we are unable to turn off MTLS selectively for specific path and why is MTLS G2O running for the net-storage origin and should it not be off by default for internal requests ?
creator: Suman Mishra

summary: 1/0 byte CL getting cached and served downstream for "ERR_READ_TIMEOUT|before_resp_body" with 200 OK to the origin.
description:
Blank page was served intermittently to the end users. Upon checking logs we can see 1/0 byte CL getting cached and served downstream while "ERR_READ_TIMEOUT|before_resp_body" with 200 OK seen to the origin.

The sample log in https://etherpad.akam.ai/p/F-CS-11337642 line 20 shows that cache was refreshed & served to client after completing 304 validation with origin, but content length is 1 and page is blank.  However, the working sample in line 10 shows the correct content-length is 9989. The issue has been resolved by purging the cache.

Wider log analysis shows corrupted cache was only served from certain edge servers, the other edge servers were able to serve correct cache. Tracing the corrupted cache, found the first instance (request ID 98bd1582) at 2026/05/08-05:17:36 GMT(Line 160). 

The log of 98bd1582 (line 180) shows parent server 23.200.140.77 returned 200 OK with 1 byte after contacting origin.

{code:java}
23.200.140.77 r 1778217456.377 0 1 114755 0 1 1 694 23.46.180.208 GET /L/124741/816182/0s/documentpacks.xero.com/ 200 ERR_SSL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT|SSLTimeout isoZSPeJV text/html documentpacks.xero.com - https://xero-prac-prod-document-packs-ui.xero.com/179fb61b-1af3-4a97-a5cd-d32a082d17fb Mozilla/5.0%20(Windows%20NT%2010.0;%20Win64;%20x64)%20AppleWebKit/537.36%20(KHTML,%20like%20Gecko)%20Chrome/147.0.0.0%20Safari/537.36 i TLSv1.3 3 - 1679 - - c6de7805 0 0 9e1ef8a.98bd1582 124741.s299 - 29235 - - - - - - 140 140 8978 802642639c0a3824 - - - o 0 r7f58f7c9bff0.-27303768.p.tl 350 sO - dbe0c880 - - - - 60.242.51.156 1692 - - - %20Cg%20__slot124741____networkESSL____levelL3____certCUS__ - - - - - cca=bbr;br=ba;mtse=tl|4267663528|9e1789e;aid=513550;pin=http|ion;ssl=3001302:::1d;netp=i;sip=1;aidv=31;fp=218;cpu=//ctx@waf/214/353\//prod@bmi/65\//prod@pim/14\//prod@sec/135;tff=8007;fsd=105269;vcd=7801 - - - 31835:69602:0:0 L3:L3:s::core_rsa2k_L3_client_50089: - mas=614:::64:121:273::94:34:392:100:;masc=::93:;cpu=//thd@phtm11/4130;f=0|1|DPF:4|DPF1:0|DPF_BP:I|DPF_ECMV:1|DPF_FRC:4|DPF_G2OP1:i|DPF_G2OP2:i|DPF_GTG2:HF|DPF_ORSESSL:i|DPF_PSE:I|DPF_ROBR:A|DPF_RUA:1|DPF_SHT:i;rct=n;rbuf=b;brl=0|0|0;swapsz=ok;fips=1;rawhs=1679;mdtmem=//ctx@waf/65\//prod@pim/65 - - 32acbc0 - - 114758522 1163 - - - 23.200.140.77|1|1|1|-1|0|11002|26|0 ua=%22Google%20Chrome%22%3bv%3d%22147%22,%20%22Not.A%2fBrand%22%3bv%3d%228%22,%20%22Chromium%22%3bv%3d%22147%22;ua-mobile=%3f0;ua-platform=%22Windows%22 -
{code}

Origin was stressful at the same time (Line 130) and returned some 200 OK with ERR_READ_TIMEOUT|before_resp_body and 0 content length(Line 165).


This issue matches KB000097028. Log from 23.200.140.77 with mtse=tl|4267663528|9e1789e based on the the request id 98bd1582 shows it synchronously refreshed cache in 23.46.180.208 under mtse 4294967295 (request ID 98ba62ed: line 191)

Under mtse 4294967295, 23.200.140.77 contacted origin and received 200 OK + ERR_READ_TIMEOUT before_resp_body + 0 content length with object status code L (Line 207) and we can see the same parent request ID 9e1789e.

{code:java}

{code}


Adding visibility to ERE to pursue this Ghost bug as I can see we have multiple customers and escalations already made under

https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/ERE-324
https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOST-4643
https://track.akamai.com/jira/browse/GHOST-6235

While I understand this issue manifests under different conditions, do we have a proper triaging and ETA on the fix?

*Subject:* Re: BBC - Buffering on ULL when using CTE


[@Kumar, Savan|mailto:savkumar@akamai.com] can you please open a ERE ticket with the details provided and help move this forward to understand how Ghost is handling chunk delivery? [@Lebouc, Patrick|mailto:plebouc@akamai.com] offered to collaborate on this to see if there is anything we have in terms of ghost tunings to enable in config based on what we find on how ghost handles it currently.
 
Thanks,
Dilip
 
 
*From:* Moghal, Mohammad <mmoghal@akamai.com>

*To:* Krishnappa, Dilip Kumar <dilipkuk@akamai.com>; Kumar, Savan <savkumar@akamai.com>; Dean, Jon <jdean@akamai.com>; Jagarlamudi, Midhun <mjagarla@akamai.com>
*Cc:* Shaik, Imtiazuddin <ishaik@akamai.com>; Lebouc, Patrick <plebouc@akamai.com>
*Subject:* RE: BBC - Buffering on ULL when using CTE


Hi Dilip,

 

Akatec won't entertain this, as they typically only handle break-fix issues and don't review new deployments. We have collected the suggested logs and now just need someone to review them. The advisory is to use the tag <forward-chunk-boundary-alignment> tag, but that hasn't yielded any results.

 

Since we don't have a direct escalation path to ERE, we are currently stuck. I would really appreciate it if you could review this and suggest the best way forward, as we owe the customer an update.

 

Regards,

Mo

Thanks,

*From:* "Kumar, Savan" <[+savkumar@akamai.com+|mailto:savkumar@akamai.com]>

*To:* "Krishnappa, Dilip Kumar" <[+dilipkuk@akamai.com+|mailto:dilipkuk@akamai.com]>, "Moghal, Mohammad" <[+mmoghal@akamai.com+|mailto:mmoghal@akamai.com]>, "Dean, Jon" <[+jdean@akamai.com+|mailto:jdean@akamai.com]>, "Jagarlamudi, Midhun" <[+mjagarla@akamai.com+|mailto:mjagarla@akamai.com]>
*Cc:* "Shaik, Imtiazuddin" <[+ishaik@akamai.com+|mailto:ishaik@akamai.com]>
*Subject:* Re: BBC - Buffering on ULL when using CTE

 

Hi,

 

You need to see *when data becomes available in the store* (sections 20, 133) and *how Ghost reads and delivers it to the client* (33, 75). The forward-side section 119 can also be added (not useful if the object is always served from cache) otherwise very useful as it logs {{ChunkedResponseInputStream}} at level 10 - logs every origin CTE chunk-size header, body bytes, trailer etc. Shows exactly what boundaries origin is sending.

 

So sections - 20 33 75 119 133

 

Note: this is going to produce a lot of log lines and might even lead to rolls. Please enable it at an arl-ID level and on specific 1 or 2 IP’s only. 

 

Regards

-Savan

*Cc:* Shaik, Imtiazuddin <[+ishaik@akamai.com+|mailto:ishaik@akamai.com]>
*Subject:* Re: BBC - Buffering on ULL when using CTE

 
[+@Kumar, Savan+|mailto:savkumar@akamai.com] can you please share any suggestions on which debug sections to enable to capture cache logs on chunked transfer delivery by ghost ?

 
Thanks,

Dilip

*Subject:* BBC - Buffering on ULL when using CTE

Hi Dilip,

 

BBC are currently experiencing an issue with low latency DASH/CMAF streaming. They are using chunked-transfer encoding, and the HTTP chunks are misaligned when delivering through Akamai leading to a latency of up to 500ms. Could it be related to how we do CTE? Do we adhere to the CTE boundaries set by the origin or we rely on our own mechanism for chunking? I’m attaching the workflow diagram sent by BBC which explains how it’s causing these delays. They say they have configured this on their end to ensure that each media chunk ends with a HTTP chunk boundary. Who do you think is the right person to review this? Any help on this will be appreciated.

 

FYI: BBC have also sent a log from their player showing MOOF not in the correct location on the response received from Akamai.

 

Regards,
Mo

summary: Request to apply pre-spreading from DNSP-Dev
description:
SBS IAT is preparing the upcoming FIFA world cup in June.

They would like to have ERE's review and help apply pre-spreading from DNSP-Dev over the hostname that has high concurrent server-to-server test as well as used for end user's live streaming.

Hostname: [sbs-live-prod-01.akamaized.net|https://akamai.my.salesforce.com/servlet/servlet.ExternalRedirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsbs-live-prod-01.akamaized.net]
Edge Hostname: [a119.w16.akamai.net|https://akamai.my.salesforce.com/servlet/servlet.ExternalRedirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fa119.w16.akamai.net]
Primary TD map: AMD Default
Backup TD map ([chausf.akamai.net|https://akamai.my.salesforce.com/servlet/servlet.ExternalRedirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchausf.akamai.net])
CW map: cw-s-au-live, cw-s-usw-live (DAI Traffic)

Workflow:

User Manifest flow: Client (AU) -> Edge/TD (AU) -> DAI Origin (AU)
Segment flow: Client (AU) -> Edge/TD (AU) -> CW (AU) -> Origin (AU)
Pristine SSAI Manifest Flow: DAI Client (US) -> Edge (US) -> CW (US) -> Origin (AU)

Traffic Projections:

Estimated concurrency - 1 Mil
Average bitrate - 4.5 Mbps
Peak throughput - 4.5 Tbps
creator: Dimitris Kim
